## AAQ: Are Devotees Made by Destiny or Choice? The Real Answer!" | His Grace Madhu Pandit Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

### karma, free will, soul
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/75xxM5pMnFs?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Now you have free will, how to respond to your subtle body, your gross body and your kala."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that while past karma influences our external circumstances, the soul possesses free will to respond to these influences.

If everything that happens to us is due to past karma, is becoming a devotee also predetermined by it? No, even if everything that happens to us, what happens to us, that means what the environment, what everything outside me does to me, what I am there outside me, I am the outside. Outside me means my body, my mind, and the world outside. Everything is outside me. I am not the body. I am sitting in the body. So, my mind, my intelligence, my ego, everything is outside me. My real ego is different. Soul's real ego is outside me, dormant. So now there is a subtle body and the gross body. Soul is there, then subtle body is there, then gross body is there and the world is there. So, the subtle body, the gross body and the world it will interact with you. But the soul will not. It will not interact with you. You are there in this world. You belong in this world according to your past karma, governed by Krishna's impersonal aspect called kala shakti. Kala shakti, according to your karma, is predestining you. You means the soul. Now you have free will, how to respond to your subtle body, your gross body and your kala.

### free will, destiny, choice
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/75xxM5pMnFs?t=160
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "So life is a combination of free will and destiny."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of encountering a dog to illustrate that even animals have free will within the constraints of their environment, suggesting life is a combination of free will and destiny.

So, you have free will. Now you have free will, how to respond to your subtle body, than the outside world. But how much you can change, how much you can, that depends on like two forces are there. If your force is big, then you will counter it. If that force is big, you will counter it. But we always have a choice, we always have a choice. What to speak of we having the choice? Today I was walking and opposite side one dog was coming. So I was first trying to give way to the dog this way. Then the dog went that way. Then I tried to give dog way this way. I went that side. The dog changed this way. Then again I changed. So as much as I was changing, even a dog has a free will. Even the dog is at that time is waiting for me whether I will go this way or this way. So I can see the dog is ready to finally go this way or that way. That means even a dog has free will. I can force, you don't go this way, I will block it. He will go that way. He will use his free will to go that way. If I go this way, that means according to the way the environment is impacting you, you have got a free will to go this way or that way.

### destiny, karma, free will
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/75xxM5pMnFs?t=260
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Potentially speaking, it is my free will alone that exists. My destiny is nothing but my past free will."
**Summary:** The speaker asserts that destiny is a result of past free will, not arbitrary divine decree, and uses the analogy of a cow tied to a tree to illustrate the limits and freedom within destiny.

So life is a combination of free will and destiny. Free will and destiny. If you say everything is distinct, that destiny, whatever destiny has come, it will come. Whatever is distinct, who created that? My own past free will. It is not that God has given, put on destiny for you differently, for him differently out of his whims and fancies. If somebody is a beggar today, he has created his destiny, not God. God has sanctioned that, because nothing will move without God, move without Krishna's sanction. Sanction is different, his wish is different. According to your wish, he sanctions. Wish and karma. I will give a simple example. If a cow is tied around a coconut tree, 100 feet rope. Within 100 feet, rope has any effect? No. Moment it goes beyond 100 feet, it will pull. Destiny is like, cow's destiny is 100 feet rope. Even though he cannot go beyond 100 feet, within 100 feet he can go anywhere. There is no destiny. Correct?

### free will, willing, modes of nature

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Free will is an illusion in this world. You think you are free. But there is a person who is willing. There is an atma who is willing."
**Summary:** The speaker clarifies that 'willing' is a more accurate term than 'free will,' as actions are influenced by the modes of nature, but the soul is the decision-maker.

Let us use the word willing. The willing shakti. Dog has got willing shakti. That willing shakti is influenced by the modes. But it is his willing shakti. He is not a machine. He is not a machine. He is influenced by that and therefore he is deciding. The soul is there, acting. He is not a robotic dog. If there is a robotic dog, you can say 100%. There is no willing there. Input, it output, it will decide. But there is an action. Experience inside that dog. In a robot dog, there is no experiencer. So the experiencer wills this way or that way. But that is why I said change the word free will. You are right. That free will, that means freely deciding is not there for anybody in the world. They are all influenced by three modes of nature. But we take a decision. Highly influenced. If you say free will, free will is an illusion in this world. You think you are free. But there is a person who is willing. There is an atma who is willing. Whether influenced or uninfluenced. Less influenced or more influenced. A decision maker is a soul.

## AAQ: How can lust be utilized in serving the Lord | HG Madhu Pandit Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

### lust, purification, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/y3m5VxHCwYQ?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When material lust, material anger, material all these things, when they are engaged in service of the Lord by a conditioned soul, it gets purified."
**Summary:** This segment discusses how lust can be engaged in the service of the Lord and purified, contrasting it with the actions of pure devotees.

So, in this material world, lust is considered a perverted reflection of love. In Bhakti Yoga, it is suggested that we can engage our lust, anger, and greed to serve the Lord. So, how is this lust serving the Lord in the spiritual world? If the order of the Lord is there, He will purify it. Just like Krishna says, "I am sex life according to regulative principles." So, even though, that means it is a compromise. When material lust, material anger, all these things, when they are engaged in the service of the Lord by a conditioned soul, it gets purified. That means you become free from that lust. You become free from that anger. So, this is different from pure devotees. An awakened person can also be angry. Hanuman was angry. That anger is not doubt-telling anger. That anger is coming out of his pure Bhakti. You understand, so that is different. A pure devotee's anger, everything is different. Of course, they are free from lust. Even Bhaktivinoda Thakur says that if I can bring forth hundreds of pure devotees, I won't mind engaging in sex. Divine forces Krishna has made that to trap the living entities, correct, but if you know the real purpose, you can use that to untrap also. The end use is important.

So life, what is called life symptoms in this material world, they are only patches of light. The Kshetrajna is reading the story out of it. You are given that capacity to read the story out of it. The director of the movie has, see, has, similarly the director of the movie, of this movie is the super-soul. Everything facilitates for you to read a nice movie out of it. Starting with I am the body. If you, little you understand, good enough, be happy. Ultimately it takes time. It takes time. Our consciousness has to purify. The seer itself will, will transform. Then the seeing will also, higher levels of seeing will happen. If you give me dog's ears, I can hear more frequencies. It is all everywhere. But I did not hear. Now I can. Similarly, spiritual, spirit is everywhere. Krishna is everywhere. It requires spiritual eyes. That is why Prabhupada said, people are, can you show me God. Prabhupada used to ask, do you have the eyes to see God? No, I will learn. Okay. You are real determined to see God, I will tell you how to prepare your eyes. Chant Hare Krishna. Follow four relative principles. Read Bhagavad Gita. Surrender to Krishna. You will get the eyes to see Krishna.

---

## AAQ: Is it wrong to seek one's glorification? | HG Madhu Pandit Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

### glorification, Krishna consciousness, devotional service, love of Godhead
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/nttc7zSQKH4?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Misdirected love of Godhead is called lust."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa explains that the desire for glorification is a natural instinct stemming from our connection to Krishna, but in the material world, it is perverted; true fulfillment comes from serving Krishna and taking pride in that choice, while avoiding wrong praise and misdirected love, which manifests as lust.

Another point, important point that Prabhupada is mentioning here is that it is a natural instinct for the Lord to hear and enjoy His personal glories. And because of this, each of us are an individual part and parcel of Krishna. We also have a tendency to feel happy when somebody glorifies us. But this is asat, in the material world, that is pervertedly reflected. In the spiritual world, the devotee also likes to be known in the eyes of the Lord, that He is happy with my service. And therefore when the Guru says, yes, you have done service, good service, then he also likes to hear that. Very good, you have distributed hundred Bhagavatam sets. Very good, Prabhupada says, you should enjoy that. Don't feel shy, it's your nature. Don't enjoy wrong praise. Don't seek wrong praise. Don't seek wrong glorification. Don't take credit for what you do not deserve. You deserve credit for choosing to serve Krishna than Maya. For your choice, you require, that is rewarded, you should feel proud about it and you should expand that pride. Pride, that I will never serve Maya, I will only serve Krishna. So Krishna's, we are amsa of Krishna. So it's the same tendency is there in us. We are amsa of Krishna, Krishna is sacchidananda, we are amsa, but our present. There is nothing wrong in chasing after happiness, ananda. But we are wrongly chasing, because without Purna, without Krishna connection, there is no question of getting any strength. If I cut my fingers from the body, it will lose all its strength. Our current situation is like that. Even the cut fingers, still hungry for oxygen, blood, everything, doesn't stop. Of course, it's a very crude example. If it is one jiva, one finger is one jiva, cut off from the main, the Lord, that main connection with the Lord is function of devotional service. Gets cut off, then that soul will still, because nature is that, where is ananda, where is jiva? I don't want to die. So all the tendencies, we being part and parcel of Krishna, Krishna's nature is there in us, but it has to be properly redirected. Prabhupada says misdirected love of Godhead is called lust. Just see, lust is defined as misdirected love of Godhead. Your nature is to love God, but you have forgotten Krishna, so it has turned into lust in this material world.

---

## AAQ: When Does Krishna Guide You to Your Guru? |His Grace Madhu Pandit Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

### Guru, Krishna's Guidance, Paramatma, Devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ykH9_HZueRk?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Paramatma brings you to Guru, and Guru takes you to Krishna."
**Summary:** This segment explains how Krishna, as Paramatma, guides individuals to a Guru based on their spiritual readiness and inclination, and how the Guru then leads them to Krishna, emphasizing the devotee's role in teaching devotion.

You see, who comes into contact, who doesn't come into contact, that is arranged by Krishna, who is seated in the heart of the devotee. Every soul is germinating in this material world. Krishna is in your heart. Does this devotee still not fed up of this world? Does he want to come to me or not? Is he questioning what is life? All that Krishna is monitoring in the heart, as Paramatma. So therefore, Paramatma, not destiny, Paramatma who knows your spiritual past, who knows your spiritual inclination, who knows how much you are fed up of this world, who knows whether this person is seeking for the truth beyond this illusion, whether this Atma is seeking for purpose of life, all that somebody is monitoring. Who is that? Paramatma. Paramatma takes you to Prabhupada. What is the use of Paramatma bringing you to Prabhupada? Somebody who is not ready to give up his material life and who is completely absorbed in illusion. When Prabhupada went to America, even though they were all very materialistic, these foreigners, they all have searched for truth. What is life? They all had this question. So right there, immediately Prabhupada planted the answers. So it is not that Prabhupada went to America and he touched everybody and only few become devotee. No. He didn't touch many. Millions of Americans are there. They don't even know who Prabhupada is, right? So Krishna arranged some souls who are ready. It is said that Krishna brings one to Guru and Guru takes you to Krishna. Paramatma brings you to Guru and Guru takes you and trains you and makes you realize the Supreme Lord Krishna. That Krishna's one aspect is Paramatma. Vande krishnam jagat gurum vise. Who is that? It is the Supersoul. So Supersoul brings you to Guru, Guru brings you to Krishna. Why? Because you have to learn how to become a devotee. Somebody who is a devotee is the best person to teach you how to become a devotee, not God. God is not devotee. God is receiver of devotee service. So God leaves that duty to devotee. Okay, you make them devotee, but I will guide them to come to you.

---

You said that the perception of credits, one must have the right credits and take only what belongs to him. Perception of? The credit, how we see credit and how we take credit. So you said our transcendental credit is to Guru and Krishna. So how does a devotee see credits? Because we see that in our organization there are big devotees who have achieved a lot like Akshaya Patra for example. So how do devotees handle credits without seeing Puja, Laba or without accepting as Pratishtha or it doesn't become an Anartha. So how should a devotee handle the credits that he gets?

**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** A devotee should understand that the most important recognition is the pleasure of Srila Prabhupada, the Guru Parampara, and Krishna.

You see when we are in this material world, these things we have to face. It comes across us. For instance we deal with money, we deal with positions, we deal with awards, we deal with you know. Just like everybody in the material world, between this award, that award, they will also get awards. As far as devotee is concerned, first thing he understands that the only recognition that I value is if Srila Prabhupada, Guru Parampara and Krishna, they are pleased. If they are pleased.

### pleasing Krishna, devotional service, instruction
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/7y2AIc-Q_4Y?t=85
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Lord is pleased by trying to follow His instruction."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of trying to please the Lord and His devotees by following instructions, even if done imperfectly.

That is what I always seek. Even if I don't know whether they are pleased or not, I am constantly seeking that. And I have faith because they have told that if you do this, I am pleased. So whether I am actually doing it properly, it's a different matter. But I am trying to do it properly. Let's say I chant Hare Krishna. If you chant Hare Krishna, Lord is pleased. So I am trying to do it properly. Let's say I chant Hare Krishna. Lord is pleased. But whether I am chanting offenselessly, that's another question. But the fact is that I am trying to chant offenselessly, yes, the Lord is pleased because Lord is pleased by trying to follow His instruction. So to please the Lord, to please the pure devotees of the Lord, to please the Vaiṣṇavas who are representing or representing the desires and wishes of a pure devotee. Yes.

### detachment, service, utility
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/7y2AIc-Q_4Y?t=145
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If at all in my service the Lord has given me some credit, it is due to the mercy of the Guru and Krishna, to be invested back in the service of the Lord."
**Summary:** Worldly rewards should be ignored or reinvested in the service of the Lord, recognizing that any credit received is due to the mercy of the Guru and Krishna.

This is what he is seeing in reciprocation, I want to see this, not other rewards. So as far as all the other rewards are concerned, worldly rewards are concerned, or what the karmas come and give us, those things one should just, how can even, you know, all these things, you should just ignore them. You cannot even offer it to somebody, because if you want to offer something to somebody, that person should also like that, right? So it's the spirit of the devotee to offer everything to Guru, but whether the things that are offered by the people, by the karmis, whether they are offerable or not, it's a difference. It's a different matter. But the spirit is, the devotee is saying that if at all in my service the Lord has given me some credit, it is due to the mercy of the Guru and Krishna, to be invested back in the service of the Lord.

### service, reinvestment, material awards
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/7y2AIc-Q_4Y?t=215
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** A devotee's perception of money, fame, and recognition should be focused on how to reinvest these resources back into the Lord's service.

That is the perception of the devotee. The perception of the devotee who gets lot of money is not that I got lot of money, yes. Now his perception is how I can invest back in Lord's service. Here is something, some... Fame, recognition has come, his goal is now how to encash on that and reinvest it back in service of the Lord. Totally utility point of view, what you get. It is nothing transcendental about that, the material awards and that you get here in this material world.

### appreciation, instrument, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/7y2AIc-Q_4Y?t=247
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "But you give credit, you glorify a devotee for having been an instrument in the hands of the Lord for whatever that you are doing."
**Summary:** Within the devotee community, appreciation should be given to others for being instruments of the Lord, regardless of their level of purity, as the Lord can use anyone for His service.

Now as far as between, within the community of devotees, the devotee's duty is to appreciate the other devotee's service to the Lord, service to Krishna. But when he is appreciating that service, he is not appreciating the Lord. He is not appreciating the service to Krishna, he is not appreciating his ability. Please understand, devotees have crossed all that platform. Because Krishna, he knows, I am the ability in man, Krishna has said. The Lord is doing it. But you give credit, you glorify a devotee for having been an instrument in the hands of the Lord for whatever that you are doing, whether that instrument has come because you are pure or not pure, it is a different matter. But you are an instrument. A neophyte can also do a big service. The Lord may encourage him to do a very big service, but doesn't mean that it is pure devotional service. So just see how these are also various things, material measurements of how big service, small service is all, you cannot, can be very misleading. Because ultimately Lord has got unlimited reasons why a neophyte is made to do big service sometimes. To keep him in devotion service, to feed his anartha so that it burns away in a regulated way, in devotion service to burn it, he may do it, it is left to the Lord.

---
### empowerment, Krishna consciousness, Maya

**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** Consistent service comes from Shakti Avesh Avatara, and the Lord empowers devotees to do big things for His mission, but it's crucial to distinguish between Krishna consciousness and Maya consciousness in these endeavors.

And consistently if somebody has to serve the Lord, and that is a different thing, that is Shakti Avesh Avatara, like Prabhupada. The Lord Shakti is in him. He is in him. He is in him. He is in him. He is in him. He is in him. Where he does big, big, big, big things. Not big because it is big. It is big because there is a big mission of the Lord here in this world. The Lord is waiting to empower the servants of the Lord with his power to do big things. So there is a Krishna conscious perspective to doing big things in this world and by the, among the devotees, but there is also a Maya consciousness of doing big things among the devotees. So everywhere the consciousness is important.

### consciousness, anartha, process

**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers

**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of a new devotee wanting to build a skyscraper in the Indian Ocean to illustrate the difference between genuine Krishna consciousness and acting on one's own desires (anarthas).

We need to understand with the right spirit. Not doing all, doing all big, big things for Krishna is Krishna consciousness. No. It can be Krishna conscious or it can be in your head, it could be completely Maya also. I always give this example, a new devotee joins, he says, yes, you should think of doing big, big things for the Lord. You cannot stop me. I want to build a skyscraper in the middle of the Indian Ocean. I want to build a skyscraper in the middle of the Indian Ocean. I want to build a skyscraper in the middle of the Indian Ocean. The temple president says, you are mad. Prabhu, what is the principle? When I am thinking for Lord Krishna, you are telling I am mad. What kind of representative of Krishna are you?

### devotional service, purity, Krishna Shakti
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/7y2AIc-Q_4Y?t=426
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The glory of Prabhupada is that his service to his, whatever he did was pure devotional service to the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that Prabhupada's glory lies in his pure devotional service to the Lord, not just in material achievements, and that such service is made possible by Krishna Shakti.

You will say, no, you, there is a process. This is your anarttha. This is your desire to do. It's all doesn't go so easily. You will not, when you read Prabhupada doing big things in the neo-faith, oh my God, he achieved with penniless, he built that. These are all good for karmis. This kind of glory is penniless. Prabhupada was, so many people penniless have become multi-billionaires in this world. Yes or not? Without nothing, they have built an empire. Is that the glory of Prabhupada? The glory of Prabhupada is that his service to his, whatever he did was pure devotional service to the Lord. That is that, what he, who was the beneficiary of that doing? That is what makes Prabhupada great. So many people have started penniless and made billions for themselves. Prabhupada started penniless and made billions for Sankirtana movement.

### Krishna Shakti, instrument, inner consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/7y2AIc-Q_4Y?t=501
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "I want to be like Prabhupada. Means you have to understand his heart and be like Prabhupada in his heart. Not like Prabhupada in the outside how it appears."
**Summary:** The difference lies in Krishna Shakti, which enables pure devotional service. To emulate Prabhupada, one must understand and embody his inner consciousness, not just imitate his external actions.

That is what the difference is. And that is Krishna Shakti. Only with Krishna Shakti, you can do seva for Krishna. Does not allow big services like that. Only by Krishna Shakti, you can do it. So he is Shakti of a Shavatara. Shakti of Lord Krishna has come into him. Then he is able to do that. That means he is able to become an instrument. Become an instrument for Lord Shakti to come inside and do all these things. So we need to. So when you read, initially we may get excited because we also say, Okay, okay, nothing Prabhupada had, he did all that. It is a devotional service also because you are appreciating Prabhupada. But should not think that is what it is. Therefore, I can also do something like this. So these are all material ambitions comes. These are not the inner consciousness of a pure devotee. I want to be like Prabhupada. Means you have to understand his heart and be like Prabhupada in his heart. Not like Prabhupada in the outside how it appears.

## Chant Hare Krishna and Exactly the Same Sound You Hear | Sri Madhu Pandit Dasa | Soulful Japa
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

### Krishna's compassion, Eternal relationship with Krishna, Forgetfulness and Maya
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/PAoJkpffwXw?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "All our forgetfulness of who we are is the cause of all our suffering."
**Summary:** The speaker describes Krishna as the source of everything, emphasizing his compassion and our eternal relationship with him, which is currently obscured by forgetfulness and Maya.

Kilir kale nama rupe Krishna Avatara. Krishna, the supreme personality of Godhead, the cause of all causes, the source of everything in existence, sacchidananda vigraha, who is within everything and without everything, who is within and without, as well as who is residing in his own abode in Goloka Vrindavana. That Krishna's one of the qualities, that he is unlimitedly compassionate. The existence is not only Krishna, but his unlimited energies. We are all his energies, we are all his amshas. We are also eternal, just as Krishna is eternal. And since we are eternally coming from him, we all have an eternal relationship with him. Now we are forgotten. Now we are thinking we are this body, we are belonging to this world. Whether we are forgotten or not, that the Atma is part and parcel of the Supreme Atma, Supreme Person, the Supreme Being and the Tiny Being. We are all tiny beings. So the Supreme Lord, who is the Supreme Being, the father of all living entities, He never forgets His relationship with us. He never forgets that we all belong to Him. He never forgets, but we forget. We go into Maya, He doesn't go into Maya. We forget our eternal life and create false stories in this material world each life. I am this body. But He never forgets. Therefore, Kalerkale Nama Rupe Krishna Avatara Krishna is always descending into this world in a way in which He can give His mercy to us and make us remember who we are. All our forgetfulness of who we are is the cause of all our suffering. So it's a Maya energy that is covering us. That is blinding us. That Maya energy can be countered only by another energy, Krishna's energy, direct energy. Maya is Krishna's inferior energy. Those who desire independent existence, for them this Maya world is created. But that is only His desire. That is not His constitutional position. So our original position is that we belong to Krishna. Awakening this original relationship with Krishna is possible only if this Maya energy is dissolved.

---
### Nama Avatara, Power of the Holy Name, Purification of the Heart
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/PAoJkpffwXw?t=273
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So this Nama is so powerful that it can penetrate all the gross and subtle covering over the soul and go and touch the soul."
**Summary:** Krishna descends as Nama Avatara, investing all His energies into the sound of the holy name, which has the power to purify the heart and awaken our relationship with Krishna.

So Krishna comes down in this material world in the Nama form, in the sound form. Where He invests all His energy, Sat energy, Chit energy, Ananda energy. Sat energy means eternity energy. Chit energy means knowledge energy. Ananda energy means pure love and pleasure. That energy is entering this world. Sat energy is entering the sound that you and me utter. This is where Shraddha comes, faith. This is a revelation. Once you utter Nama, Krishna Nama, it is not ordinary sound at all. Into that sound has entered all Krishna's Shakti first. And Krishna's Shakti and Krishna cannot be separated. Just like sun and sunlight cannot be separated. So why the sound becomes Shakti Avesha Nama? Because of the presence of Krishna. Krishna Nama Prabhu is present in the Shakti Avesha Nama. And it is the Nama Prabhu who is shining this sunshine like Satchidananda shine into the sound. So by hearing this sound, we take the Nama Avatara who is appearing on our tongue in the sound form. By hearing Him, we are making the Nama Avatara the Shakti Avesha Nama Avatara to touch our heart, to touch our mind. This is gross body and subtle body. Grossly appears as the hearable sound. Then it goes into our subtle body. Subtle body is which gets purified. Chetodharpanamarjanam And then once this subtle body, manas, buddhi and ahankara is purified by this Satchidananda. Then the Nama goes and touches the soul. So this Nama is so powerful that it can penetrate all the gross and subtle covering over the soul. And go and touch the soul. By disciplined repeated touching of the Nama with attention and devotion, our heart becomes completely purified.

---
### Purification and Forgetfulness, Krishna's Presence, Kala Shakti
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/PAoJkpffwXw?t=479
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It is artificial to think that Krishna is not with me anytime."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that purification awakens our remembrance of Krishna and dispels the illusion of separation, while also discussing Krishna's presence as Paramatma and the influence of Kala Shakti.

Purified means that forgetfulness of my Lord is awakened. And the forgetfulness goes. Then one does not move around in this world itself as a standalone living being. It is artificial to think that Krishna is not with me anytime. Whether we are devotee, non-devotee, in what bodies we are, it is illusion to think that Krishna is not with us. Krishna has different forms. As you surrender, I reciprocate. Krishna for this world is expanded as Paramatma, that is His Leela in this material world by which He permits all the living entities to do whatever they want and earn the result of that according to their karma. So Krishna is there. Krishna doesn't forget us. He is there in everyone's heart. But then those who do not recognize Him as a person, as a supreme father, as a supreme source of everything, as my complete whole, counter whole, I am incomplete on my own. So to those who do not recognize Krishna as a person, supreme person in your heart, as your maintainer, as your provider, as your eternal guardian, those who do not recognize that Krishna also has another Shakti called Kala Shakti. So all living beings who are not devotees of the Lord, who do not recognize, who do not want to be linked up with Krishna, they also touch Krishna in the form of Kala and that is how everybody's life keeps moving. It's a Kala Shakti. Nobody can stop that. That is why we grow old. That is why Sukha comes, Sukha goes, Dukha comes, Dukha goes. Continuous change is happening in this material world. Continuous change is happening in everyone's life. That is the Kala Shakti of Krishna. Those who surrender to Krishna, those who accept him as his supreme Lord, those who accept their original relationship with Krishna as eternal servant, the Kala Shakti subsides. It will be there. But Krishna will shape which way Kala should hit you or gift you or whatever it is. Sukha, Dukha, everything.

---
### Power of Nama, Sat, Chit, Ananda, Realization of Self
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/PAoJkpffwXw?t=709
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "By devoted attention to the holy names, to the incarnated sound, there will be gradual flow of Satya energy, Chitta energy, Ananda energy into our heart."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the power of the holy name as a reservoir of energies, explaining how devoted attention to it facilitates the flow of Satya, Chitta, and Ananda into our hearts, leading to self-realization and seeing others as eternal selves.

But Krishna is in control of the devotee's life. So this Nama, this Nama is a reservoir of all energies because as I told you, unless that energy is there, we cannot wake up. We need Satya, Chitta and Ananda energy to flow within us. By devoted attention to the holy names, to the incarnated sound, there will be gradual flow of Satya energy, Chitta energy, Ananda energy into our heart. When Satya energy flows into our heart, it becomes crystal clear that I am separate from this body. Just like how in a coconut, the copra, it separates, the fruit separates from the shell. Now it is all merged. But it becomes very clear that this body is excluded from the I. And that realization that I have no birth, no death, I am eternal, that becomes a natural realization of oneself. That also changes that person's because of this flow of Satya energy, he starts seeing all people also being in forgetfulness but they are actually not the body, they are their eternal self. Such a self in this material world, all enacting as different body persons, in maya, he sees that. He starts seeing not only his self as different from the body, he sees other selves as different from their bodies but trapped in the bodily concept of life. Engaged in this maya drama.

### Chitta and Ananda Energy, Relationship with Krishna, Asat, Achitta, Nirananda
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/PAoJkpffwXw?t=862
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "We live life as if we don't die."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the flow of Chitta and Ananda energy, leading to the realization of our relationship with Krishna and the expression of love through devotional service, contrasting it with the temporary and illusory nature of material existence characterized by Asat, Achitta, and Nirananda.

Chitta energy flows into our heart. Our original nature, that I am. Satya energy means you get realization that I am spirit, I am Sat, I am eternal. Chitta energy means I am eternal, then what? I am part and parcel of the supreme eternal. Chitta energy comes, then the supreme, who is the supreme being? Who is the supreme Satya? Krishna. And what is my relationship with him? It's a personal relationship of service, personal relationship of bhakti, personal relationship of surrender. Chitta energy flows into our heart. Ananda energy is next awakening of our innate tendency, innate nature to serve my supreme lord. So love and ananda is expressed by activities of devotional service. And those who worship the holy name, those who awaken, gradually, by touch of the supreme sacchidananda, then slowly one loses his taste for any other work than Krishna's work. Or even if you do any work, you will see it is connected to Krishna. So this example is given by Prabhupada of the fire and the iron piece. So the fire of sacchidananda, fire of shaktiavesha nama enters our heart and our iron-like heart catches fire of satya chittananda. Right now our heart is influenced by asat achitta nirananda. So our experience is asat. That means temporary we think as eternal. We see people as eternal. Means people's bodies are eternal. Asat. We live life as if we don't die. We live life as if we are not going to grow old. The reality is different. You will grow old, you will die, everything. But we will be in illusion. That is called asat achitta and jadananda. The sense pleasures are equal to pleasure of the soul. Jadananda. Whereas our real ananda is satya chitta ananda. Not jada ananda. It's atma ananda.

### Soulful Japa, Attention in Chanting, Transformation through Association
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/PAoJkpffwXw?t=1086
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Attention means you have entered the fire."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the process of Japa is associating with the Nama Avatara, emphasizing the importance of attentive and devoted chanting over mechanical repetition, comparing it to entering a fire versus merely being close to it.

So we don't have to do lot of jnana, lot of thinking. Nothing we have to do. The entire process of japa is association with the nama avatara. The more and more you associate your heart will awaken. Original consciousness will awaken. So the challenge is how effectively do I associate? And that is the training of soulful japa. How effectively we can. You can mechanically chant and finish your sixteen rounds. You can keep the piece of iron very close to the fire. But it will have effect of getting little heat. But if you enter the fire, it will become red hot very fast. There is no comparison between the efficiency of inattentive mechanical chanting and attentive chanting. Attention means you have entered the fire. Attentive, devoted chanting and hearing means rapidly the flow of satchitan comes. The whole misconception about the present life. Misconception about the life of body centered life. Discovery of one's deathless situation of eternal service to the supreme lord.

There is no struggle to think of Krishna. Krishna is inseparable from the soul. That is the nature of the soul. My bodily life is my life. Discovering one's eternal life everything becomes automatically. Just like in a dark room as soon as you put the light on everything becomes clear. Krishna consciousness awakening is exactly like that. The light of Krishna consciousness will expose all the illusory existence that we are having and put us on the eternal service of the Lord. So therefore

## Dedicating the Success of Our Service to Srila Prabhupada | @madhupanditdasaofficial
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

### gratitude, faith, divine protection, obstacles
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Py-nk5cbuKc?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The path of the devotees is not a path of truth. The path of the devotees is a path of faith. And the path of the devotees is a path of ance."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the importance of faith and devotion in overcoming obstacles, emphasizing the Lord's protection and guidance for those who follow Prabhupada's instructions.

Bro, it's been like 24 years that Lordships made divine appearance in the HK Hill, and every moment we relish in HK Hill, it's just because devotees like you have worked hard. I'm very thankful to you because the sacrifices and hardships that you have taken to construct this temple, because of which we are relishing here. I have basically two questions for you, bro. The first question is, there must have been many hurdles and difficulties while you were trying to construct this temple. What always kept you motivated? This is my first question. And second question, in the past two to three decades, whatever has happened in your leadership is a miracle. Coming up with the mega temples like this, soulful japa modules. How do you feel about your success in the mission of Guru? Devotees, they live their life on a day-to-day basis. They have faith that following Prabhupada's instructions of chanting Hare Krishna Maha Mantra 16 rounds and following the morning sadhana, one gets guided, protected by the Lord the whole day, in spite of all the weaknesses, in spite of all the fall downs. Overarching protection of the Lord is there, and His will will be manifest through devotees who follow the instructions of the spiritualist. So in the process, lot of obstacles from the material point of view. There will be all kinds of obstacles just like any other institution building. All those obstacles will be there. But here, we don't deal with those obstacles on our own. Neither we carry that burden on our head too much. We leave it to the Lord and execute our devotional service, and He gives the intelligence to deal with such problems. See that includes the loving kindness, the love for the Lord, the prayers, the prayers of sacred leaders and the prayers of the devotees. All that He gives to the devotees, and the paradigms of the devotees. This is also predicated on us. All our good deeds shall be inculcated in the sense of our actions, and we have to work and work and work. That shall be the right way. The path of the devotees is not a path of truth. The path of the devotees is a path of faith. And the path of the devotees is a path of ance. This path of próthesis and wisdom to the devotees, buddhi yoga. Our buddhi gets connected with Krishna's buddhi.

### divine mercy, Krishna's presence, faith, provision
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Py-nk5cbuKc?t=175
**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

All the obstacles are there. It will be there. You will cross over by His mercy. And those obstacles become an opportunity for a devotee to see Krishna's hands everywhere. Just like I mentioned in several other classes, how we started maybe a two bedroom house with anxiety and how we would get every month money to run a temple, 15-20,000 rent and all these things. How in 40 years, 35 years, 35 years, not a single instance where we had to face shortage of, particularly you can say, saksha. No. And never we have had any arrangement of some security. Even today we don't have any such a big temple as to run. People can't believe how it is possible. How next month's electricity bill will come, how next month's clean how you will maintain, how salaries will come. So the Lord is maintaining. With your own eyes you can see. See if you want, you need to have eyes to see this. This is not Shraddha. This is not simply Shraddha. It is a fact. Devotees experience that. It is a fact that the Lord provides. Especially so. That's why this is a Vaikuntha. Especially this is meant, it's pleasing the Lord. Everything that's happening here is pleasing the Lord.

In this house, Hare Krishna Hill, to stay in this house at Hare Krishna Hill He manifests as Radha, Krishna, Chandra, Krishna, Balram, Nithya, Goranga, Narasimha Dev, Srinivasa, Govinda. So we remember their appearance day and we are celebrating. The Lord is very pleased. Especially doing Brahmotsava, you can see the Lord is more pleased. I don't know how many can see that. When they are decorated nicely. When they are on Falanki, they are very smiling and more pleased. When they are on the stage you can see. They are more pleased. Not a small thing. The Jivas are putting their mind fully glorifying Him, praising Him. Whole world belongs to Him. How many people are recognizing Him? Whole world is supported and maintained by Him. How many people are? No. Very few. Just see how rare it is. The activity that is going on at Hare Krishna Hill in the night in Brahmotsava is going on. All kinds of things are happening. So many things are happening in Bangalore city. Corona this, Corona that. All these things are happening. Lockdown, lock. Everything is going on. The seva of the Lord never stops. He is glorified. So Lord is very pleased. If the Lord is pleased then there is all opulence. There is all opulence. There is all victory. All auspiciousness. That is the power of devotional service.

## Ekadashi Lecture I Soulful Japa | Sri Madhu Pandit Dasa |  26.09.2023
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

### holy name, Krishna, identity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/txX01AjDsTQ?t=57
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In the case of Krishna, the Absolute, there is no difference between Krishna and His name."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa explains that Krishna and His name are identical, just as Krishna's form is Krishna Himself.

We have completed the first set of 16 rounds. Before the second set of 16 rounds, I will, as usual, glorify the holy name of the Lord. I will also glorify the Holy Name of the Lord. This is the process of worship of the Holy Name of the Lord. The worship of the Holy Name of the Lord is direct worship of Lord Krishna Himself. Unlike our name and ourselves, in the case of Krishna, the Absolute, there is no difference between Krishna and His name. Just like you say, if you see Krishna's form, you will say, "Oh, this is Krishna." You won't separate. The form is something, Krishna is something. Just as Krishna's form is Krishna, the same way Krishna's name is Krishna. This you have to understand.

---
### Krishna, attributes, personhood
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/txX01AjDsTQ?t=169
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Not only Krishna's name is Krishna, everything connected to Krishna, Krishna's personal aspect of Krishna, is Krishna Himself."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Krishna's attributes, stating that everything connected to Krishna is Krishna Himself, and that Krishna, like us, is a person.

Not only Krishna's name is Krishna, everything connected to Krishna, Krishna's personal aspect of Krishna, is Krishna Himself. So, the Supreme Lord has Nama, Rupa, Leela, Guna, all these things, just like we have. We have Rupa. We have Sharira. Sharira is Rupa. We have a name. We have a mind. We have intelligence. The same way, Krishna is a person. So, Krishna also has, we have one name. Krishna has unlimited names. We have one body. Krishna has unlimited bodies. We have one form. Krishna has unlimited forms. We have limited mind. Krishna has got unlimited mind. We have limited intelligence. Krishna has got unlimited intelligence. This is the difference. Krishna is also a person. We are also persons. God is a person. We are also persons.

---
### duality, Krishna, Advaya Jnana
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/txX01AjDsTQ?t=281
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna and Krishna's name is not different."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa explains the concept of Advaya Jnana, emphasizing the non-duality between Krishna and His name, form, and other aspects, contrasting it with the duality between our bodies and souls.

Now, this difference, Nama, Nami, Abhinatva, Krishna and Krishna's name is not different. That is another difference between us and Krishna. We have a Sharira. Our Sharira is not our Atma. But in Krishna's case, his Sharira is his Atma. Atma is his Sharira. His name is him. He is his name and name is him. He is his form and form is him. This Tattva is called Advaya Jnana. Advaya, anything directly connected to Krishna, there is no duality. Now my body is connected to me, but there is duality. I am not the body. This is the secret. If one knows God is a person, I am a person, what kind of a different person he is than me. This is Krishna Tattva.

---
### Japa, Shabda, Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/txX01AjDsTQ?t=395
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Most important thing that we have to know in the background is that this Shabda is Krishna himself who has appeared before me."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that during Japa, the sound (Shabda) of Krishna's name is Krishna Himself appearing before the devotee, and addresses potential doubts about this concept.

So when we do our Japa, most important thing that we have to know in the background is that this Shabda is Krishna himself who has appeared before me. Now the question may arise. No, but this Shabda I am creating with my tongue. Does it mean that I create Krishna? If I chant, then Krishna is there. If I don't chant, Krishna is there. Not there. And also I am just vibrating. It is a material vibration. So Krishna is the owner of all these energies. So how is it that this sound is Krishna? For that we have to understand that Krishna's Shuddha Nama is Krishna himself. In the spiritual world, Krishna is there. Krishna is Rupa. He is Nama. Everything is. Just mind. Everything is there. He is a full-fledged personality with unlimited, as I told you, unlimited names, unlimited forms, etc. That is the Supreme Lord. He is the Supreme Lord in the spiritual world.

### Nama Avatara, incarnation, energy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/txX01AjDsTQ?t=489
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna enters this sound."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa explains the concept of Nama Avatara, where Krishna's name from the spiritual world enters the sound we utter, and clarifies that Krishna doesn't incarnate into the sound but manifests within it.

So when we say Nama Avatara, this Nama that we utter, Krishna enters this sound. Krishna's Nama of the spiritual world enters this sound that we utter. And as far as Krishna is concerned, just like we are spirit souls, we enter this body. When we enter this body, our body is separate, I am separate. In the case of Krishna, he never incarnates into anybody, whether it is a sound produced by you, or whether it is a marble deity, or a metal deity. Krishna being the source of everything, being the source of that external vigraha, being the source of that, all the external energy, being the source of that, we should consider that sound to be as good as Krishna. He is Krishna himself. If he decides, I am this vigraha, then it is Krishna himself. He decides, it's my energy, it remains his energy. Energetic and energy. What is the difference between uninstalled deity and installed deity, or worshipable deity and showcase external vigraha? In one, is only Krishna's energy. In another, the energetic also has entered that vigraha. Energy and energetic is never separated. But Krishna manifests his energetic nature in the vigraha.

### Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, reciprocity, surrender
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/txX01AjDsTQ?t=634
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is everywhere."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's Achintya Veda Veda Tatva, emphasizing God's omnipresence and His reciprocation based on our surrender, explaining that Krishna's personal shakti enters the Nama.

Krishna is everywhere. Krishna is there in everything. This is Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's Achintya Veda Veda Tatva. There is no thing, there is no place, there is no situation where God is not present. But being present is one thing, His reciprocation is another thing. He reciprocates according to the principle of as you surrender, I reciprocate. The scriptures have revealed that all the shaktis, Krishna's personal shakti comes into the nama. This is nama avatara. So, the moment the Lord decides to enter the sound, shakti avesha, enter the sound that you utter, that sound, you should have faith that Krishna is Himself dancing on my tongue. He is Almighty Lord. Being in the spiritual world, still He comes down on my tongue. And why, especially on the order of the Guru, why do we chant? We chant to worship this nama avatara and take association from this nama avatara and become liberated from this miseries of this material world. Become liberated from the three modes of material nature.

### faith, Krishna, realization
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/txX01AjDsTQ?t=755
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna, He becomes that to touch you, to give you association, He becomes that."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa encourages listeners not to doubt that the sound of the holy name is Krishna Himself, emphasizing the importance of faith and the potential for realization through Japa.

So, that is the truth about this sound. So, don't worry that oh, I am producing this sound, how it can be Krishna? Krishna, He becomes that. To touch you, to give you association, He becomes that. So, therefore, in nama japa, the most fundamental, minimum bhavana is sriddha bhavana. Only if we have this bhavana, then we will not convert our japa into a mechanical japa. So, otherwise, we think that japa is just some process, finish sixteen rounds. But in reality, Krishna is coming down to relate with us, to give us association, to give us realization that He is present before us. Give us realization that He is this Samudra, He is the Surya of Satchidananda.

### Srila Prabhupada, Nama Avatara, worship
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/txX01AjDsTQ?t=849
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker discusses Srila Prabhupada's teachings on Nama Avatara, comparing it to the reflection of the sun in different water bodies, and poses the question of how to properly worship God when He comes before us.

Our soul is always craving for Satchidananda. And He is the Surya of Satchidananda. So, Srila Prabhupada has given us this secret. Now, how to worship the Nama Avatara? Nama Avatara is a fact. Just like one sun reflects on different water bodies. Similarly, Nama Prabhu in the form of Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare reflects His presence onto each of our tongues. So, the most expansive Avatara of the Lord is this Kali Yuga. In this Kali Yuga, He is Nama Prabhu in the form of Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare. If Krishna comes before you, what all would you do in a right manner? If God comes before you, what all you should do, what all you will do in the right manner? I say right manner because no real person can be dealt with according to your own whims. So this is the supreme personality of Godhead. What should be my consciousness when God comes before me? The supreme personality of Godhead.

### worship, purpose, liberation

**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy

**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa explains that Krishna incarnates to receive our worship and enter our consciousness through the Hare Krishna Maha Mantra, with the purpose of extracting our souls from the material entanglement.

I should know the situation, the situation in my background. That Krishna has incarnated to receive my worship and to enter my ear holes. Touch my mind and touch my heart by hearing the Hare Krishna Maha Mantra. So for the worship to be complete, for the Nama Avatara to be pleased. Suppose I come to your house for something. If you fulfill that, then I will be very happy. Similarly Nama Avatara is coming on your tongue for a purpose. Because every living entity is his child. Every living entity in the material world is suffering. This is not our real life. So his coming down, his purpose of coming down as a Nama Avatara, just like a magnet. There are so many iron filings, big magnet comes and all the iron filings get attracted to it and you pick out and place the iron filings somewhere else. Similarly Nama Avatara, the purpose of coming off our tongue is to extract our soul from this complicated mess. To extract our soul from out of this body, out of this bodily concept. Extract our soul from false ego.

And inattentive chanting comes because, inattentive chanting, if one does not follow the regulatory principles, it's very difficult to attentively chant. If you have to worship the holy name of the Lord, better and better, we should be free from all sins. No doubt. The holy name is so powerful. All sins are happening because of ignorance. Because we do not know what is our true welfare. For some immediate pleasure, we think that is my real welfare. And we perform sins, sinful activities. And that, since ignorance is a root cause of that, even if you're chanting without being free from sins, very soon if you hear and chant, then sin, sinful habits, plus soul's sinful tendency will all get wiped out. That's good for you? So merciful is the Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare. So the greatest treasure that we all have is this holy name.

### holy name, treasure, background feeling
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/txX01AjDsTQ?t=1528
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This treasure is God Himself."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of carefully serving and relating to the holy name, which is God Himself, and highlights that the quality of Japa depends on the quality of the background feeling.

The greatest treasure should be kept very carefully, served very carefully, related with very carefully. It is not an impersonal treasure, somebody gives you treasure, whole box of jewellery, this that. This treasure is God Himself. So therefore how much? So Prabhupada says you chant sixteen rounds and serve this Nama Mathara everyday. Prabhupada says the quality of japa depends upon the quality of the background feeling. The quality of the background is the differences of foreground mind and background mind and subconscious mind. Now I am speaking to you, you are hearing. That is your conscious mind is hearing. In the background mind you are passively relating what you are hearing with what all you know. That means your background is hearing everything in a context. Context that you are hearing the speaker, the context that what is the subject matter of hearing. These are all contexts. Background mind is a context mind. Background mind is a relating mind. It is through the background mind with which you relate with what you do in the foreground mind.

### background mind, japa, surrender
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/txX01AjDsTQ?t=1644
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa explains that a background mind focused on problems can make chanting mechanical, and emphasizes the importance of keeping the background mind in the context of Japa, such as remembering the purpose or offering feelings of love.

If your background mind is solving some problem and thinking of this problem, feeling some problem, and you are chanting in the foreground mind, then the chanting will become mechanical. So background mind should always be in the context of japa. Whether it is Shraddha, whether it is remembering the purpose of japa, whether you are remembering the golden command, or whether you want to offer feelings of priti for each bead in reciprocation to realizing how much priti he has in coming to me, descending on my tongue, or it can be Aghlada, feeling great elation that God is before me. Feeling so grateful that Lord has come before me from the depth of the heart, cry out to him with gratitude. You have learnt so many things from Prabhupada's books. What is surrender? God has come before you. Practice that now.

Utsaha means how to earn more and more, how to become more and more wealthy, how to become spiritually richer and richer, one more pleasure giving to Nama Prabhu, one more pleasure giving to Nama Prabhu. This bhavana in the background, with this bhavana in the background, you do the tasking. Tasking means building up the number of sevas. Every card is a relationship with the holy name. And, these cards are first a thought. Let's say somebody doesn't know who is Krishna. So if you tell him Krishna is supreme personality of God, it's only a thought. He hears many many many times. Just like we have heard so many times. This fact that Krishna is supreme personality of Godhead. So it has become a feeling. When we say Krishna is supreme personality of Godhead, it becomes a feeling. Bhavana. In the same way, all these bhavanas associated with japa. Number one, first these cards are meant for repeated reading. You repeatedly read them for six months, one year. Then afterwards you don't have to read. They would have become feelings related with the holy name. For instance, Krishna is Krishna's name himself. It is Krishna himself. So it becomes a feeling. Now Krishna is here. ### feelings, Nama Prabhu, concentration
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/txX01AjDsTQ?t=2111
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Good japa is chanting, hearing, feeling the Nama Prabhu."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa explains that good Japa involves feeling the relationship with Nama Prabhu, offering feelings to Nama Prabhu, and feeling the feelings that Nama Prabhu has towards us, emphasizing the importance of synchronized foreground and background minds for concentration.

Just like an angry person, behind the anger there is a thought that causes the anger. There is a reason, there is a thought that causes the anger. But when you are angry, you don't remember the thought, you don't think the thought. You feel the thought. So in the same way, good japa is thinking, sorry, chanting, hearing, feeling the Nama Prabhu. Feeling the relationship with Nama Prabhu. Feeling about Nama Prabhu. Feelings offered to Nama Prabhu. Feeling the feelings that Nama Prabhu has towards us. So if these feelings are in the background, you will connect it to Nama. Who is in the foreground? Foreground. This feeling is about whom? Feeling is about the Nama Avatara who is appearing on each bead. It is not that foreground mind is going one direction and background mind is feeling. No. Background and foreground mind works in a synchronized manner. Just like now you are listening to me, your foreground mind, background mind is synchronized. When it is synchronized, that is concentration. Then that is giving full attention. Artificially try to bring attention back, bring attention back, bring attention back. How long you will go? It is very tiring for the mind. So if you do groundwork for it, if you have support of your background mind, chanting becomes beautiful. Chanting will never become mechanical.

### soulful japa, attention, divine effort

**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker describes the method of practicing soulful Japa, emphasizing the importance of locking attention through anusarana (following the golden command) and sankalpa (determination), and explains that the shakti (energy) to control the mind comes from the Nama.

That is soulful japa. So we have devised this method of practicing this. For one year, two years you practice, rest of the life you take your first mala, first you lock your attention through anusarana, golden command, feel that I should seva by seva, distinct seva, feel saviour on each bead. All these things, the first mala you, and then you set the, you lock the attention through sankalpa, that I will hear, and vikalpa that I will not, I will not think. So the first mala locks the attention on chanting, hearing, chanting, hearing, chanting, hearing. How it gets locked? The shakti to lock. What is the, what, the mind gets dragged away by so many things. So you have to control the mind to lock. But we only struggle first round, deliberately, with faith that the shakti to control the mind is in the nama. Divya cheshta, what you are doing is a divya cheshta, is a divine effort. So divine effort, you get divya cheshta shakti from nama.

### divya cheshta shakti, smooth chanting, golden command
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/txX01AjDsTQ?t=2351
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "How beautifully the attention gets locked to chanting, hearing, chanting, hearing, chanting, hearing."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa explains that when divya cheshta shakti (divine effort energy) flows into our hearts, we experience ease and smoothness in chanting, and emphasizes the importance of following the golden command (anusvarna) for soulful Japa.

And this can be really experienced. How beautifully the attention gets locked to chanting, hearing, chanting, hearing, chanting, hearing. No, nothing else comes in the mind. If divya cheshta shakti is flowing into our heart, and it flows into our heart, we will feel no struggle, in doing the japa, we will feel that ruchi, that sense of ease with which we chant Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare. If you move your finger in some paint, or in honey, like this, you will find lot of resistance, no? You move your finger in the air, no resistance. When you get divya cheshta shakti, your chanting and hearing will be like this, in the air. Smoothly, mind will not disturb. Otherwise mind will drag. So mala one, anusvarna arthavat vidivat. Anusvarna means obedience to golden command. Arthavat means purposeful. Whenever any activity we do with a, we load the purpose in the background, then there will be focus. Your purpose should not be routine finishing of sixteen rounds. To understand the purpose is worshipping nama on each bead.

So arthavat means purposeful. Vidivat, Guru has told exactly what is the vidhi. Chant distinctly, hear clearly, the very same chant is a vidhi. And, to uninterruptedly chant. Uninterrupted means uninterrupted by any other doing. Japa time means japa doing, not any other doing. You can kirtaniya sadahari, you can chant, you are cooking, you chant while driving, all that you can do, extra. But you have to chant sixteen rounds, without doing anything else. Without solving, trying to engage your mind in solving this problem, that problem, thinking about what I should do today, this, that, all these things. That is something else. This exclusively doing for Krishna. So that is a vidhi.

## Every Devotee’s Journey is Unique, Avoid Comparison | Sri Madhu Pandit Dasa | SB 3.22.7 | 31.07.2024
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

### oral reception, Krishna consciousness, spiritual master
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/b4SP84ASur8?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna consciousness, ultimately, is a path of direct reception of the words that emanate from the transcendental platform."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes the importance of oral reception from an authorized source for spiritual growth and cleansing the heart, highlighting Krishna consciousness as a path of direct reception from the transcendental platform.

OM NAMO BHAGWATE VASUDEVAYA. Reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, Canto 3, Chapter 22, Text 7. I have fortunately been instructed by you and thus great favor has been bestowed upon me. I thank God that I have listened with open ears to your pure words. Srila Rupa Goswami has given directions in his Bhakti Rasamita Sindhu on how to accept a bona fide spiritual master and how to deal with him. First, the desiring candidate must find a bona fide spiritual master and then he must very eagerly receive instructions from him and execute them. This is reciprocal service. A bona fide spiritual master or saintly person always desires to elevate a common man who comes to him because everyone is under the delusion. Because everyone is under the delusion of Maya and is forgetful of his prime duty, Krishna Consciousness, a saintly person always desires that everyone become a saintly person. It is the function of a saintly person to invoke Krishna Consciousness in every forgetful common man. Manu said that since he was advised and instructed by Kardamma Muni, he was very much favored. He considered himself lucky to receive the message by oral reception. It is especially mentioned here that one should be very inquisitive to hear with open ears from the authorized source of the bona fide spiritual master. How is one to receive? One should receive the transcendental message by oral reception. The word, Karnarandhraja, means through the holes of the ears. The favor of the spiritual master is not received through any other part of the body but the ears. This does not mean, however, that the spiritual master gives a particular type of mantra through the ears in exchange for some dollars and if the man meditates on that, he achieves perfection and becomes God within six months. Such reception through the ears is bogus. The real fact is that the bona fide spiritual master knows the nature of a particular man and what sort of duties he can perform in Krishna Consciousness and he instructs him in that way. He instructs him through the ear not privately but publicly, you are fit for such and such work in Krishna Consciousness, you can act in this way. One person is advised to act in Krishna Consciousness by working in the deity's room. Another is advised to act in Krishna Consciousness by performing editorial work. Another is advised to do preaching work. And another is advised to carry out Krishna Consciousness in the cooking department. There are different departments of activity in Krishna Consciousness and a spiritual master, knowing the particular ability of a particular man, trains him in such a way that by his tendency to act, he becomes perfect. Bhagavad Gita makes it clear that one can attain the highest perfection of spiritual life simply by offering service according to his ability, just as Arjuna served Krishna by his ability in the military art. Arjuna offered his service fully as a military man and he became perfect. Similarly, an artist can attain perfection simply by performing artistic work under the direction of the spiritual master. If a man is a literary man, he can write articles and poetry for the service of the Lord under the direction of the spiritual master. One has to receive the message of the spiritual master in order to be perfect. Spiritual master regarding one's capacity for the spiritual master is expert in giving such instructions. This combination, the instruction of the spiritual master and the faithful execution of the instruction by the disciple makes the entire process perfect. Srila Vishwanath Chakravarti Thakur describes in his explanation of the verse in Bhagavad Gita, that one who wants to be certain to achieve spiritual success must take the instruction from the spiritual master as to what his particular function is. He should faithfully try to execute that particular instruction and should consider that his life and soul. The faithful execution of the instruction by the disciple is the best way to achieve spiritual success. The faithful execution of the instruction which he receives from the spiritual master is the only duty of a disciple and that will bring him perfection. One should be very careful to receive the message from the spiritual master through the ears and execute it faithfully. That will make one's life successful. That ends the bhaktivedanta. So the manu is expressing his gratitude. I thank God that I have listened with open ears to your pure words. And he rejoicing in his good fortune. The favor he received by listening to instructions from Haridavamuni. So this is a process of spiritual elevation. This is a process of spiritual growth. This is the process of liberation from material energy. What is the process, the process is oral reception. You have to hear from authorized source. When you hear from the authorized source, submissive oral reception, that will cleanse our heart of all the lower gunas. Tama guna, raja guna, and all the non-guna and eventually even sattva guna and put us in pure vishuddha sattva, transcendental consciousness, just by hearing. It is not the path of Krishna consciousness. Krishna consciousness, ultimately, is a path of direct reception of the words that emanate from the transcendental platform.

As far as spiritual bhakti is concerned, when you compare it with the process of receiving transcendental words from the spiritual master, receiving transcendental sound, the material speculative intelligence is like a shutter. It has to be opened up. What does that mean? First, one should have faith that the shabda, that the words that emanate from a pure devotee, has God, the knowledge is God, is contained within that. The intelligence to understand those words are also contained within that. You only have to receive transcendental intelligence. Your material intelligence gets purified by transcendental intelligence. Material intelligence cannot lead us to transcendental intelligence. Only oral reception can lead us to transcendental intelligence. Intelligence. By receiving oral, by oral reception of transcendental facts and transcendental intelligence, which is contained in those words, the Shakti is there in those, the transcendental knowledge Shakti or Vidya Shakti is there in that transcendental words. This is a very mystical process. We don't understand, we don't try to understand Bhaktivedanta Parpat. Like a college professor tries to understand, like a philosophy professor tries to understand Prabhupada's books. This is not the way we understand, we read. Okay?

So that is the secret. By simply, repeatedly hearing, that is why we say you should read, read Prabhupada's book, read Prabhupada's book. Something you don't intellectually understand, leave it aside. Go on reading. Leave it for now. One day it will become apparent. It will become clear. Try a little to understand. Beyond that you don't try. Then it becomes speculation. Smoothly it should come. Understanding should come smoothly. No spiritual knowledge can be actually captured. No spiritual knowledge can actually be controlled. No spiritual knowledge will stay with you if you have a sense of ownership. Vidya will run away if you have a sense of ownership. You should have a sense of serving that knowledge. By giving out that knowledge. Hearing repeatedly that knowledge. What is the meaning of sense of ownership of knowledge? Moment in the idea comes. Okay, now I know this. It's mine. I am a knowledgeable person. I have it. But you know in one second your knowledge can go. It can go into forgetfulness. It is just like Karmis think. I am rich. I got lot of money. In one moment money can go anytime. He can become a pauper. In the same way transcendental knowledge also. You cannot have a sense of possession. You cannot have a sense of possession towards a transcendental knowledge. There should be a sense of reception. All reception. Welcoming and hearing the sound. That comes from a transcendental source. And that sound will expand transcendental knowledge into our heart. Realization into our heart. Transcendental feelings into our heart. And unless transcendental feelings come into our heart. Nothing will get purified. Because all our impurities are in the form of feelings. Wrong feelings. And where are these wrong feelings? These wrong feelings are all stored in our subconscious mind. That is why habits and all don't change. Intellectually you are convinced but your habits don't change. Why? Because they are all stored and packed and tied together with certain feelings in your subconscious. So unless the subconscious gets purified. Subconscious means you are not conscious.
