It contrasts short-term memory (chemical state) with long-term memory (structural change in the brain) to illustrate the importance of deep absorption.

It has to get purified. Means the right feelings have to go inside as we discussed yesterday. Submissive hearing. Submissive hearing means even the feeling of the speaker will go inside. The feeling has to go inside because the contamination is inside. Deep inside. Unless it enters. Simply it remains at an intellectual conviction at your conscious level. That is not enough. You will get temporary benefit. 2-3 days you will be fired up. After hearing something. You hear a japa seminar 2-3 years. 2-3 days your chanting will be very nice. Afterwards it is gone. Why? 2-3 days it is in your short term memory it is there. Scientists have found out that in short term memory means it is only a chemical state. Today you hear something in your neurons. The chemical you know chemical adjustment. Long term memory means that chemical adjustment goes and changes your neurons structure. There is a structural change in your brain. Just other day I was reading this article about neuroplasticity of brain. Brain is such a wonderful thing. Neuroplasticity. Your change can happen by difference in chemical concentrations in the mind. In the brain. Let us say now you learn one sloka. In your short term memory it remains. Immediately in the brain there are certain chemical levels which is corresponding to this learning sloka. And it remains there. But in 2-3 days it will go away. It will go away. It will go away. But in 2-3 days that chemical will go to normal. You have forgotten this sloka. You cannot repeat that sloka. But if it goes into long term memory. That goes into a structure. Just like we go on saying you know. Say every day we start with Bhagavatam by chanting you know. Nityam Bhagavata Seveya Bhagavati Uttama Slokye Bhaktir Bhavati Naishitiki. Where is this? You are not consciously saying. It has become a structural change in your mind. Let us say first time when you learn that sloka. First 2-3 days. It has not yet become. You will forget it. Now you will not forget it. Because it will just come. Because it has become a structural change in your mind. So anyway. So that is about simple memory thing. So these things have to go deep inside through feelings. Then there has to be a submissive oral reception. That oral reception is so important. That it is such a big thing. ### japa, chanting, attention
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/N1mRycUGyCc?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Japa means chanting the holy name, hearing that very name that you chant, and tasking to repeat and increase the number of chantings and hearings without any break in attention on the sound."
**Summary:** This segment introduces the concept of japa as chanting and hearing the holy name with focused attention.

As we have discussed many times, japa means chanting the holy name, hearing that very name that you chant, and tasking to repeat and increase the number of chantings and hearings without any break in attention on the sound.

---
### mind, tasking, thinking
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/N1mRycUGyCc?t=35
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Mind is never quiet; it is always tasking something."
**Summary:** This segment explains that the mind is always engaged in tasking, either thinking or doing.

So, the mind is always tasking something, either tasking or doing. Suppose I tell you, get me a glass of water. It's a task that your mind will guide your senses. You'll go where the water is and bring the water to me. Till then, the mind is engaged in tasking. Do it. Tasking can be done. A tasking can be a doing. A tasking can be a problem-solving. Tasking can be a thinking. The mind involves in tasking either a doing or a thinking. For instance, you have a problem. You encounter a problem. Then your mind engages in tasking to find a solution for that. So, the mind is either always tasking. Mind is always tasking. Either thinking tasking or doing tasking. Mind is never quiet. It is always tasking something.

So, in japa, in nama japa, chanting, chanting the holy names of the Lord, it's japa meditation. Meditation on the sound because it is not an ordinary song. The name of the absolute Lord, therefore, the name is Lord himself. Lord is before you as a sound incarnation, is before you as a Shabda Avatara. As a Shabda Avatara, Avatara means the Lord comes in a very accessible way to our senses. So, Shabda Avatara means the Lord comes, enters a sound in order to give us association. This is called incarnation. So, this sound, Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Ram, Hare Ram, Hare Hare, is not an ordinary sound. It is a sound in which the Lord has entered. It becomes a Lord's sound body. Touching this sound is equal to touching the Lord. The Lord is all pure, all knowledge, all bliss, all love.

Many times I have given a summary talk on soulful japa. So now onwards on our Ekadashi, I will deal with the cards, the cue cards that we have made. These cue cards are how best to utilize these cue cards. These cue cards are ultimately the content in these cue cards, the intellectual content, the spiritual content, the emotional content has to enter our subconscious mind by repeated reflection and contemplation. Once it enters our subconscious, then it creates a wonderful background to our chanting and hearing in the foreground. The foreground mind will be chanting and hearing and tasking. The background mind will be relating with the Nama Prabhu who is before us. This is the basic premise of Nama Prabhu. This is the basic premise of Nama Prabhu. This is the basic premise of Nama Prabhu. Soulful japa. Soulful japa means whole mind japa.

### Shraddha, Truth Narrative, Heart Talk
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3N84GvoFdBY?t=966
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Shraddha that Krishna himself has incarnated before me on each bead as his persona of Shakti Avesha Nama."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the truth narrative, which is Shraddha, emphasizing the belief that Krishna himself is present on each bead as His Shakti Avesha Nama persona, and that spiritual life is all about talk and narrative.

Truth narrative is during japa. Truth narrative is called Shraddha. Shraddha that Krishna himself has incarnated before me on each bead as his persona of Shakti Avesha Nama. See Nama Prabhu. This is a simple matter of fact. Faith. Matter of truth. Absolute truth. As soon as I am chanting Krishna who is within and without of everything is come before me as the hearable, chantable sound. Is manifest. Is pratyeksha. Immediately. So that truth, that feeling is called Shraddha. Shraddha is fundamental. Any other Bhavana is not there also. Shraddha is fundamental. That is why I said even in mindful chanting Shraddha is a must. Without Shraddha mindful chanting is like a mechanical chant. Mechanically you may hear every word that you are chanting. Because of the mind control. You control the mind that each sound is captured. But Shraddha, why each sound you should take inside because he is Krishna himself has come before me. That is Shraddha. So that is a heart talk. Shraddha is a heart talk. See the soul in this material world in this body. Trapped in this material world. Trapped as in Indra. One of the faculties that the soul, even now most powerful faculty of the soul is its ability to talk. Remember that. Not ability to think, not ability to see, it is ability to talk. So spiritual life is all about Shraddha. It is about talk. Your mind immediately aligns with the talk. Narrative. That is why I call this as narrative. Bhavana is nothing but emotionalized and intellectualized narrative. Narrative of something you are convinced about.

## Gaura Purnima 2026 | 1st Anniversary of Arunodaya Kirtan | Sri Madhu Pandit Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

### Arunodaya Kirtan, Congregational Chanting, Lord Chaitanya's Grace
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TLxtrwKrtV0?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Today, by Lord Chaitanya's grace, it has become a vibrant spiritual activity touching thousands of lives."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa celebrates the first anniversary of Arunodaya Kirtan in Bangalore, highlighting its impact and the blessings of various deities and Srila Prabhupada.

Hare Krishna, happy Gopurnima to all of you. Today we are very happy to share a wonderful milestone by the mercy of Shri Shri Radha Krishna Chandra, Krishna Balaram, Thayagauranga, Srinivasa Govinda, Pralana Shingadev and the blessings of Vishwaguru Shilaprabhu Paan. We have achieved the milestone of completing one full year of Arunodaya Kirtan in Bangalore city. One year ago, this initiative began as a humble offering, a sincere desire to bring devotees together from the early hours of the morning in congregational chanting of the holy names of the Lord in the streets of Bangalore. Today, by Lord Chaitanya's grace, it has become a vibrant spiritual activity touching thousands of lives.

---
### Nama Sankirtana, Love of God, Shri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TLxtrwKrtV0?t=53
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Because our dormant love of God already exists within our heart and it can be awakened simply by chanting Hare Krishna Mahamantra daily with sincerity and enthusiasm."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of congregational chanting as the mission of Shri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, explaining that it awakens dormant love of God without any pre-qualifications.

When we perform congregational chanting, we are directly connected to the mission of Shri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. Shilaprabhupada explains that Lord Chaitanya, the greatest proponent of love of God and the father of congregational chanting of the holy name, personally inaugurated the religion of this age, Nama Sankirtana. Mahaprabhu declared, "I shall personally inaugurate the religion of this age, Nama Sankirtana, the congregation chanting of the holy names." He said, "I will make the world dance in ecstasy." And what is this process? Shilaprabhupada beautifully explains that there is no pre-qualification required, no material condition to be fulfilled, because our dormant love of God already exists within our heart and it can be awakened simply by chanting Hare Krishna Mahamantra daily with sincerity and enthusiasm. Through this joyful process of chanting and dancing, one gradually develops genuine love for the Supreme Lord. This is the great mercy of Lord in this age.

---
### Accessibility of Devotion, Arunodaya Kirtan Results, Prasadam Distribution
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TLxtrwKrtV0?t=125
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "And over the past year, we have witnessed the results practically."
**Summary:** The speaker highlights the accessibility of devotional service through chanting, sharing the practical results of Arunodaya Kirtan in various settings and the abundant distribution of prasadam.

There is no requirement for high birth, no requirement for scholarship and no complicated ritual. Simply chant, Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare. And over the past year, we have witnessed the results practically. Arunodaya Kirtan has been conducted in so many places, in temples, in homes, during festivals, in the community gatherings. During Janmashtami, Ram Navami, Nithyananda Trodeshi, Narsimha Jayanti, Ratha Yatra and many more occasions, thousands have participated in Bangalore city in chanting and dancing. Along with the Kirtan, Prasadam has been distributed abundantly, following the instructions of Srimad Bhagavatam.

---
### Sankirtana Yajna, World Peace, Unity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TLxtrwKrtV0?t=179
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "In such a disturbed climate, congregational chanting of the holy names offer a unifying spiritual platform."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that in this age, intelligent persons worship the Supreme Lord by the sacrifice of Sankirtana, and that congregational chanting offers a unifying spiritual platform in a disturbed world.

In this age, intelligent persons worship the Supreme Lord by the sacrifice of Sankirtana. Srila Prabhupada describes how even the wild animals in the forest of Jarikanda joined Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's Sankirtana. Today the world is disturbed socially, politically, economically and in so many other ways. There is visible strife between individuals, divisions between communities, tensions among nations. So much mistrust, conflict and anxiety. They have become common feature of modern life. In such a disturbed climate, congregational chanting of the holy names offer a unifying spiritual platform. By bringing people together, beyond the differences of background, status and ideology, we are helping them reconnect with the common foundation of love of God. Through Nama Sankirtana, our hearts are purified, relationships are harmonized and genuine unity is achieved among all.

---
### Encouragement, Home Kirtan, Following Srila Prabhupada
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TLxtrwKrtV0?t=255
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Let every home become a center for Kirtan."
**Summary:** The speaker encourages everyone to continue the mission of Sankirtana with enthusiasm, suggesting that every home become a center for Kirtan and to follow in the footsteps of Srila Prabhupada.

The more we perform this Sankirtana Yajna, the more we advance in love of God. Therefore, on this important occasion of completing one year of Arunodaya Kirtan, I would like to encourage all of you, let us continue with our mission with even more enthusiasm. Let every home become a center for Kirtan. If you have the facility, invite devotees to your home, distribute prasadam, hold Kirtan for a few hours, at least once a week. Gather your friends, your family, your neighbors. Just as the rising sun dissipates darkness, congregational chanting dissipates the darkness of ignorance from the heart. Lord Chaitanya predicted that this Sankirtana movement would spread every town and village of the world. Srila Prabhupada established ISKCON with the purpose of teaching this very same process. Let us follow in the footsteps of our Gurudev, Srila Prabhupada.

---
### Arunodaya Kirtan Invitation, Commitment, Spiritual Benefits
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TLxtrwKrtV0?t=323
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Simply chant with faith and enthusiasm. Distribute prasadam with joy. There is no loss. There is only gain, spiritual happiness, purification, unity, and ultimately, love of God."
**Summary:** The speaker invites everyone to participate in the Arunodaya Kirtan on Gaurapurnima, emphasizing the importance of making a firm commitment and highlighting the spiritual benefits of chanting and distributing prasadam.

I would also like to invite all devotees to participate for the Arunodaya Kirtan on 3rd March, Gaurapurnima, the most auspicious day of Lord Chaitanya's appearance, who is the inaugurator of the Sankirtana movement 500 years back. This year, it falls on a weekday. You may have office commitments, important meetings, or responsibility for preparing your children for school. Yet, in your schedule, rise a little earlier if needed. Plan your day in advance and make it a firm commitment not to miss this Arunodaya Kirtan of 3rd March, 2026. Let us assemble in large numbers, in thousands, and offer our collective chanting as our heartful tribute to Lord Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. On this sacred morning, let us begin the day with the holy names and receive His special mercy. Let us take this Arunodaya Kirtan to more areas, more communities, more homes. Let thousands become lakhs. Let lakhs become millions. Simply chant with faith and enthusiasm. Distribute prasadam with joy. There is no loss. There is only gain, spiritual happiness, purification, unity, and ultimately, love of God. This is Krishna Sankirtanam. Sisi Go Nithaya Ki Jai. Arunodaya Sankirtan Ki Jai. Vishwaguru Sheela Prabhupada Ki Jai.

---

To sleep, the manifestation of Krishna. So if somebody wants to serve God, then what need not think in his mind, where is God to receive my service? How do I know he is receiving my service? All that to learn from guru, sadhu, and shastra, that Krishna receives our service immediately. How much relationship we want with God, God is ready to give himself to us. Krishna's only business is to be with his devotees, to be aware of his devotees, to show mercy to his devotees, show love to his devotees, and therefore devotees' only business is always to think of Krishna, always serve him. Krishna being absolute, there is no limitation for him, and he decides, 'I am the deity, the deity,' and he decides, 'I am the sound that you chant, it's Him.' And Krishna is the Surya of sat-chit-ananda. Krishna means Krishna, the Supreme Personality with the supreme energies. Both Krishna and his energies incarnate in Kali Yuga, kalikale nama rupe krishnavatara. They descend in the most accessible manner. Simply you have to move your tongue and God is right there. How to recognize Him? How to feel Him? He is there. That is something that you will achieve by doing that, by hearing from guru, sadhu, and shastra.

### attention in chanting, sat-chit-ananda, Krishna's pleasure
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/7xTYvkfhG4o?t=811
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is very pleased if we chant and hear. Pleased for us."
**Summary:** This segment uses the analogy of iron in fire to explain how attention during chanting allows us to connect with Krishna's sat-chit-ananda, and how Krishna is pleased when we chant attentively because it helps us become liberated.

Devotee, he is chanting mechanically, he is not hearing, thinking of so many other things while the tongue is chanting. Still the Lord is there, but He is saying, 'Oh, how foolish my devotee is. I am before him. I am available as a sound for him to embrace with his attention, but his attention is going somewhere else.' If he embraces me, then there can be flow of sat, chit, ananda, shakti into him, to awaken him, his consciousness, because Krishna is the Surya of sat, chit, ananda. Just like Prabhupada says, a piece of iron put in the fire, slowly it becomes hot, hotter, hotter, red hot. Red hot means fire like fire. Red hot means fire like fire. Red hot means fire like fire. In the same way, Krishna is thinking, he is chanting, 'I am there, the fire of sat, chit, ananda,' but he is not coming into the fire of sat, chit, ananda. Only when you give attention, then you are in the fire. So Krishna is very pleased if we chant and hear. Pleased for us, but we don't get anything. He gets our happiness. My Jeeva who is suffering will get liberated.

---

Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare. So therefore, you have to chant here with Shraddha. Shraddha means Krishna is this sound. Omnipotent. If we touch this sound, how can sound be touched by hearing? Krishna is physically before you. You can embrace Him body to body with your arms around Him. Now also you can embrace Him, but you have to embrace Him on the same time, Him with your attention. Just like a piece of iron put in the fire, the fire embraces the iron embraces the fire. Similarly, when we do japa, Krishna is before us. Krishna is before his devotee.

## Grattitude to Nama Prabhu and Srila Prabhupada | Sri Madhu Pandit Dasa | 23.05.2025
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

### japa, mindfulness, chanting, hearing
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/6py3PgIu4zQ?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So, improving the japa means moving from mindless japa to mindful japa, then to spiritful japa, then to soulful japa."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa explains the progression of japa from mindless to soulful, emphasizing the importance of engaging the mind and consciousness in the process.

So, improving the japa means moving from mindless japa to mindful japa, then to spiritful japa, then to soulful japa. It is a question of practice. If we do not consciously take care, because of the nature of the mind, our japa will be mindless japa. Mindless japa is just tongue japa. Only tongue you are giving to Krishna, not giving your consciousness, not giving your mind, not giving your ears. So, mindless japa is very offensive japa. You are doing japa, but you are neglecting to hear. You are neglecting to give your mind to the nama of the Lord. You are neglecting to give your mind to the avatara who is before us and all these different stages of good quality chanting, good quality japa has to be practiced in a systematic way. You are neglecting to give your mind to the avatara who is before us.

---
### mindfulness, training, japa cards
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/6py3PgIu4zQ?t=119
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "You have to take help from, for training you have to take help from the Soulful Japa cards."
**Summary:** The speaker recommends using Soulful Japa cards to train the mind to move from mindless to mindful japa, suggesting a structured approach to understanding the essence of japa.

You are neglecting to give your mind to the avatara who is before us and all these different stages of good quality chanting, good quality japa has to be practiced in a systematic way. Mind has to be trained for several months to move from mindless to mindful. To move from mindless to mindful, you have to take help from, for training you have to take help from the Soulful Japa cards. Card number 1 to 1.6, then 2.1 to 2.6, then 3.1 to 3.6. For several months, probably I would say at least two months, you completely focus on mindful japa by reading the cards 1.1 to 1.6, 2.1 to 2.6, 3.1 to 3.6. By understanding what is in the card, which gives us, which will load into your consciousness, into your background mind, into your memory, what is, why you are doing japa, what is proper japa, who is the holy name, who is the chanter, what should be your consciousness. So those things.

### hearing from authority, faith, spiritual experience
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/INEV6vpZpUE?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Hearing from an authority is a better experience than gaining personal understanding."
**Summary:** This segment introduces the principle that hearing from a spiritual authority provides a better experience than personal understanding, challenging common sense and emphasizing the need for faith.

Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya. Reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, 10-3, Chapter 22, entitled The Marriage of Kardamamuni and Devahuti, Verse No. 10: YADATU BHAVATASILA SHRUTA RUPA VAYO DUNAN ASHRANON NARADHA DESHA TVAYAYA SITRITANISHAYA. Translation in purport by His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Srila Prabhupada: The moment she heard from the sage Narada of your noble character, learning, beautiful appearance, youth and other virtues, she fixed her mind upon you. Purport by His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Srila Prabhupada: The girl Devahuti did not personally see Kardamamuni, nor did she personally experience. She did not personally see Kardamamuni, his character or qualities, since there was no social intercourse by which she could gain such understanding. But she heard about Tridamamuni from the authority of Narada Muni. Hearing from an authority is a better experience than gaining personal understanding. She heard from Narada Muni that Tridamamuni was just fit to be her husband, therefore she became fixed in her heart that she would marry him, and she expressed her desire to her father who therefore brought her before him. Thus ends the Bhaktivedanta Parvat. Devahuti, the daughter of Swayambhuva Manu, came to fix her mind upon desiring Kardamamuni, is being explained by Manu, and it is specifically explained in the purport by Prabhupada that she knew about Kardamamuni only by hearing from Narada Muni. And by hearing from Narada Muni about Kardamamuni, she got a better experience than even gaining personal understanding. So this principle, hearing from an authority is better experience than gaining personal understanding, it is a very heavy principle being made here. Thank you. Generally, this does not appeal to common sense. One will think that first I should hear and then I should experience. First I should hear and then experience. In spiritual life also we have a tendency, because that is how our mind works. Now I am reading these things, one day I will directly experience. But that is completely being, that process is completely being countered by the statement of Prabhupada. Hearing from an authority is a better experience than gaining personal understanding. We have to believe it. It does not appeal to common sense. It is a very heavy principle. It does not appeal to common sense.

### hearing, purity, spiritual sound
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/INEV6vpZpUE?t=252
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "According to this, if you hear from an authority about Krishna, it is better than seeing Krishna."
**Summary:** This segment explains that hearing about Krishna from a pure source is superior to seeing Krishna with impure senses, emphasizing the importance of a pure source and submissive hearing in spiritual life.

This is relative. Which is better? Just like which is better, hearing about Krishna or seeing Krishna? According to this, if you hear from an authority about Krishna, it is better than seeing Krishna. Because if you happen to see Krishna, if you are not pure, you are not able to understand Krishna. If you are not pure then you will think oh maybe somebody from some drama party has come before me even if he is genuine Krishna. Now hearing Krishna purely, there are different ways of hearing about Krishna, the source should be pure. Now let's go into this hearing experience number one source should be pure. Here because Narada spoke, the hearer should be submissive, because only when you are submissive then the perception will go into your subconscious. At the conscious level your hearing is always being analyzed and judged. Real hearing is not analyzing and judging. Submissive hearing means allowing that sound to enter you and that sound will expand that perception expand the meaning of the shabda. One should have faith that this shabda transcendental of course Prabhupada is talking about transcendental hearing which is you know in our normal world what is it's the other way around no, you hear something and then you hear about America and go and see America, that is correct relative world it is correct, but in the spiritual world it's not like that not spiritual hearing nor during our sadhana stage, it is not like that. Anything to do with spiritual shabda is not that. One has to have faith that the spiritual shabda is impregnated with the meaning of that shabda. What multi-dimensional meaning that are all contained in it. If I use a word, if I say pen, in that word pen, in the material world, pen is not there. In the spiritual shabda, say Hare or Krishna or Rama, in that shabda, Hare is there, Krishna is there, Rama is there. There is no difference between shabda and the personality of that shabda. Because everything is personality. Prabhupada gives example in the spiritual world also. Water, water, you say. You have water.

Whether your thirst should be quenched or not, that water should mercifully decide. Here you put water on the tongue, there is no question of not getting quenched. Because the water here is just matter. It has to. But in the spiritual world, water is a person. So you say water, water, water. That is why it is said, Atta, Ashi, Krishna, Nama, Naba, Naba, Ved, Gra. Mahim, Indriya. Anything in the spiritual world you cannot capture. Everything is a person. There has to be a relationship. You say water, water, water. Water is not different from water. But that water decides personality or I should keep this person thirsty. You decide to keep thirsty. To quench the thirst or not to quench the thirst is the decision of the personality of water. But it is not different. So in the same way, the holy name, Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare. If the shabda is there, then automatically we should automatically become pure in a moment, because it is Krishna himself I am touching. No, he is a person. You deserve, you qualify. Are you begging? What is your mood? Have you fed up with material world? Are you ready to experience me? No, all those things. Yes. Now I will purify you. Chetodharpanam. Chetodharpanam Arjanam. That too, if you are hearing, is a relationship. Oh, and where is the question of, you know, even Chetodharpanam Arjanam, this fellow is completely offensive, this Jeeva. He is utilizing my name for so many things. He thinks I can, you know, I can be used for everything. For his power, knowledge, fame, everything, he can use Harinam. So will Harinam respond? Will not respond. He will respond. He will respond. He will respond appropriately. But the fact is that the Lord is there. Nama Incarnation is there. The Nama Incarnation, the specific character is there. He is very, very merciful. The most merciful incarnation is Nama Incarnation, which is brought down by Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. And this Hare Krishna Maha Mantra is non-different from Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. Radha Krishna Anya. This mantra is nothing but the personalities of Radha and Krishna. So spiritual hearing means you have to remove the shutter of rational mind. Submissively one should hear. Submissively and faithfully one should hear. It's a big, it's a very big transformative power, transformative process that we are all possessing.

How to read Prabhupada's books? How to read Prabhupada's books so that it enters your subconscious? Conscious mind, we are constantly filtering it. How does something go into the subconscious when you are very emotional? Isn't it? That is how people get trauma and all, isn't it? The situation is charged with emotions, your subconscious opens. That is how you say, by this experience I learnt. It opens. Otherwise rational mind will not, your lesson will just remain there. And in few days it will go away. Whatever you learn with your rational mind, so much logic you will tell and this is that, this is that. It will remain for two, three days and it will go away. Those things are not called realizations. Realization is when it goes into your subconscious. And there it brings about permanent change in your perception. Permanent change in your attitude towards life. Towards life. So how to make this shabda of Prabhupada enter into our subconscious? As I said, one of the things is to stop this, remove the shutter of rational filter that we have. Where we analyze and judge everything. I mean you are reading everything. How Krishna is God, why Krishna is God, all these things. Your rational mind. Sometimes you hear, you are very submissively sitting in the class, you hear something. It goes and penetrates your subconscious. Your paradigm shifts. Because all these paradigms are nothing but perspectives which we have stored in our subconscious. How we see things. How we see people. How we see situations. Right? Our mind is full of three things. Three or four things you can see. If you take in mind, it contains in that box, there are four things. Perception of things. Here you see a camera. You see a kartal. You see this thing. Things, things. Room. Building. You have mapped those buildings into it. That is perception of things. Then there is perception of people. What do I think of this person? This person? This person? This person? Right? This person is a man. This person is a woman. You see. Or this person is nice person. This person is bad person. He is friend. He is enemy. So perception of people. Then there is perception of situation. Is it not? Continuously full of these things. Say today you are going out preaching. It is a preaching situation. It is a compound. You get at a glance. Just like I say guru issue. Moment I say guru issue. It is a situation. So many things you will derive out of it. What is guru issue? I tell you. Give me. What is this guru issue? If I ask you to come here. You will stand and you will describe what it is. Actually you are describing what you have your perception. Is it not? So perception of things. People. Situation. And actions. You also perceive actions. Sinful activity. Not sinful activity. Is it not? Actions. Good act. Bad act. Actions. Here there is a fight. There is a situation. And in the situation there is an action of a fight. So there is an action. So object. People. Situations. And actions. O. P. S. A. Our head consists of only O. P. S. A.

There are so many dislikes. Objects. Persons. Again there you see. We paste. I like this person. I don't like this person. Then situations. You like and don't like. Yes or no? Prasadam all. Prasadam. You like that situation. If you are called by authority to be chastised. You don't like that situation. Oh. Why he is calling me? Immediately it is pasted. Automatically pasted. I don't like. These are all we have put likes and dislikes and connected. Our head is full of all these things. When our free will is connected. When we use our free will. And put priti on something like. Yeah no sense object is always fitting on the primary context we should take care by suppressive hearing while second really if you have right context then automatically the right relationship with that sense object will come where is the fear ideally in between there are rules and regulation till the paradigm shifts that's what I'm still the paradigm this there are rules and regulations be very careful this paradigm doesn't go so easily for a primary and second it doesn't go so easily this paradigm this old paradigm of yours doesn't go so easily so be very careful when you relate with the positive sex rules and regulations are required since control is required restraint is required tapasya is required because the old paradigm will come and push when it push you have to hold it that's called tapas you have to control sense control mind control sama dhama gross sense control not allow use your willpower to stop the old paradigm from leading it to action now dhama's lead you are you please use your you use your free will to directly to not to get influenced by the old paradigm at the mind level these two things can be done only if we have the power to tolerate pain which is called tapas so these are all the Brahman has quality without tapas Shakti without without without tolerating without somebody you know I cannot take any pain my mind anything and distasteful I cannot take that means there's got no tapasya theater every moment everything should be pleasing every moment.

## How the Principle of Gurutva Functions Universally | Sri Madhu Pandit Dasa | SB 3.22.8 | 06.08.2024
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

### Guru-disciple relationship, Humility, Devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YgfS_Iid9sM?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When a disciple is perfectly in consonance with the spiritual master having received his message and executed it perfectly and sincerely he has a right to ask a particular favor from the spiritual master."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa begins by reciting the verse and translation, then reads Srila Prabhupada's purport, which discusses the relationship between a disciple and the spiritual master, emphasizing the importance of satisfying the guru before asking for favors.

Om namo bhagavate vasudevaya. Mad-Bhagavatam, Canto 3, Chapter 22, Text 8. Sabhavandh trasneha pariklishthatmano mama srotam arhasi dhinasya shravitam kripaya mune. Translation in purport by S. D. V. Bhai Charanarvinda Bhaktivedanta Swamishila Prabhupada. Translation: O great sage, graciously be pleased to listen to the prayer of my humble self, for my mind is troubled by affection from my daughter. Purport by Srila Prabhupada: When a disciple is perfectly in consonance with the spiritual master having received his message and executed it perfectly and sincerely he has a right to ask a particular favor from the spiritual master.

---
### Guru-disciple relationship, Humility, Prayer
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YgfS_Iid9sM?t=61
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Swayambhuva Manu wanted to disclose his mind regarding the function he wanted to execute due to affection for his daughter."
**Summary:** The lecture continues with the purport, explaining that a pure devotee generally doesn't ask for favors, but if needed, it must be done after fully satisfying the spiritual master. Swayambhuva Manu's concern about his daughter's marriage and seeking Kardama Muni's attention are highlighted.

Generally, a pure devotee of the Lord or a pure disciple of a bona fide spiritual master does not ask any favor either from the Lord or the spiritual master. But even if there is a need to ask a favor from the spiritual master one cannot ask that favor without satisfying him fully. Swayambhuva Manu wanted to disclose his mind regarding the function he wanted to execute due to affection for his daughter. Thus ends the bhaktivedanta purport. Swayambhuva Manu is seeking the attention of Karadvama Muni to consider his prayer with regard to something that is disturbing his mind or troubled by the affection for my daughter.

---
### Humility, Respect, Grace
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YgfS_Iid9sM?t=125
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The guru is pleased with the disciple when he receives service from the disciple."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa elaborates on Swayambhuva Manu's concern about his daughter's marriage and his respectful approach to Kardama Muni. He emphasizes the humility in addressing the spiritual master and the importance of pleasing him, not as a transaction, but through grace.

Swayambhuva Manu was concerned about getting his daughter married and obviously he had in his mind Karadvama Muni. So he is saying here, O great sage, graciously be pleased to listen to the prayer of my humble self. This is how one addresses the spiritual master even if he has to, if he has to be addressed with respect to any request. See the humility. He is not just going and asking something. He is respecting him so much. He says, graciously be pleased. I am not saying that, I qualify that you be pleased with me. Graciously be pleased. Please be graceful. If someone asks somebody, please be pleased. I want you to be pleased with me. When is somebody pleased? When there is an exchange. The guru is pleased with the disciple when he receives service from the disciple. When he receives that, he is pleased with the disciple. But the disciple is thinking, just because I did service, even if he is pleased, it is because of his grace and not because of my contribution.

---
### Guru-disciple relationship, Grace, Humility
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YgfS_Iid9sM?t=271
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It is his free will to be pleased or not be pleased with me."
**Summary:** The lecture continues to emphasize that even with service, the guru's pleasure is due to his grace, not the disciple's merit. The relationship between guru and disciple is not about earning mercy, but about the guru's free will and grace.

Even if I do service, he need not be pleased. So he says, graciously be pleased. This is the way Swami Prabhupada Manu is addressing, the spiritual master would address, as a disciple will address a spiritual master. So everywhere, in the relationship between guru and disciple, this is a very key point. That, it is not a relationship of, you know, earning mercy or earning something by doing service to the spiritual master, you earn something. That is not the relationship. That is not the mood also. I will serve my spiritual master. It is his free will to be pleased or not be pleased with me. It is his own grace upon me, if he is pleased. That attitude is there.

---
### Humility, Devotional Service, Krishna Consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YgfS_Iid9sM?t=377
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The spiritual master is pleased with pure devotional service."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the disciple's humility stems from recognizing their inability to offer pure devotional service directly to Krishna. The spiritual master, being situated in absolute purity, offers the disciple's service to Krishna, as the spiritual master is pleased with pure devotional service.

Because the disciple doesn't think that he can actually, he has the quality, humility to do pure devotional service to the Lord. The spiritual master is pleased with pure devotional service. Because spiritual master, he is situated in a platform of absolute purity in his relationship with Krishna. So whatever service the living entity is offering to the spiritual master, naturally, the spiritual master doesn't want any service. He offers it to Krishna. Ekala, Ishvara, Krishna, Arasava, Brijya. Only Krishna is Ishvara. So a pure devotee of the Lord is offering pure devotional service. So when the disciple is offering service to spiritual master for Krishna to please him, the disciple knows that whatever I am offering is not worthy of going to Krishna. It is only by his grace it is reaching Krishna.

---
### Grace, Spiritual Master, Krishna Consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YgfS_Iid9sM?t=532
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If you are getting attached to Krishna, it is because of the mercy of the spiritual master."
**Summary:** The speaker uses an analogy of needing 100 units of humility for service to reach Krishna, and how the spiritual master compensates for the disciple's lack, offering it to the Lord. He emphasizes that advancement in Krishna consciousness, attachment to Krishna, and purification are all due to the spiritual master's mercy.

The disciple never thinks that I can serve the spiritual master directly. I can serve Krishna directly. I can serve my spiritual master directly but I cannot serve Krishna directly. I have no qualification to serve Krishna directly. Krishna is surrounded and served by pure devotees of the Lord. So therefore, if at all my service has to reach Krishna, it is reaching Krishna because of the spiritual master's grace upon us. Whatever is lacking, whatever is lacking from the disciple, whatever the disciple offers to Krishna, it is pure devotional service. Whatever is lacking, the spiritual master compensates it and then offers it to the Lord. This is his kindness. Suppose something, a quantity of let's say 100 is required. Just to give an example, 100 units of something is required. Say 100 units of humility is required for a service to go to Krishna. And we are offering service to the spiritual master. You may have one or you may have minus one or minus two or whatever. We offer that service to the spiritual master. The spiritual master, whether it is minus one or minus 100, he will make it plus 100 and offer it to Krishna because he sees something is there. So much is the transparent via media function of the spiritual master. So merciful he is. And if we are making advancement in Krishna consciousness, it is because of the mercy of the spiritual master. If you are getting attached to Krishna, it is because of the mercy of the spiritual master. If you are getting purified, it is due to the mercy of the spiritual master. That mercy, that is what graciously, the Lord is, the spiritual master, please be graciously be pleased with me. You cannot say, be pleased with me. Graciously be pleased with me. So sensitive. Because he recognizes that in terms of his absolute goal of going back to Krishna is most unqualified. So in that mood, he is praying, Swayambhu Ammanu is praying to him. That's this verse.

---
### Guru-disciple relationship, Instructions, Favor
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YgfS_Iid9sM?t=647
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The desiring candidate must find a bona fide spiritual master and then he must very eagerly receive instructions from him and execute them."
**Summary:** The speaker transitions to the previous translation and purport, which discusses how to accept and deal with a bona fide spiritual master, emphasizing the importance of finding one, eagerly receiving instructions, and executing them.

I thought I had a lot of questions were there the other day on the previous purport. I'll just read that once again. Previous translation. I have fortunately been instructed by you and thus great favor has been bestowed upon me. I thank God and I have listened with open ears to your pure words. Purport. Srila Rupa Goswami has given directions in his Bhakti Rasamrita Sindhu on how to accept a bona fide spiritual master and how to deal with him. First, the desiring candidate must find a bona fide spiritual master and then he must very eagerly receive instructions from him and execute them.

### Krishna Consciousness, Service, Spiritual Master
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YgfS_Iid9sM?t=690
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It is the function of a saintly person to invoke Krishna Consciousness in every forgetful man."
**Summary:** The lecture continues with the purport, highlighting the reciprocal service between the guru and disciple, the guru's desire to elevate the disciple, and the importance of receiving the transcendental message through oral reception. It clarifies that the spiritual master knows the nature of the disciple and instructs them accordingly in Krishna Consciousness.

This is reciprocal service. A bona fide spiritual master, a saintly person always desires to elevate a common man who comes to him because everyone is under the delusion of Maya and is forgetful of his prime duty, Krishna Consciousness. Saintly person always desires that everyone becomes a saintly person. It is the function of a saintly person to invoke Krishna Consciousness in every forgetful man. Manu said that he was advised and instructed by Kardama Muni he was very much favored. He considered himself lucky to receive the message, by oral reception. It is especially mentioned here that one should be very inquisitive to hear with open ears from the authorized source of a bona fide spiritual master. How is one to receive? One should receive the transcendental message by oral reception. The word Karna Randhrahey means through the holes of the ears. The favor of the spiritual master is not received through any other part of the body but the ears. This does not mean however that the spiritual master gives a particular type of mantra through the ears in exchange for some dollars and if the man meditates on that he achieves perfection and becomes God within six months. Such reception through the ears is bogus. The real fact is that a bona fide spiritual master knows the nature of a particular man and what sort of duties he can perform in Krishna Consciousness and he instructs him in that way. He instructs him through the ear not privately but publicly. You are fit for such and such a work in Krishna Consciousness you can act in this way. One person is advised to work in Krishna Consciousness by working in the deity's room another is advised to act in Krishna Consciousness by performing editorial work another is advised to do preaching work another is advised to carry out Krishna Consciousness in the cooking department. There are different departments of activities in Krishna Consciousness and the spiritual master knowing the particular ability of a particular man trains him in such a way that he becomes perfect.

---
### Service, Instructions, Perfection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YgfS_Iid9sM?t=817
**Verse References:** BG 18.45
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The faithful execution of the instruction which he receives from the spiritual master is the only duty of a disciple and that will bring him perfection."
**Summary:** The speaker continues reading the purport, explaining that one can attain the highest perfection by offering service according to their ability, as instructed by the spiritual master. The combination of the spiritual master's instruction and the disciple's faithful execution makes the process perfect.

Bhagavad Gita makes it clear that one can attain the highest perfection in spiritual life simply by offering service according to his ability just as Arjuna served Krishna by his ability in the military art. Arjuna offered his service fully as a military man and he became perfect. Similarly an artist can attain perfection simply by performing artistic work on the direction of the spiritual master. If one is a literary man he can write articles and poetry for the service of God under the direction of the spiritual master. One has to receive the message of the spiritual master regarding how to act in one's capacity for the spiritual master is expert in giving such instructions. This combination the instruction of the spiritual master and the fateful execution of the instruction by the disciple makes the entire process perfect. Srila Vishnu Achakravati Thakur describes in his explanation of his verse in Bhagavad Gita that one who wants to be certain to achieve spiritual success must take the instructions from the spiritual master as to what his particular function is. He should faithfully try to execute the particular instruction and should consider that his life and soul. The faithful execution of the instruction which he receives from the spiritual master is the only duty of a disciple and that will bring him perfection. One should be very careful to receive the instruction and execute it faithfully that will make one's life successful.

---

### Faith, Process, Spiritual Master
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YgfS_Iid9sM?t=1024
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This process is so powerful having the right faith in the process itself that the presence of Srila Prabhupada is not only right now in front of me and you."
**Summary:** The devotee continues sharing their experience, noting that while they could write to Srila Prabhupada, they often didn't send the letters, respecting his time for service. They observed the answers to their questions emerging through the process of devotional service, highlighting the power of faith in the process and the continued presence of Srila Prabhupada.

was one element that solved or at least for several of us that impediment let's call it or distance it was that we could write Srila Prabhupada any moment but we knew also that there were hundreds of letters every day. In my memory I can still continue writing but never delivered it. These letters were accumulated and were checked by our own in the course of time because we were consider about his time for the service and then in due course of time we could see the letters being answered through the process of devotional service. And it's a very good practice even I regret I cannot do it now but I do in another way but it's very scientific and practical not having envy but most of them became too familiar. So this process is so powerful having the right faith in the process itself that the presence of Srila Prabhupada is not only right now in front of me and you.

## How to Become Free from Past Karmas? | Sri Madhu Pandit Dasa | SB 3.22.12 | 14.08.2024
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

### sense gratification, karma, body
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lEvontw9tWU?t=32
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "We have taken this body in this material world for enjoying, so therefore the Lord has mercifully given this body for just like every karma He gives, He gives us a body according to our karma."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa explains that everyone in material life desires sense gratification and that the body is given according to one's karma, with a fixed potential for Sukha and Dukha (happiness and distress) that unfolds over time.

To deny an offering that has come of itself is not commendable, even for one absolutely free from all attachment, much less one addicted to sensual pleasure. In material life, everyone is desirous of sense gratification. How we manage the forces of the mind, the body, and the senses that rise up? We are given guidelines by Krishna in Bhagavad Gita, by our Acharyas, by the Purvacharyas. How you conduct your life in a manner in which you handle these urges? We handle these natural, bodily, mental urges. We cannot deny that these urges are not there. Nobody can deny. We have taken this body in this material world for enjoying, so therefore the Lord has mercifully given this body. Just like every karma He gives, He gives us a body according to our karma. This body has got a certain value. It has got a certain potential of Sukha and Dukha, which is unveiled, it's a function of time. Tomorrow on Sukha Dukha, day after tomorrow on Sukha Dukha, next year, 100 years from now, 10 years from now, 15 years from now, it's a function of Kala, Sukha Dukha. But Prabhupada says and the Shastra says that Sukha Dukha is a function of time. It's a function of time. So Sukha Dukha, material Sukha Dukha for the body and the mind, is fixed for everybody when one takes birth. It is fixed. Sukha and Dukha is an experience. So Sukha Dukha is fixed. Not that this particular Sukha is fixed, this particular Dukha is fixed. Not that the full chart of where all you will get Sukha, where all you will get Dukha is fixed. That is not what it means. Then you have no choice. Your Sukha Dukha is fixed means, suppose there is a currency. Currency is used to buy something, no? Suppose there is a currency called Sukha currency. And there is another currency called Dukha currency. When you are born, you are given a purpose. It is not called Sukha currency. ये यह Kook LDK हो रहेंग्गी, जिसी पास आप आज 60 शत्riage लिया हैं, तारे से 60-60वें क Australia पाका लगा जाता है.

---
### devotional service, austerity, karma
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lEvontw9tWU?t=812
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If the devotee is having the, you know, has determined to work hard for Prabhupada, take trouble for Prabhupada, sweat for Prabhupada, take tension for Prabhupada, take anxiety for Prabhupada, please understand that you are burning your Dukkha currency."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that devotees use their 'currency' for Krishna's service, transcending the duality of the material world. Taking trouble for Prabhupada burns 'Dukkha currency,' while avoiding such trouble leads to suffering unrelated to Krishna's service.

What the devotee does is what? He uses that currency for Krishna's service. The duality of the material world is felt in terms of heat and cold, misery and happiness. A Krishna conscious person is above duality because he does not hesitate to act in any way for the satisfaction of Krishna. Just see, when you take trouble to serve Prabhupada and Krishna, bodily, mental struggle you take against your wish. Tapasya means against your default mode. Now your present wish is to serve Prabhupada and Krishna. But there is a default mode, lazy fellow, all these kind of things, don't want to do, live happily. All this kind of philosophy, you know, concocted, karmi philosophy will be there. If the devotee is having the, you know, has determined to work hard for Prabhupada, take trouble for Prabhupada, sweat for Prabhupada, take tension for Prabhupada, take anxiety for Prabhupada, please understand that you are burning your Dukkha currency. If you don't take trouble for Prabhupada, if you don't take tension for Prabhupada, if you don't take anxiety for Prabhupada, Dukkha will come to you some other way, which is not service of Krishna. It may even stop service of Krishna, right? If you become very sick, then you will not be able to even do service, isn't it? Now I am talking about how devotees manage pleasure and pain.

---
### austerity, devotional service, struggle
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lEvontw9tWU?t=927
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In Krishna consciousness after you have come, if you don't struggle for your spiritual, for service, if you don't struggle to control your senses, then you will be made to struggle for material existence."
**Summary:** The first secret to managing pleasure and pain is to take authorized austerities voluntarily, such as preaching Krishna consciousness, raising funds, and convincing others. If one doesn't struggle for spiritual advancement and sense control, they will be forced to struggle for material existence, which is ultimately useless.

In Kota, which is there as per law of karma, management of pleasure and pain. So secret number one is, take authorized austerities. One of the things that disturb ones most in life, for everybody is what? When problems come. Isn't it? Nobody likes problems. So better we take voluntarily problems. In this material world, to preach Krishna consciousness is very problematic. To raise funds is very problematic. To convince somebody is very problematic. You need patience, tolerance, be behind him, be behind him, be behind him. Be behind him. You have to put chest. To make one devotee, right? Or you take up a project, you have to be behind it. Every problems that the karmas have in building a project, the devotee will also have to face. So all these things when we do for Krishna, it has got very high value to make our devotional life peaceful. Therefore austerity should be taken. So many times in different classes I have told, in Krishna consciousness after you have come, if you don't struggle for your spiritual, for service, if you don't struggle to control your senses, then you will be made to struggle for material existence. Struggle will not be, that struggle will not be taken away. When you struggle for material existence, it is useless. It doesn't help you in any way. It's all temporary. When you struggle for Krishna consciousness, it is giving you eternal benefit. So, where do you, suppose you say, anyway you have to struggle. If you struggle here, you will get something for this life. If you struggle here, you will get something eternal for your soul. Which will you choose?

---
### austerity, sadhana, spiritual life
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lEvontw9tWU?t=1077
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "We should, body and mind should have austerity every day. Something you don't like, you will have to do every day."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of daily austerity for the body and mind, focusing on activities that one's 'default system' resists, such as completing 16 rounds of chanting. He cautions against generalized austerities not aligned with pleasing Vishnu, distinguishing them from those performed for karmic benefits or pleasing other deities.

This is intelligent management. Therefore, we should struggle. We should, body and mind should have austerity every day. Something you don't like, you will have to do every day. Sadhana, something your default system does not want. Maybe something got trained and still there is some small percentage it doesn't want. Take that percentage and hit it. Go, do it. Do it. Maybe you don't want to be in one shot and finish your 16 rounds. After few rounds your mind will say something else. No. Take austerity. Do it. Finish it. There are hundreds of ways in which in our personal life, in devotees personal life, in Krishna consciousness movement that Prabhupada has given, there are hundreds of ways of picking up austerities, bona fide austerity. We are not generalizing the principle of austerity. Okay, I will do one thing. Today, you know, I will, after morning program, you know, suddenly you will see one brahmachari in the middle of the brahmachari ashram, standing on one leg. Prabhupada, today I have decided, 10 hours I am going to stand like this. Why? No, tapasya. So that I can burn off my, you know, papa bijas. No, we are talking about tapasya which will give us eternal benefit. Oh, I have to do tapasya Prabhu, you told me. No, Mondays I will fast and Fridays I will fast. No. These are all good for karmas. Yes, it helps them, encourages them. Every kind of austerity. Austerity for pleasing Shiva, austerity for pleasing Ganesha. Monday for Shiva I will fast. Thursday I will fast for Ganesha. All this they have. Let them do. But we want to do those austerities which are pleasing to Vishnu, to the Lord. So we have to be very careful in simply, that is another extreme.

### austerity, spiritual master, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lEvontw9tWU?t=1209
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Bhakti Siddhanta Saraswati Thakur used to blast any disciple if he goes to Radha Kunda and sits. He said, you are not qualified even to dip in Radha Kunda."
**Summary:** The speaker cautions against extreme austerity, emphasizing the importance of following the guidance of Srila Prabhupada. He uses the example of chanting 64 rounds at Radha Kunda, explaining that while it seems like a good austerity, it may not be appropriate for everyone and that Bhakti Siddhanta Saraswati Thakur discouraged unqualified disciples from even dipping in Radha Kunda.

Some devotees go into this whole austerity mode and all this kind of things. So there are bona fide austerities. Within even those bona fide austerities, Srila Prabhupada has given us, even within these bona fide austerities, again a set of austerities. Why he has given a set of austerities? Just suppose somebody says, oh I want to go to Radha Kunda and chant every day 64 rounds. See, it is a, how can you say no, it is not a good austerity. Now you have to again see what our bona fide, our spiritual master has told us. Stand 16 rounds nicely, probably another 10 lives. Then one day you will go to Radha Kunda. There is a stage for everything. Bhakti Siddhanta Saraswati Thakur used to blast any disciple if he goes to Radha Kunda and sits. He said, you are not qualified even to dip in Radha Kunda. Even Prabhupada, I think only once or something he went dip, but afterwards he stopped. Because all the foreign disciples started swimming and everything in Radha Kunda. So Prabhupada stopped that. That is why as a principle we are not encouraging everybody, oh go to. Now ISKCON has become big, lot of congregation, it has become a fad. Go, Radha Kunda, dip, dip, dip, Radha Kunda. It is a very sanctified place. Radha Kunda is your, you know, you can't experience Radha Kunda unless you are a very elevated devotee. It is not that gross eyes what you see that Kunda. You can make lot of offences. Unless you are free from, you know, lust, you cannot sit for bhajan like that. Niranthara Bhajan, continuing to chant 64 times. Or like Haridas Thakur. So austerity also is given to us by Srila Prabhupada.

---
### austerity, chanting, holy name
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lEvontw9tWU?t=1347
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In fact, the greatest tapasya that one can do in this age is to hear the holy name of the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that the austerity given by Srila Prabhupada, such as chanting 16 rounds nicely, is the path to qualification. He highlights that the greatest tapasya in this age is hearing the holy name of the Lord, which is easier than other austerities but yields incomparable results.

And that austerity, for instance, when you chant your 16 rounds nicely, hearing, without offences, then you get qualified to chant your 17th round. Otherwise put all your efforts in chanting 16 rounds nicely. Austerity. Plenty of austerity. Whole life austerity is there. In fact, the greatest tapasya that one can do in this age is to hear the holy name of the Lord. See, tapasya can be known, can be understood by two means. One is how difficult it is. Another is what is the result of it. See, don't mistake, always tapasya means pain. It's not so. There are authorized tapasyas. Each tapasya has a pain and a result. If you take chanting Hare Krishna Mahamantra, the hearing of Hare Krishna Mahamantra, definitely is not so difficult as standing on one leg for 10 hours. Right? Much easier. But the result of that one hearing of Hare Krishna cannot be compared to 100 years of standing on one leg. So that's what you have to understand. Don't think that tapasya only means, you know, tapasya means authorized tapasas. Authorized tapasas, authorized austerity, because there is some, definitely some tapas is there in hearing because mind goes here and there. But it is not, it is, it is, it is easy, easier than many other tapasyas. But the value of this tapasya is something which is, what is revealed in the scriptures. Discounted tapasya for Kali Yuga. Just simply you hear then you get, you know, the results of so many Ashwamedha Yajna, Shastras. Even then you cannot equal to this tapasya of hearing this Hare Krishna Mahamantra. So, we have to be very careful in understanding purport of Prabhupada, understanding these verses.

---
### guidance, interpretation, sense gratification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lEvontw9tWU?t=1517
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Otherwise from Bhagavatam, whatever you want, you can get. All kinds of stories are there. Whatever you want to justify, you will get it."
**Summary:** The speaker stresses the necessity of an Acharya's guidance to avoid misinterpreting scriptures like the Bhagavatam, which contains diverse stories that can be used to justify various viewpoints. He emphasizes understanding verses in the context of Prabhupada's teachings, particularly regarding sense gratification.

That is why an Acharya's guidance is required. Otherwise you will misunderstand. Otherwise from Bhagavatam, whatever you want, you can get. All kinds of stories are there. Whatever you want to justify, you will get it. Sometimes devotees, sometimes devotees also argue, no, no, actually Bhagavatam is for Grahasthas. And the Paramahamsas say, no, no, Bhagavatam is a Paramahamsa Shastra. It is for Paramahamsas. The fact is that for those who want to practice Vaishnavism, it is available for everybody, irrespective of any Ashrama. Bhagavatam caters to Grahasthas. Bhagavatam caters to Paramahamsas also. So such an all-encompassing literature is Srimad Bhagavatam. Therefore we need guidance. How it is applicable to each. And that is how I explain now. For a devotee, you have dedicated your life to Krishna consciousness, to preach Krishna consciousness. That is your focus now. Now how do we understand this verse? You are not in material life. So how do you understand this verse? We have to understand this in context with everything else that Prabhupada has told about sense gratification. Now just take this one thing about sense gratification. Now that is one way of, one thing about taking, taking austerities, taking pressure, taking tension, to work for Srila Prabhupada, to produce results for Srila Prabhupada, etc. That is one way I said it will automatically burn out the currency of Dukha.
