### identified pleasure, soul happiness, detachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2tyr7PYsMp8?t=1312
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "You are taking feel good and feel bad from something which you have really no connection at all."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of watching a cricket match to illustrate the concept of identified pleasure, where one experiences emotions based on something external and unrelated to their real life.

Just like how you identify with the pleasure. Example I had given about a cricket game. You have your real problems at home, your real wife, your real love, your real power, how much money you have and all those real, all rasas are there, reality is there in your house or in your real life. You buy a ticket for a match. You go and sit in the gallery. You buy a ticket for a match. You go and sit in the gallery. You buy a ticket for a match. You go and sit in the gallery. You buy a ticket for a match. You go and sit in the gallery. You go and sit in the gallery. You have forgotten all these, your real problems. Whole day cricket match you are watching. India and Pakistan is playing the test match. And your emotions are moving from happiness to distress, sukha to dukkha, hope, all hope, excitement, everything. Those, you paid money for that. You get excited. When your favourite batsman is hitting a six, you get excited. Or your famous, your admired bowler is bowling and takes a wicket, then you are very thrilled. Now you are identified with the Indian team. So anything good happens to the Indian team is your happiness. It's called identified happiness. Suddenly, you know, any one member of the Indian team falls injured. Why? Because the Indian team falls injured. You feel very bad. So, that means, you know, you are taking feel good and feel bad from something which you have really no connection at all. Arrey cricket match to chal raya hai, it has nothing to do with my real life problems. I am sitting here and going into rasas, ups and downs, hope, you know, excitement, success. Success, failure, sukha, dukkha, completely forgotten your house, you are in another world. So this is called identified pleasure. So in the same way, this material body is outside me. This material body, sukha, dukkha is all identified pleasures. Identified pleasures will never give soul happiness. Because it is identified pleasure. It is identified pleasure. It is identified.

## Krishna will run your day if you do this! | @madhupanditdasaofficial
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

### chanting, mindfulness, Krishna consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YPsji1KqC1I?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna will run your day."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa explains the importance of chanting the Hare Krishna Maha Mantra with a fresh mind in the early morning to allow Krishna to guide one's day and life.

Hare Krishna Prabhu, when should one chant Hare Krishna Maha Mantra? The best time to chant Hare Krishna Maha Mantra is after a long sleep when you wake up because your mind is fresh. Reason being, a fresh mind is required. What does a fresh mind mean? Once you start your day, so many thoughts and this and that, it keeps like a guna, kala, karma will be churning inside you and something and all will be coming in your mind. In the midst of that, Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare. So one side you are mechanically chanting, the other side all this thinking is going on. So that is not a quality chanting. You are not touching unless the shabda that you chant goes inside through your ears into the heart. Effectiveness is very less. As I said, it is a science. If you take a piece of iron and keep it near the fire, you will get a little heat. Put it inside the fire, red hot, little far away, little heat. So you want a little heat from Hare Krishna or you want a full fire? Full fire means attentively, early morning, before your day's prarada karma starts, finish your association. Finish your association with Krishna. Krishna will run your day. Your life will become smooth. He will run your day. Externally there may be so many difficulties. External difficulty is not the measure of difficulty. What you are feeling about that difficulty is difficulty.

---

Just like so many people saw 5000 years back when Krishna came, as him being one among so many persons who have moved along. Avatara means himself. More or less, we will treat the holy name like an impersonal sound. Once in a while something you may remember, 'Oh Krishna is this Krishna himself.' That is not enough. Once in a while is not enough. Throughout each bead, Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare. It is Krishna himself that obviously you don't have to repeat again and again if it is embedded in your subconscious. Hare Krishna Mahamantra. Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, Chaitanya Charitamrita reveals that Krishna Naama is a Surya of Satchitananda. Surya of Satchitananda means son of Satchitananda. What is normal son? It's a son of light and heat. Now we are talking about son of Satchitananda. Light and heat is energy. So son of Satchitananda means Sat, Chit and Ananda energy. Existence, knowledge and bliss. It's an infinite source. Because that is Krishna. Krishna is Satchitananda Vigraha, Anadhi Radhi Govinda, Sarva Karna Karna. So Krishna Naama

### Satchitananda, soul, material world
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/UGmkk8Lsf7g?t=374
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Soul's nature is to seek Sat. Chit, pure knowledge, pure understanding, not ignorance. And Ananda means bliss, love, loving exchanges, exchanges of loving, exchanges of pleasure."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the concept of Satchitananda, explaining that the soul naturally seeks eternity, knowledge, and bliss, but is always incomplete and hungry in the material world due to the influence of Maya energy.

is a Surya of Satchitananda. Krishna is Surya of Satchitananda. Krishna Naama is also Surya of Satchitananda. All that our soul seeks in this world or in the spiritual world is to be immersed in this Sat, Chit and Ananda Shakti. That is our natural atmosphere. Whereas now the soul is in the atmosphere of Asat Shakti, Achit Shakti and Nirananda Shakti. This is Maya energy. There is internal energy, spiritual energy of the Lord. Satchitananda energy. Sat means eternity. We all want eternal life. We all want eternal relationship. We all want eternal love. There is no doubt about it. But in the bodily life we never get any of these things. So soul's nature is to seek Sat. Chit, pure knowledge, pure understanding, not ignorance. And Ananda means bliss, love, loving exchanges, exchanges of loving, exchanges of pleasure. These are all very natural for the soul. But somehow or the other even though very natural for us in this material world we are always defeated in our efforts for Satchitananda. So soul is always incomplete in this material world. Therefore it is eternally it is hungry.

### Satchitananda, analogy, chanting
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/UGmkk8Lsf7g?t=488
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Just like our soul is like the piece of iron. We have to put in the fire of Satchitananda."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of iron in fire to explain how chanting Hare Krishna influences and affects us, comparing the soul to a piece of iron that needs to be placed in the fire of Satchitananda.

Never satisfied. So Krishna Nama is Surya of Satchitananda. The Surya of Satchitananda. And now Prabhupada explains how this works. When you put a piece of iron in the fire, the heat moves from the fire to the piece of iron. And gradually the piece of iron gets heated little by little. The longer it stays it gets heated and becomes red hot. The iron gets the quality of fire. At least 5 to 6 times Prabhupada has explained this phenomenon of how chanting works. So it is very important for us to remember this analogy of Prabhupada. Because from this analogy many things we understand about the phenomena of how actually Hare Krishna chanting influences us, it affects us. Just like our soul is like the piece of iron. We have to put in the fire of Satchitananda.

Sat Shakti Chitti Shakti Ananda Shakti Nam Nam Akari Boudha Nija Sarva Shakti Tatrarthita Niyamita Smaranena Kalaha. That Lord Chaitanya says the Lord has unlimited names and in each of His names He has invested all His Shakti. He has invested all this Shakti. Another example that Prabhupada gives is like the electrical wire. When electricity is not flowing through the wire it is a dead wire. When electricity flows through it you touch it, you get a shock. So in the same way we have to understand the holy name the shabda, avatara means Nam Nam Akari Boudha Nija Sarva Shakti. Just like electricity is a Shakti Krishna Satchitananda Shakti is flowing in the sound you and me utter. Very important. In the sound that me and you utter all His energy is flowing.

## Married Life in Krishna Consciousness Movement | Sri Madhu Pandit Dasa | SB 3.22.11 | 13.08.2024
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

### marriage, household duties, spiritual advancement
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/nupyoz3cd-c?t=43
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The household duty of a man is not to satisfy his sense gratification but to remain with a wife and children and at the same time attain Advancement in Spiritual Life."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the role of a wife in household duties and how a householder should aim for spiritual advancement alongside family life.

Translation: Therefore, please accept her, O chief of the brahmanas, for I offer her with faith, and she is in every respect fit to be your wife and take charge of your household duties. Purport by Shrila Prabhupada: The words 'in household duties'—another word is also used here: 'सर्वात्म नानु रूपाम'—the purport is that a wife should not only be equal to her husband in age, character, and qualities but must be helpful to him in his household duties. The household duty of a man is not to satisfy his sense gratification but to remain with a wife and children and at the same time attain Advancement in Spiritual Life. One who does not do so is not a householder but a ग्रह में दी. Two words are used in Sanskrit literature: one is ग्रहस्था and the other is ग्रह में दी. The difference between ग्रह में दी and ग्रहस्था is that...

युगा धर्मा in युगा धर्मा simply by the यद्ञ of chanting this Hare Krishna Mahamantra. ### ashrama, chanting, protection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/nupyoz3cd-c?t=627
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Even though we know that there is nothing, there is no perfect ashrama that can be followed in the present environment, but those who chant the Hare Krishna Maha Mantra, those who serve the Hare Krishna Maha Mantra, because the spiritual potency comes from the Hare Krishna Maha Mantra, one will be able to, in whatever ashram he is there, he will be able to easily follow those principles."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the difficulty of following ashrama principles perfectly in the current environment but highlights the power of chanting the Hare Krishna Maha Mantra for protection and spiritual progress.

At the same time for the outside world we have to set an example, so we have to be very very careful. Even though we know that there is nothing, there is no perfect ashrama that can be followed in the present environment. But those who chant the Hare Krishna Maha Mantra, those who serve the Hare Krishna Maha Mantra, because the spiritual potency comes from the Hare Krishna Maha Mantra, one will be able to, in whatever ashram he is there, he will be able to easily follow those principles. Even if the environment is not there, in a sense he will be able to follow all those things. There is no difficulty for a devotee who is chanting sixteen rounds to follow the principles of any of these ashramas. Not at all difficult. Because the holy name is so powerful. Chanting is so powerful. It is not that you chant Hare Krishna sixteen rounds and your material life is in a mess. It will not be like that. The chanting of Hare Krishna is so powerful that it will in the midst of all this, just like look at the devotees in Hare Krishna hill or any of our temples. The outside world is such a big mess. And here devotees have no such thing. They are simply chanting, dancing, doing their service. Anxieties of struggle for existence is being spared for everybody in this Hare Krishna hill. How is it possible in Kali Yuga? Where will you get this luxury in Kali Yuga? Where you don't have to think about your eating, sleeping, waiting, defending. You don't have to think. You only think of serving Prabhupada, serving Prabhupada. Your whole life gets going. Nowhere. Because material energy does not allow that. Maya does not allow that. Because maya cannot enter when there is Sankirtana. Where there is chanting, maya cannot enter. So we keep ourselves under protection of the divine energy of the deities, of the holy name of Guru's mercy. By all these things we are actually continuously on a day to day basis we are protected and that is why we are in this particular situation. That is why we are in this particular situation.

That is only theory. I have already got it. No, I am here. But you should forget. You should not forget that if not for this. If not for this. I will be no different from millions of Karmas outside who are just Grahamedhis. ### perception, amplification, analogy

**Verse References:** —

**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of diabetes and insulin to explain how neglecting one's inner perception weakens it, while repeatedly indulging in a perception amplifies it, emphasizing that this amplification happens internally.

Terminology also, disuse atrophy they say. If something is not used regularly, it will die. So same thing, think it's just like in diabetics, they say, if you don't use your pancreas, instead you artificially put insulin from outside, what you are doing is you are killing it more. So finally, your increase of insulin has to go on, your dose of insulin go on increasing, they say. Same thing, think like pancreas, you got perception in your mind, it's all in your subtle body, no? If you go on using it, it becomes stronger and stronger. If you go on seeing female as enjoyable, enjoyable, enjoyable, it becomes empowered, empowered, empowered and one day you will feel, so enjoyable it is. It's all in the head. Amplify, amplify, amplify, amplify, amplify, amplify. Nothing is there outside that person who is there, maybe it's, let's say every day you say, see one female. No amplification happening in her, but you, day by day, amplification, amplification, amplification, amplification. Nothing is that, female is just walking up and down. In your head, it is amplifying. So default perception, any perception, good perception or bad perception, it is amplifying.

### perception, brahmacharya, bhagavatam
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/HxXjfqk32To?t=442
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees
**Key Quote:** "The Brahmachari's method of removing what perception that you have got before joining is gone, of female, it will all just fuse off if you don't touch it for many years."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by stating that perceptions are strengthened by repeated contemplation and application, and weakened by neglect, explaining that Brahmacharis can overcome previous perceptions by avoiding them and engaging with authorized descriptions in the Bhagavatam.

By repeated contemplation and repeated application, it gets strengthened. By starving it, it becomes weak and dead. So don't have to fear. The Brahmachari's method of removing what perception that you have got before joining is gone, of female, it will all just fuse off if you don't touch it for many years. That is the principle of Brahmacharya. If at all you touch it, you hear like this thin waste daughters of, a very purifying Shabda it is. Your attraction to thin waste daughters will go away, Their description of beautiful breasts in Bhagavatam, you hear that, you hear Shabda, it will go away. Female beauty is described in Bhagavatam. You hear that story of Puranjana all that thing you hear? The authorized way for you, for a Brahmachari to touch these concepts is through Bhagavatam.

## Self-certification is Not a Spiritual Qualification | Sri Madhu Pandit Dasa | SB 3.22.5 | 24.07.2024
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

### association, saintly person, liberation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GFHkEL1Sipk?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Somehow or other, if one meets a saintly person completely engaged in transcendental duties and achieves his favor, then one's life mission becomes complete."
**Summary:** This segment focuses on the importance of associating with saintly persons and how even a moment of association can lead to liberation and the fulfillment of one's life mission, as exemplified by Srila Prabhupada's experience with his guru.

Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya. Reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, Canto 3, Chapter 22, Text No. 5. Tavasandarshanadeva Chinname Sarvasamchayaha Yatsvayam Bhagavanpretya Dharmamaharirakshisho. Translation and purport by His Divine Grace Shri La Prabhupada. Now I have resolved all my doubts simply by meeting you, for your lordship has very kindly and clearly explained the duty of a king who desires to protect his subjects. Purport by Shri La Prabhupada. Manu described herewith the result of seeing a great saintly person. Lord Chaitanya says that one should always try to associate with saintly persons, because if one establishes a proper association with a saintly person, even for a moment, one attains all perfection. Somehow or other, if one meets a saintly person and achieves his favor, then the entire mission of one's human life is fulfilled. In our personal experience, we have actual proof of this statement of Manu. Once, we had the opportunity to meet Vishnupat Shri Srimad Bhaktisiddhanta Saraswati Goswami Maharaj and on first sight, he requested this humble self to preach his message in the western countries. There was no preparation for this but somehow or other, he desired it and by his grace, we are now engaged in executing his order which has given us a transcendental occupation and has saved and liberated us from the occupation of material activities. Thus, it is actually a fact that if one meets a saintly person completely engaged in transcendental duties and achieves his favor, then one's life mission becomes complete. What is not possible to achieve in thousands of lives can be achieved in one moment if there is an opportunity to meet a saintly person. It is therefore enjoined in Vedic literature that one should always try to associate with saintly persons and try to dissociate oneself from the common man because by one word of a saintly person, one can be liberated from material entanglement. A saintly person has the power because of his spiritual advancement to give immediate liberation to the conditioned soul. Here Manu admits that all his doubts are now over because Karadhamma has very kindly described the different duties of individual souls, thus ends the Bhaktivedanta purport.

---
### duty, respect, brahmana, kshatriya
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GFHkEL1Sipk?t=233
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The brahmana is always ready to give advice as per his duty to the kshatriya, to keep him aware of his responsibilities, as a servant of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, to protect all the other three classes."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the roles and relationship between a brahmana and a kshatriya, emphasizing the brahmana's duty to advise the kshatriya and the kshatriya's duty to respect the brahmana as a source of the Lord's mercy.

There is no moment of rest. Manu is glorifying the words of Karadhamma muni. He is glorifying Devi. He is glorifying Karadhammaman. The king, he is glorifying the power of association through the words of a brahmana, like Karadhamani. And this is the natural relationship between a kshatriya king and the brahmana. The brahmana is always ready to give advice as per his duty to the kshatriya, to keep him aware of his responsibilities, as a servant of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, to protect all the other three classes. And it is the kshatriya's duty to give very special respect to brahmana, as a person from whom the Lord's mercy is received. Those brahmanas who are in the kshatriya, who are situated in transcendental platform, they represent the vision, the will of the Lord, with respect to mankind. And those things are, according to those advices, the kings rule the country or rule the planet.

### association, mercy, spiritual advancement
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GFHkEL1Sipk?t=341
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Spiritual growth happens by constant shower of mercy of the spiritual master, mercy of the deities, mercy of prasadam, mercy of great devotees like Tulsimaharani, mercy of holy rivers, mercy of holy dham."
**Summary:** This segment contrasts the material paradigm of achieving through hard work with the spiritual process of advancement through receiving mercy from various transcendental sources, such as the spiritual master, deities, prasadam, and holy places.

Here, Srila Prabhupada in the Bhaktivedanta purports, he explains the principle of association with great saintly persons. Even a moment of association, even a moment of association, sadhu-sangha, one can attain all perfection by achieving his favor. This is a very important principle in the process of spiritual advancement for every devotee. Normally, everything in this world is achieved. That's the paradigm of everyone, that I work hard and I have to achieve. I work hard and I have to be successful. So when we come to spiritual life also, naturally the tendency is to think that I work very hard for my sadhana, etc. and then result is guaranteed. In other words, it's like a making spiritual advance, turning spiritual advancement to simply following some process. In the material world, it's like that. You go through a process, the result has to be there in the end. So we have to be very careful in our mind to change this paradigm. This paradigm has been ingrained into us through our education system. We are all victim of this. By default, our thinking is like this. It is only when you understand the principle of spiritual flow of mercy, how actually spiritual growth happens, not like how you learn in school. Spiritual growth happens by constant shower of mercy of the spiritual master, mercy of the deities, mercy of prasadam, mercy of great devotees like Tulsimaharani, mercy of holy rivers, mercy of holy dham. What is this mercy, mercy, mercy? How does it? How does it impact me? How does it impact a sadhaka?

---
### descending process, realization, mercy, begging
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GFHkEL1Sipk?t=528
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Spiritual advancement means receiving key understandings through the descending process."
**Summary:** This segment explains that spiritual advancement is a descending process of receiving mercy and realization, emphasizing the importance of a begging and anticipating mode for a devotee to receive the Lord's mercy in various forms.

Spiritual advancement means receiving key understandings through the descending process. At least we should, even if our mind is stuck in a process-oriented thinking, turn it into it is a descending process. That today if I have some advancement, it's not because I read Prabhupada's books alone, not because I thought a lot and I understood. It is because I read Prabhupada's books and Prabhupada gave his mercy. It is because I thought about all this and Krishna in the heart gave me the realization. Realization and understanding. So everything that we have today is a combination of whatever we desire, whatever is our attitude towards the desire and what we have received from a transcendental source. All that we receive from a transcendental source, it is not temporary. It is permanent. It stays with us. In the material world, so many energies come and go. You say spiritual, if you do your PhD this life, next life that knowledge is gone. But if there is a mercy of from the transcendental source of enlightenment, that will remain with the soul even though it may get covered, but doesn't matter. It remains. It's an eternal asset for the soul. So our business in spiritual life will be successful if we constantly collect mercy. Of course you cannot go on like plucking a flower, you cannot collect mercy. Mercy has to be begged. So the begging mode, anticipating mode is extremely important for a devotee. The devotee is constantly in the mode of begging and anticipating the mercy of the Lord in various forms.

### sadhana, mercy, transcendental process
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GFHkEL1Sipk?t=675
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Every day the mercy coming out of the altar to each devotee is different. Externally it is same, internally it is different."
**Summary:** This segment discusses how even seemingly repetitive sadhana practices are unique experiences of receiving mercy, emphasizing the importance of being sensitive to the transcendental aspects of these processes rather than viewing them as mere routines.

Just like we can say, okay, every day we are coming for sadhana, it is a process. 4.15 you have to be there, then samsara dava has to be sung, then Tulsi has to be, Tulsi Maharani's song has to be sung, she has to be glorified, then you go, Prabhupada Puja, etc., etc., all these processes you have to follow. So in the neophyte, a devotee thinks, this is, this is, okay, I have to go for this, I have to go for this, this is a process. Even though externally every day it appears to be the same thing, 4.15 Mangalarati, 4.15 same song, every day same song, don't you get bored? 30, 40 years same song, samsara dava, samsara dava, samsara dava, you don't get bored. The fact is that we don't get bored. Why? Every day the mercy coming out of the altar to each devotee is different. Externally it is same, internally it is different. What plays in the devotee's heart, his understanding, his realization, his situation for that day, his faith, his prayers, all are different. So you don't get bored. That is a variety. So every day is different. Every day Mangalarati is different. Every day Guru Puja is different. Every day Tulasi Aarti is different. It is different. If you go into an ascending process, process paradigm, then you will feel, oh what is this, what is this, the same thing, same thing, because you are only seeing that. You are not seeing the difference, you are not sensitive to the what is not visibly seeable things that happen from the Lord's mercy. The glance of the Lord upon the devotees, the glance of the spiritual master upon the devotees, who is worshipping him, Divya Gyan, Hire Prakashita. So it is a transcendental process. It is a transcendental thing that is going on.

### Jnana-Prayasa, service, qualification, mercy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GFHkEL1Sipk?t=891
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Essence of all realization is devotional service to the Lord."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the dangers of imbalanced effort on the jnanic path, cautioning against excessive speculation and emphasizing that true qualification and understanding come through service and the mercy of the spiritual master.

So we should hold on to the lotus feet of Srila Prabhupada, beg for the mercy of the Lord. There is another kind of contamination when there is an imbalanced effort on a devotee's path, Jnanic path. You have to think, think, think. You have to cross sometimes this thinking stage. Sometimes you have to think. We all thought about it and came to Krishna consciousness. Go on thinking, thinking, thinking, thinking everything. When we have to put effort to think, then that is speculation. Thinking is not wrong, but thinking will automatically come. Thoughts will automatically come. When you struggle to think, then it is Jnana-Prayasa. Unnecessary endeavor. You have a philosophical question, you think too much, you cannot answer now. Jnana-Prayasa. Wait, get the Adhikara. Get the qualification. Adhikara for the soul means enough more services to be done. You have to raise your spiritual platform. Then you will get the answer. Then you will have qualification to understand the answer. It is not that for every question that you ask, you have to get an answer now. What is the use of you getting the answer if you cannot understand the answer? So the question has to be raised, no doubt. It is our nature to raise question. But then we have to understand that ultimately the Adhikara, the qualification comes when the spiritual master is pleased, when Guru is pleased, Srila Prabhupada is pleased, Srila Prabhupada is pleased with the devotee who is sincerely trying to advance and who is trying to serve him because ultimately essence of all Jnana is what? Service to the Lord. Essence of all realization is what? Devotional service to the Lord.

### service, Srila Prabhupada, mercy, books

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers

**Summary:** This segment emphasizes the immense blessings of serving a pure devotee like Srila Prabhupada and highlights the importance of his books as his most significant contribution to the world, carrying the mercy of the spiritual world.

So when we get an opportunity to directly serve a pure devotee like Srila Prabhupada, we are all greatly blessed where we are able to accumulate what nobody can accumulate in millions and millions of births. Today we are able to understand Krishna consciousness, we are able to chant Hare Krishna, we are able to understand Bhagavatam, read Bhagavatam, talk about Bhagavatam verses. All these things are only possible because of the mercy of Srila Prabhupada. Now Srila Prabhupada is not an ordinary sadhu. Ordinary sadhu, person to person, you sit with him, he talks something, he can bring about transformation in you. Srila Prabhupada is a sadhu, here see very clearly explained the duty of a king who desires to protect his disciples. He is very grateful for Kardama Muni explaining to him everything, for the words that came out of Kardama Muni. But today Srila Prabhupada's words, it is all Lord's Leela. These words of Srila Prabhupada are printed in this form. His words are captured in sound. In recorded sound. If one has faith in Srila Prabhupada, then one can continuously receive his mercy. He can receive his mercy. Transcendently receive his mercy. Anybody can receive his mercy. That is why Prabhupada always stressed, distribute my books, distribute my books, distribute my books. This is the most important thing that Prabhupada considers as his contribution to the world, are not the temples, but it is the books. Srila Prabhupada himself has considered this to be most important contribution, which is coming from the spiritual world under the order of the Guru Parampara and Krishna, that he has contributed this to this world. It is his books.

### mercy, collecting, Srila Prabhupada, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GFHkEL1Sipk?t=1245
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Mercy is available for those who want to take it."
**Summary:** This segment contrasts the traditional process of begging for mercy with the current situation where Srila Prabhupada has made mercy readily available, emphasizing the need to develop the capacity to collect it and recognizing this as a special time in history due to Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's movement.

Normally availability of mercy is the problem. The recipient is qualified and then he begs, begs, begs, begs. He does all his tapasya, then he begs for the kripa, begs for the kripa. Now the problem is not that way. Prabhupada so mercifully has given, Krishna has made this arrangement. Mercy is available for those who want to take it. So in that sense, we lack collecting capacity. We are not collecting enough. Today, mercy is available in Srila Prabhupada's books. His words are available. We only have to collect it. How much you can collect, how fast you can collect, we advance in spiritual life. We progress in spiritual life. So this is not an ordinary, ordinary period of time in the history. It's a very special time in the history. As far as spiritual growth is concerned, all because of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's movement, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's Hare Krishna movement, and His mercy distributed by Srila Prabhupada all over the world, all these things are very easily available to us. When something is easily available, we don't value it much. So we have to be very careful. We have to respect, respect to the availability of this transcendental source on earth. Take maximum benefit, to take this association from Srila Prabhupada. Sadhu Sangha, Prabhupada Sangha is available, which is a fact.

### Srila Prabhupada, association, qualities, meditation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GFHkEL1Sipk?t=1363
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The person can be perceived by his interest. The person can be perceived by his determination. By his personal qualities, you can perceive a person."
**Summary:** This segment explains how to associate with Srila Prabhupada even without physically seeing him, emphasizing the importance of reading his life history, perceiving his desires, aspirations, purity, and determination, and meditating on his qualities to imbibe them.

You don't have to see Prabhupada. If you read his life history, he'll touch you. You can feel him. Therefore devotees should all read Srila Prabhupada's Leelas. They should hear, and they should read Srila Prabhupada's pastimes of how from 1965, he built up this movement, till he left this planet, all the different activities that he did. When you read it, then you'll, you'll be able to, you have to perceive a person, means you have to perceive that person's desire, you have to perceive that person's aspirations, you should perceive that person's purity, you have to perceive that person's Iccha Shakti. Perceiving, those things are perceiving the person. How do, a devotee asked me, how to be conscious of Prabhupada? It's not simply his form. You get stuck there. The person can be perceived by his interest. The person can be perceived by his determination. By his personal qualities, you can perceive a person. You can pinpoint, very uniquely, who was Prabhupada, by his determination to go to America and preach, by his desire to please his spiritual master, by his aspiration to spread Lord Chaitanya's movement to everywhere, irrespective of any country. His confidence and trust that the Holy Name is powerful enough to change everyone. These are all the things that one can perceive in Srila Prabhupada's heart. Once you perceive that, you go on perceiving that, you'll, if you perceive these things in Srila Prabhupada, then those qualities will come into our heart also. Our determination to progress, our faith in the Holy Name, our aspiration to preach Krishna Consciousness, unrestrictedly in this world, to bring down the mission more and more into this material world, all these aspirations also will come to us by meditating on Prabhupada and his qualities. Any questions?

### sadhana, process, mercy, transcendental process
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GFHkEL1Sipk?t=1529
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Think of the process as a transcendental process of effort and mercy."
**Summary:** This segment addresses a question about attending sadhana as a process, clarifying that it's acceptable if understood as a two-way transcendental process involving both our effort and the mercy of Prabhupada and Krishna, contrasting it with the material concept of process where only visible effort guarantees results.

Generally, sadhana we attend like a process. Will it be okay, Prabhu, or anything consciously we have to make some effort? It's a process. As you understand what process means to you. If it is a two-way process, if you think it is a two-way process, that means you are doing something and Prabhupada is also doing something to you. You are doing something and Prabhupada and Krishna is doing something to you. If this perception is there, then thinking of this as a process is fine. But normally in this material world, process generally means it's you put your effort only and the result has to be there. Or even your effort, somebody, all other people's effort, it's all visible, grossly visible efforts. In the material world also, process sometimes means this way and that way, people, all those things, combination, combination of... Just like a factory produces something, they say it is a... It was a process of teamwork, they will say. But here, it's not visible like that. It's a transcendental process. That means your effort you can see, grossly. But, the other effort that, that other mercy that comes from Prabhupada and Krishna and Lord in the heart, these things cannot be seen. If you are Shrabdha, you can capture it. You know this is coming from... You feel that it is mercy. So think of the process as a transcendental process of effort and mercy. Shrama, cheshta from our side, kripa from the Lord. Kripa from transcendence, effort from our side. So, it is God helps those who help themselves.

### Mayavadis, guru, eternal dependence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GFHkEL1Sipk?t=1655
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "We understand it's an eternal process. I am eternally dependent upon my guru."
**Summary:** This segment contrasts the Vaishnava understanding of the guru with the Mayavadi perspective, emphasizing the eternal dependence on the guru for guidance and service, even in the spiritual world.

Mayavadis... The contrast is Mayavadis. Mayavadis say there is nothing like kripa. First of all, there is nothing like God, so where is kripa? It's all... It's all like you have to gyanically come out of it. This entanglement. That is the reason why Mayavadis say that if you are... Say you should accept a guru, they say, to neophytes. And then they say that if you don't advance enough to one day give up the need for a guru, then you are not advancing. So this is, you take a guru, I mean in a derogatory way if you put it, in a personal way if you take it, you accept a guru so that one day you can stamp him out. You advance, then you climb above his shoulders and then here I am. Advanced. So we think, we understand it's an eternal process. I am eternally dependent upon my guru. I go to the spiritual world also, I depend upon my guru. Here he will teach me how to be liberated from material energy, how to purify. There he will teach me how to adopt the right kind of mood to serve the Supreme Lord. Nobody can go and directly serve the Supreme Lord. You need a, there is transcendental intelligence in the spiritual world. So there are masters in the spiritual world who knows how actually to please Krishna. You cannot just go and please Krishna the way you want. So my guru is eternal guru.

### guru, surrender, authority, shravanam kirtanam

He is my guru in my spiritual world also. That is the reason why we make a big issue about the guru issue. He is not a joke. When you surrender to a spiritual master, it's not a joke. That surrender that being talked about, the same faith that you have in Krishna has to be in the guru. Means what? Unless one is authorized by the predecessor spiritual master, one cannot become guru. That is why Prabhupada did not authorize anybody. Because it will amount to cheating. Because the principle says that this is what a guru should be. He should be in the category of Narada Muni. He should be one who is embraced by Lord Krishna. Anybody can be a teacher. Anybody can give, can voice Prabhupada's teaching just like we are speaking now Bhagavatam. These are all gurus who can assist the main guru. Now I am talking on behalf of Srila Prabhupada. Today, tomorrow I will sit there, you will talk on behalf of Prabhupada. It's just a shravanam kirtanam process. It's just a shravanam kirtanam process.

### guru, faith, absolute truth, self-certification

**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "These two processes of capability cannot be self-certified."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the importance of absolute faith in the spiritual master and the need for the guru to be capable of taking surrender and transparently passing it on to Krishna, emphasizing that these capabilities cannot be self-certified.

So that is the perception we have. janme janme prabhu sei. Even grossly we sing janme janme prabhu sei. I mean imagine if some guru falls down. You do guru puja everyday janme janme prabhu sei. That means all along till now whatever prayer we made is a joke. That means in see why it is a joke is because in a path to transcendental achievement no portion of it can be no portion of that process can be based on falsity. One, this was very nicely explained once by Vejavaswami. He said this process is to achieve the absolute truth. The path cannot be a path where there is falsity. That means it's not the path. The path also has to be absolute truth. So faith in the truth. So faith in the spiritual master has to be absolute. If it has to be absolute, then the person whom you are putting that faith has to be absolute. There is a science behind all these things. Not simply, you know, anybody, you know, a few friends, few God brothers say Prabhu, so many years now you are a devotee. Why don't you do some service to Srila Prabhupada of accepting some disciples? Okay, okay, I don't want to be guru. I don't want to be personally guru. But all my God brothers are pushing me. Yes, yes, I want to serve Prabhupada like this. But then there is a short-circuiting, a faithful process. According to our Siddhanta, a person accepts a spiritual master, a person is a spiritual master, he has to, you know, surrender his soul to him. Is a person capable of taking that surrender? ### japa quality, mindless japa, mindful japa, concurrent chanting, chesta shakti
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/-GhND-zjUwg?t=117
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Chanting and hearing that very chant makes it very easy to lock the attention is the first secret of locking the attention, first secret of begging for the chesta shakti from the Lord himself, from the nama himself."
**Summary:** The speaker reviews the four stages of japa quality: mindless, mindful, heartful, and soulful, explaining mindful japa as concurrent chanting and hearing, attracting chesta shakti from Nama Prabhu.

So if you recall, we categorized the progress of our japa, quality of our japa. Stage one is mindless japa. Stage two is mindful japa. Stage three is heartful japa. Stage four is soulful japa. Mindless japa, I just touch upon each of them also without going into too many details. Please recall, mindless japa means what? Mind is not on you japa. You're doing auto japa. That means your physical body is doing japa and your, you know, trained mind is tracking how many beats, all those things, and in this way it is in auto mode. So this is called mindless japa. What is the problem with mindless japa? You are not giving your soul. Your soul is somewhere else, but Krishna wants your soul, wants your attention. Where your attention is, there the soul is. Therefore attention is very much linked to good japa. Now what is mindful japa? Mindful japa is when our attention is locked to chanting and hearing that very chant. And please get familiarized with all these words. We use a word concurrent chanting. That means you should, when you chant and you hear that very chant concurrent, and automatically that will attract chesta shakti from Nama Prabhu himself. So in concurrent chanting, chanting is a doing effort, hearing is a knowing effort. Chanting is called doing effort, is kriya chesta, and knowing effort is called jnana chesta. So kriya chesta, when you want to do something, then it generates doing attention because without attention on something you cannot do. And similarly, hearing is one of the five input knowing activities, so hearing it comes under knowing the sound, so for that you need to have attention to know Hare Krishna Rama. So you need attention. That knowing attention is released when there is knowing effort. That means we have to offer our kriya chesta and jnana chesta on every manifesting mala, on every manifesting nama, not mala, every manifesting nama. Okay, these are some of the secrets of attentive japa, not only chanting, not only hearing, chanting and hearing that very chant makes it very easy to lock the attention is the first secret of locking the attention, first secret of begging for the chesta shakti from the Lord himself, from the nama himself. When you attract the chesta shakti from the Lord, what happens? You like doing and knowing. You learn the sound. You like chanting and hearing, chanting and hearing. You want to repeat again and again. You like, like, like keep doing, keep doing something that gives you energy. You like to do, keep doing, is it not? Then we talked about tasking.

### tasking, repetition, stacking, scattering
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/-GhND-zjUwg?t=396
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Japan is not chanting come hearing alone. Japan means chanting come hearing repeated. What is a vidhi which is a way it has to be repeated? If there's a qualifying qualifying factor there for repeating what is that uninterrupted."
**Summary:** The lecture emphasizes that japa is not just chanting and hearing, but also uninterrupted tasking, which is repetition. He contrasts scattered tasking with stacking, explaining that stacking builds up the effect of purification of the heart.

Tasking is a very important part of Japan. Japan does not mean chanting and hearing only. That is a misconception we carry in our head because we think Japan is chanting and hearing. We take special care for chanting and hearing and we neglect something more important, very important, equally important. I would not say more, equally important is uninterrupted tasking. And tasking means what? Tasking means repetition, repetition of chanting come here in channel, chanting come hearing. So Japan is not chanting come hearing alone. Japan means chanting come hearing repeated. What is a vidhi which is a way it has to be repeated? If there's a qualifying qualifying factor there for repeating what is that uninterrupted. And what happens in stacking in in in in tasking there are two ways of asking. One is okay I had to finish my 16th round somehow the whole day you finish or you chant about you know half a mala or something else comes you keep it then again start other half mala like this kind of things so that is the first scattered bricks you see every now and then you chant and they are like placing many bricks spreading the bricks on the table and you see what is the effect of the second drawing proper tasking is stacking. Stacking means one above the other. In this what happens if you see the weight on the table the force on the table below each brick on the left side is one brick weight whereas suppose your table is the table is your heart and here in this table you see it is piling up what is the weight sum total of all this similarly in tasking as you go one round two round three round four round it builds up the effect of purification of the heart is built up it is not scattered chanting so it is tasking by stacking not tasking by scattering.

## Soulful Japa lecture I Sri Madhu Pandit Dasa I 29.07.2023
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

### invocation, mantra, Hare Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9Mb2l1YFPX0?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Hare Krishna Maha Mantra is the necklace of 16 words."
**Summary:** The lecture begins with traditional Vaishnava prayers and introduces the Hare Krishna Maha Mantra as the sound incarnation of Krishna and Radharani.

Om Agyanati Murandhasya Gyananjana Shalakya Chakshurun Militam Yena Tasmai Shri Gurave Namaha. Namah Om Vishnupadaya Krishna Prishtaya Bhutale Srimati Bhaktivedanta Swami Nithilamane. Namaste Saraswati Deve Guravani Pracharani Nirvishesha Shrunyavadi Paschatya Deshatarani. Jai Shri Krishna Chaitanya Prabhu Nityananda Shri Advaita Gadhadhara Shiva Sadhi Gauravukta Vidya. Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare. Hare Krishna Nama Prabhu Ki Jai.

---
### Hare Krishna, Radharani, love
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9Mb2l1YFPX0?t=42
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "This sound, Hare Krishna and Rama, they are the personalities of infinite love, compassion, consciousness."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the Hare Krishna mantra, explaining that the names Krishna and Rama represent personalities of infinite love and compassion, attracting all beings.

So this Hare Krishna Maha Mantra, the necklace of 16 words, Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare, Nama Shabda Mala, is the incarnation of the sound incarnation of the Supreme Personality of Godhead Krishna, the Param Purusha, and the Param Prakriti Srimati Radharani.

### consciousness, sound, Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9Mb2l1YFPX0?t=94
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is come down in the Shabdavatara, just like our body is tiny consciousness, this sound has got infinite consciousness."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that we are naturally attracted to personal qualities, and the Hare Krishna mantra embodies infinite love and consciousness, with Krishna manifesting as sound.

We are all attracted to persons, especially persons who have wonderful personal qualities. We are all naturally attracted and this sound, Hare Krishna and Rama, they are the personalities of infinite love, compassion, consciousness, and whole world. Just like we are tiny consciousness, our body has got tiny consciousness. Krishna is come down in the Shabdavatara. Just like our body is tiny consciousness, this sound has got infinite consciousness.

### Shabdavatara, Krishna, Shakti
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9Mb2l1YFPX0?t=176
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The sound that we chant is like the vessel into which Nama Prabhu, who is Krishna himself, shines all his satya, chitta and ananda shakti into that sound."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of sunlight reflecting in water to explain how Krishna's energies manifest in the sound of the Hare Krishna mantra.

Krishna himself manifests as the sound. The sound that we chant is like the vessel into which Nama Prabhu, who is Krishna himself, shines all his satya, chitta and ananda shakti into that sound. Just like one sun in the sky shines sunlight into so many water bodies and forms the reflection of that sun. The same way one ever effulgent, eternally sounding Nama of this Supreme Lord in the spiritual world takes avatara first by shining all his shaktis into the sun.

---
### Shabda Avatara, Shakti, Nama Prabhu
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9Mb2l1YFPX0?t=261
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "For the Lord, there is no difference between his Shakti and himself; there's no difference between Nama Prabhu's effulgence and Nama Prabhu himself."
**Summary:** The speaker explains Krishna's descent as Shabda Avatara, emphasizing the unity of the Lord and His Shakti, and the non-difference between Nama Prabhu's effulgence and Nama Prabhu Himself.

The sound that we utter. Therefore he's called Shabda Avatara. Krishna can come in Sharira Avatara, Meena Sharira, Narasimha Sharira, all that he can take Avatara's. Shabda Sharira. Shabda Sharira, when he comes, as I said, the Supreme Lord descends into that sound with all his Shaktis. Sarva Shakti. Question may be asked, the Lord himself comes or his Shakti only comes? For the Lord, there is no difference between his Shakti and himself. There's no difference between Nama Prabhu's effulgence and Nama Prabhu himself.

### purification, heart, consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9Mb2l1YFPX0?t=337
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Nama Shakti's, their function is to purify our heart; purifying the heart means the heart is contaminated."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the function of Nama Shakti in purifying the heart, which is currently contaminated with asat, achit, and nirananda.

But when we are, yet there is a difference. Nama Shakti's, their function is to purify our heart. What is the meaning of purifying the heart? Purifying the heart means the heart is contaminated. What is heart? Heart is consciousness. What is consciousness? Sat Chit-ananda. Sat Chit-ananda. Right now our heart is contaminated with asat, achit, niranand. False sat, false chit, false anand.

### Asat, Achit, Maya
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9Mb2l1YFPX0?t=394
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Asat, we consider as sat, I am the body."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the concepts of asat (temporary), achit (ignorance), and how they manifest in our perception of reality.

That is our present consciousness, each of our consciousness. What is temporary, we consider as everlasting, sat, asat. Everywhere we are seeing, things are changing, eternally changing, our body is changing, yet we think that, we never think that the body dies. So asat, we consider as sat, I am the body. And so much of achit, we have forgotten we are eternal, we have eternal relationship with Krishna. Achit means, I am the body and bodily relationships are mine. The bodily world is real world. The world of bodily egos are real world. This is achit.

---
### Nirananda, Material Pleasure, Contamination
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9Mb2l1YFPX0?t=475
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Nirananda means no ananda, but we think it is ananda."
**Summary:** The speaker describes nirananda (false pleasure) and how we falsely perceive material sense pleasures as true happiness, leading to a contaminated heart.

Lies, we are living in lies. Nirananda, jadananda. Nirananda means no ananda, but we think it is ananda. All the material sense pleasures, so called material love and intimacy in this world, we think it is true. Only love, intimacy and pleasure in association with Krishna is satya, is truth. Others is nirananda. We have got, our heart is full of false pleasure beliefs, that this will give me pleasure, that will give me happiness, this will give me… This is called nirananda.

---
### True Pleasure, Krishna, Sat Chit Ananda
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9Mb2l1YFPX0?t=527
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Whereas a true pleasure conception is giving pleasure to Krishna, love of Krishna, the pleasure of love of Krishna, the pleasure of seeing Krishna, the pleasure of beauty of Krishna, these are all pleasures."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts false material pleasure with true pleasure found in Krishna, emphasizing that the soul is ultimately satisfied only with satya, chitta, and ananda.

Heart is contaminated. Whereas a true pleasure conception is giving pleasure to Krishna, love of Krishna, the pleasure of love of Krishna, the pleasure of seeing Krishna, the pleasure of beauty of Krishna, these are all pleasures. Now we are contaminated. We take pleasure in seeing material beauties, which are maya or just momentary appearances. Does not last. So, our heart is full of asat, achit and nirananda, thinking that to be satya, chitta and ananda. Because the soul is never satisfied with asat, achit and nirananda. The soul's original essence is satya, chitta and ananda.

### Holy Name, Purification, Shakti Avesha Nama
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9Mb2l1YFPX0?t=590
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Therefore the holy name first shines pure satya, chitta and ananda energy."
**Summary:** The speaker explains how the holy name transmits pure satya, chitta, and ananda energy, using the analogy of iron becoming red hot in fire, and introduces the concept of Shakti Avesha Nama.

Therefore the holy name first shines pure satya, chitta and ananda energy. Unlimited quantities is available. Just like Surya has got unlimited light and heat. Similarly, when Krishna descends into our heart, through our ears, just as the iron put in the fire becomes red hot, in the same way, when nama, Hare Krishna nama goes into our heart, automatically there is an energy that goes into our heart, satya, chitta and ananda energy. One will realize the truth. And only when our heart is cleansed of asat, achit and nirananda, only then, what is the point in shuddha nama coming now to your tongue? So, first he sends the shaktis. Shakti Avesha Nama. So, we worship Shakti Avesha Nama, so that Chetodharpanam happens.

### Shuddha Nama, Purification, Reciprocity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9Mb2l1YFPX0?t=670
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "There is no difference between His reflection and Him; there is no deflection, there is no distance between Him and His name."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that when the heart is cleansed, the Lord manifests the shuddha nama on the tongue, emphasizing the absolute nature of the Lord and the reciprocal relationship in chanting.

When our heart is cleansed, as I said, Lord is absolute. There is no difference between His reflection and Him. There is no deflection, there is no distance between Him and His name. He will immediately manifest on our tongue the shuddha nama, provided our heart becomes pure. When our heart becomes pure, then our body and senses also become pure. They become spiritualized. This is the goal of chanting Hare Krishna Maha Mantra. Therefore, very effectively if you want, you have to take, you want the effect of nama avatara, you want to experience our original satya, chitta and ananda, then we have to see that the holy name, one after the other, goes into our heart. It can go into our heart, through hearing. Chanting the holy name of the Lord, He is worshipping, and as you worship Him, the Lord is gifting us with His association, if you hear. So we chant, and the Lord reciprocates by entering our heart, as iron put in the fire becomes red hot, the Lord, to the extent to which we are ready to give up satya, chitta and ananda, immediately He is ready to replace it with satya, chitta and ananda.

To go into our heart? **Summary:** The speaker explains that chanting without thinking is a great tapasya (austerity) made possible by the mercy of Nama Prabhu, who provides the 'divya chesta shakti' (divine energy) to focus attention.

Very big yoga, though it is very simple, it is simply executable, because of the mercy, from the, nama prabhu, somewhere, shakti should come, that is called, divya chesta shakti, to do, chanting and hearing, that shakti, to lock the attention, that shakti, to keep away from thinking, comes from, the holy name, hearing the holy name, so therefore, it is very important, that the first round, that we chant, should always be, deliberate hearing, bead after bead, mantra after mantra, mantra after mantra, automatically, it gets locked, that connection, should be established, I always give example of, an engine starting, in the beginning, with the battery power, the piston is rotated, then the spark plug fires, then the fuel takes over, then the engine fires, then the engine is run, not on your, on the battery power, rather it charges the battery, similarly,

### Hrithpurvaka Japa, Soulful Chanting, Involvement
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9Mb2l1YFPX0?t=1834
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker introduces Hrithpurvaka Japa, emphasizing the importance of chanting from the heart and involving the whole soul, mind, senses, and spirit in the process.

Next mala you practice Hrithpurvaka Japa. There is a tendency for us to mechanically chant. So Hrithpurvaka Japa means heartfully we should chant. We should do from our heart. That means my whole soul should be involved. Soul means your spirit is there, your heart is there, your mind is there, your senses are there, everything is given to the sound incarnation. When Krishna has come before me, everything should be given to him. My mind should be given, my intelligence should be given, my desiring capacity should be given, my enjoying, everything that I, my delightful, every delight, everything should be given to the Nama. That is called Hrithpurvaka Japa. If you don't practice this there is a tendency that okay I

## Soulful Japa: Session 25 : Strengthen Background Mind to Link Chanting & Hearing -Module 38-53
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

### chanting and hearing, reprogramming the mind, linked chanting
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2AgVcz8ve94?t=27
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Chant and know the chant instead of hearing because my old programming is there hearing when there were two things I had to do chanting and hearing."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa explains the importance of linking chanting and hearing, emphasizing that hearing is knowing and chanting is doing, and shares his personal experience of reprogramming his mind to overcome the perception of hearing as a separate activity.

When you are fresh, let me give you the main concept for the day. All that we are going to talk about in these next few days are different kinds of knowledge that will strengthen our background linking direction. That means in the background we have to have the direction to not chant and hear separately, but chant and hear the same word, and hearing is knowing chant. If most of you, because at least it was for me like that, hearing was also like a doing. So always I was putting doing effort to chant and to hear, and it was not going anywhere. So that is the reason why I keep telling that hearing is a knowing and chanting is a doing. I had to tell myself a hundred times to come out of that endeavor to hear as a doing. So finally I came out of it. So that is, after that, for quite some time I used to simply say chant and know the chant, chant and know the chant, chant and know the chant instead of hearing because my old programming is there hearing when there were two things I had to do chanting and hearing. So chant and know the chant, chant and know the chant. So in this way I reprogram my mind with, you know, replace my old program as two doings as only one doing and one knowing of that very doing as Srila Prabhupada says, chant and exactly the same word you hear, exactly the same word you hear.

**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa discusses the materials posted in modules 39-45, emphasizing their purpose in strengthening the background direction for linked chanting by providing reasons and conviction, and encouraging devotees to read them for inspiration and to direct their background mind.

Now I have posted some material in module 39 onwards. 37 is a training module, 38 is a golden codes, and 39 to 45 I have written some things in the modules. All those modules are meant for strengthening this background direction because if you have enough reasons, see anything to go into our subconscious mind, if you give reasons it will go inside. Why it works, why chanting come hearing, why not separate, all those things once you understand and you are convinced that it has to be, you know, together simultaneously on the same sound, then it is very easy for our background mind to accept and finally to go into the subconscious mind. So therefore you should not get confused with the so many modules being posted. The training portion is only one thing: connect the foreground mind gets direction from the background mind or help from outside in a printout or whatever it is in order to practice linked chanting come knowing chanting come hearing so that is the purpose of all this exercise. So just because I am posting many more modules, it's just for your reading in order to strengthen your determination and conviction that yes, they have to be done together, they have to be done together. So in to support your training again I am writing many things, you please keep reading all those things, then it becomes very clear for you and then your background mind will, uh, not only the effect of three times chanting before the thing before the each round, but during the day when you read these modules it will give you an inspiration to direct your your your background mind will will direct in a very strong and a determined way to know the sound that you are chanting always every time each time so that is that is uh the important thing that I wanted to tell.

### doing and knowing, attentive chanting, soul energy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2AgVcz8ve94?t=723
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna wants you to chant Hare Krishna, not your simply your tongue and your automatic chanting. Krishna wants you to chant. Where are you? You are where your attention is. What is your soul energy? Soul energy is attention."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa uses the analogy of throwing and catching a ball to illustrate the difference between doing and knowing, emphasizing the importance of attentive chanting where the soul's energy (attention) is present, rather than automatic or inattentive chanting.

I have given a lot of examples already, and still I'll give one more. Today morning I thought of one simple thing, how doing and knowing. So here there is a ball. Now suppose I want, somebody tells me, I want you, I want to teach you something. I will ask him what is it? So he will tell me. Prabhupada, do one thing. First take the ball, one hand, then you throw to the other hand. The other hand should catch, then to the other hand, then to the other hand, then to the other hand, then to the other hand, like this, practice this. Now you see, there is doing and knowing. The very simple exercise. Doing and knowing. In this case, doing and knowing, after some time it gets so practiced that even without knowing you will do. But that's not what we want in japa. We want the knowing to be always there, inattentive, this can become inattentive doing. What is that inattentive doing? Got practiced. I can think of that, I can think of this, I can do so many things, already practiced thinking. So that means inattentive doing. Inattentive, auto doing, even doing I am not attentive, auto doing. What is attentive doing and knowing? Attentive doing is consciously throw, catch and know. If I can do without consciously, why should I, what is the need for me to be conscious? This is where it comes. Krishna wants you, not your hand and strength of your hand and throwing the ball, all that, no. Krishna wants you to chant Hare Krishna, not your simply your tongue and your automatic chanting. Krishna wants you to chant. Where are you? You are where your attention is. What is your soul energy? Soul energy is attention. You give your soul energy.

But still, you never get fed up. You are attentive to taste. Because to taste, you are attentive to taste. You are attentive to taste. To taste, you have to be attentive. To taste, you have to be attentive. Similarly, that taste, doing, knowing, taste, connected to Nama Prabhu. Doing and knowing the Nama Prabhu is definitely a taste, a transcendental taste. I told you that energy comes from the Nama Prabhu. This is the fact that people, devotees who have started practicing this are able to do 50 rounds, 25 rounds easily. Knowing, doing and tasking. This, I may get fed up of this because this is a ball. There is no end to it. There is no end to it. There is no end to it. There is no energy coming from this ball for knowing and doing. I will get fed up of it. In the sense, it will be uninteresting for me. But the chanting and hearing, chanting and knowing, knowing that it is God's name, Lord's name, it gives energy and simultaneously, that simultaneous linking direction. ### variety, attention, offering
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2AgVcz8ve94?t=1017
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Ensure that even starting, even before starting the japa, make it very clear that all this is an offering to the Nama who is before me each time."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa contrasts the simplicity of ball-throwing with the variety in the Hare Krishna mantra, emphasizing the need for attention to each name and the importance of feeling that the chanting is an offering to the Nama before starting japa.

It is a very simple activity. Your table tennis is a little more complicated because it is not possible. Can you play table tennis mechanically? This I can do mechanically a thousand times. Table tennis, you cannot do mechanically. Why? You do not know where the ball is going to come next. Why? You do not know where the ball is going to come next. But at least in the case of Hare Krishna Maha Mantra, we know after Hare Krishna is coming, after Krishna, Hare is coming, after Hare, another Krishna is coming. So it is not as simple as this, it is not as complicated as the table tennis. Yet in this, there is variety, sixteen names are coming in an order. My knowing has to be on that. Hare first Krishna, then Hare, then Krishna, then like that. So, one should not overlap with the other. That means at that particular point of time, each time what you are chanting, your attention is on that. This will work wonders. And this is what is doing wonders, bringing that the transcendental energy from the Nama Prabhu flows into our heart and mantra after mantra after mantra, we feel like, you know, chanting more, more, more and more and more. And this is one thing. So, this is as far as the feeling, instruction for all of you, that during the training, you please ensure that, ensure, how do you ensure? Not during the japa. Ensure that even starting, even before starting the japa, make it very clear that all this is an offering to the Nama who is before me each time. Okay. This is very important. It's not an offering to simply a thought. It's an offering to Nama. No, it's an offering to the Shabda, which is in front of you, who is in front of you each time. So the receiver of this service is that Nama himself.

**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa explains the difference between auto doing and conscious effort, using the example of someone completing rounds of japa while their mind is preoccupied, resulting in inattentive japa.

There is an auto doing and there is a Kriyachesta where you are doing consciously. Somebody chants 16 rounds in one sitting. Starts with bead one. Goes on. Some problem during the day time he is thinking how to solve it. It is a very complicated problem. Thinking goes on and on and on. And his beads are also moving on and on automatically. See. Mala after mala. Sakshi mala also is moving. Sixteen rounds he has completed because for years together he has practiced and it can automatically do it. At the end he keeps his mala down. You ask him did you hear any mantra? Did you chant any mantra? He will say not. I did not. I did not give any chesta. I did not give any doing chesta. I did not give any knowing chesta. This is called inattentive japa. So our body is capable of inattentively doing things. So there are certain things which can be inattentive.

The content of the dream is false, but the attention with which the dreamer is watching the dream is true, and the experience of pain and pleasure, as Prabhupada said, even in his gross body is crying out, so he has got an experience. **Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of understanding 'Shakti Avesha Nama' (the name infused with energy), urging devotees not to perceive the sound as ordinary, and connects this to the desire to please the Nama Prabhu through concurrent chanting and hearing, driven by intention and purpose.

This is something, the Shakti Avesha Nama is Shakti Avesha, it is Shakti has entered into it. This understanding is very, very, very important for the devotee to not to see the sound as ordinary sound. So then we said, we also discussed about pleasing, the Nama Prabhu, that means each desire to please, desire to please by what? Concurrent chanting and hearing. So desire is also there, okay. So intention is there, purpose is there, intention, purpose, desire and intention. So this is what basically we discussed yesterday.

### Purpose, Completeness, Satchitananda
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/-Bclb_Qg6zk?t=535
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The purpose is to feel a sense of completeness, completeness in Sat, completeness in Chit, completeness in Ananda."
**Summary:** The lecture summarizes the previous day's discussion, stating that the purpose of chanting is to feel a sense of completeness in Sat (existence), Chit (consciousness), and Ananda (bliss), collectively known as Satchitananda.

That there is purpose, there is desire and there is intention. The purpose is to feel a sense of completeness, completeness in Sat, completeness in Chit, completeness in Ananda. Okay. That is Satchitananda completeness. That is the purpose.

### Emptiness, Krishna, Association
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/-Bclb_Qg6zk?t=571
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The living entity in the material world, they're all feeling empty. Something is missing."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the feeling of emptiness experienced by living entities in the material world, attributing it to a missing connection with Krishna, which they futilely seek in various material pursuits.

The living entity in the material world, they're all feeling empty. Something is missing. Something is missing. Something is missing. And what is that something, Krishna? And that something missing, they are searching here, here, in this, in this, in this, in this, happiness in this, love, pleasure, sense, pleasure, love, power, knowledge, all kinds of things, wealth, education, beauty, influence, power, money, you're all looking for that. Like mad people, they've gone mad because we can be complete only in association of a relationship with Krishna.

### Devotional Service, Organism, Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/-Bclb_Qg6zk?t=623
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Existence is one organism."
**Summary:** The lecture explains that completeness is found in devotional service to Krishna, comparing it to a part serving the whole body, and asserting that existence is one vast organism with Krishna as the supreme person.

With Krishna of being his part and parcel and relating with him in devotional service. A part has to serve the body. My hand has to serve the body. My whole thing is one organism. Similarly, existence is one organism. And that supreme person is? Krishna. Supreme person is Krishna. And we are tiny, tiny persons. Nithyo nithyanam chetanas chetananam, ekoyo bahuman, yo vidadati kaman. One eternal is maintaining all these eternal. This is the organism. Existential organism, not even universal organism. Forget material world, spiritual world, all put together existence. Existential organism. So huge.
