### Infinite, Personal Relationship, Completeness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/-Bclb_Qg6zk?t=682
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Though it's inexhaustible, each of part and parcel, he has got a personal relationship with them."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the infinite nature of the Lord and His personal relationship with each individual soul, even within the vastness of existence, and reiterates that completeness is achieved by connecting with Krishna.

Huge organism, because Lord is infinite. Inexhaustible is his leelas, inexhaustible is his number of expansion, inexhaustible. Though it's inexhaustible, each of part and parcel, he has got a personal relationship with them. This is the catch. This is an unbelievable thing. Proof of that is, he's seated in the heart of every, even a germ, he's sitting in the heart of the body. This is the catch. This is the germ he's sitting, not only in humans. And he says he's the seed giving father of all living entities, Sarva Yonish, not only humans. So that is the organism. There is an existential organism. We can feel completeness. Only when we connect with the body. Only when we connect with the body. Only when we connect. When we connect ourselves, this hand will feel completeness when it connects itself to the body.

### Forgetfulness, Normalcy, Hunger
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/-Bclb_Qg6zk?t=747
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If we disconnect from Krishna, which means forgetfulness of Krishna, then there is, we are not normal."
**Summary:** The lecture uses the analogy of a severed finger to illustrate the state of disconnection from Krishna, leading to a state of being 'not normal' and constantly hungry for Sat, Chit, and Ananda.

If I cut my finger off, it will have the shape of a finger, but it will not be a finger. It will not function like a finger. The same way, if we disconnect from Krishna, which means forgetfulness of Krishna, then there is, we are not normal. We are not human. We are not human. We are normal. Normal means what I'm starving. I'm always Sadh hungry, chit hungry, Ananda hungry. Sadh hungry means hunger for freedom, hunger for power. That is Sadh hunger. The material world, everybody is seeking Sadh means power and freedom. Knowledge hungry, seeking knowledge and intelligence. Knowledge hungry. intelligence to do.

### Starvation, Maya, Satt, Chit, Ananda
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/-Bclb_Qg6zk?t=812
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Not that you are hungry, you are satisfied. No. You are still starved."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that despite seeking fulfillment in the material world, individuals remain starved for true Sat, Chit, and Ananda, as Maya provides only fake versions of these.

Different things. Intelligence to do different things, to apply that knowledge. Hungry and starved. That's important. Not that you are hungry, you are satisfied. No. You are still starved. You are given something as knowledge but it is not knowledge. Maya gives you power and freedom. But which is not real power and freedom. Fake. So Satt power and freedom. Chit is knowledge and intelligence. Rasa. And Ananda is love. Loving activities. Pleasurable activities. That is Ananda.

So we in the material world are always feeling incomplete as far as Satt, Chit and Ananda is concerned. Is anybody satisfied with their power and freedom in this body? Is anybody satisfied with their... We can ask are you completely satisfied? Are you completely satisfied with your knowledge and intelligence? Are you completely satisfied with your Ananda, love and loving activities? Yes or No. Everybody will say no. But there is a way to say yes. And that is why Krishna Consciousness, that is by associating with Krishna. When we associate with Krishna. Krishna is the ocean of Satt, Chit, Ananda, Rasa.

### Nature of the Soul, Maya's Trick, Second Birth
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/-Bclb_Qg6zk?t=1354
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Because I am not connected to Krishna, I am independently wanting this Satchitananda Rasa."
**Summary:** The lecture explains that the soul's inherent nature is Satchitananda, but due to disconnection from Krishna, it seeks this independently, leading to Maya's illusion of identifying with the material body.

That is my nature. Because I am not connected to Krishna, I am independently wanting this Satchitananda Rasa. Maya has, Maya energy has given us a material body and first it will tell you that you are this body. This happened right from childhood, it happened. This is Maya's trick. That is why you say after certain age when you go to a Guru, you get your second birth. First birth is I am this body. Second birth I am not this body. When you come to Guru, you learn that no, I am not this body, I never have birth, I never have birth, death. So because of independent, because of forgetting Krishna, we thought we can be, we are still now, everybody in the material world still now is thinking that I can feel, I can feel my completeness tomorrow, tomorrow, tomorrow, tomorrow, tomorrow, tomorrow. But Kala will continuously defeat you, defeat you, defeat you, defeat you, defeat you.

Even scientists are not able to, you know, touch a tip of it and it is bewildering them more and more and more. More the science is advancing, it's not, you know, filling the gap of ignorance, but it is increasing the gap of ignorance. Our highest Rasa is also our original Rasa. In the material world, we run after this Rasa, that Rasa, so many Rasas. Actually, they are all not connected to us. But Krishna, Krishna Rasa or Bhakti Rasa is our original Rasa. And Rasa means Sat Rasa, Chit Rasa, Ananda Rasa. Sat Rasa means Rasa of its experience. Sat means it is the experience of permanency, experience of freedom, experience of power, experience. Sat means experience of unrestricted power. At your beck and call. That's what we want in this material world. Sat. What is entitled for this little Jeeva. We are small part of God. So when, whatever power we, that is that power, freedom and eternity. That is the Sat experience.

### Sat Chit Ananda, Krishna consciousness, eternity, freedom, power
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1ZErQDx4KV0?t=1115
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna consciousness is a perfect Sat Chit Ananda experience. Because it's not based on this body which is temporary."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa explains that Krishna consciousness is a perfect Sat Chit Ananda experience because it is based on eternal connections and relationships, providing freedom from material energy and the power to serve Krishna.

Now Krishna consciousness is a perfect Sat Chit Ananda experience. Why is it perfect Sat Chit Ananda experience? Because it's not based on this body which is temporary. We are talking Bhakti is all about eternal connection with Krishna, eternal relationship with Krishna, our eternal self related to the supreme eternal self. So, there is experience of eternity there. Everything, everything is grounded on eternity, not on any temporary transient thing in Krishna consciousness. Krishna Nama is eternal. Jeeva is eternal. Seva to the Nama is also eternal. And freedom. Sat Krishna conscious freedom means freedom from the material energy. Freedom from forgetfulness of Krishna. Because what do you want? Freedom is freedom from bondage. Bondage is caused by what? My nature is freedom, but I am not experiencing freedom because I am bonded in material world. We all want to be free, but we are bonded. In one way, you must be thanking, oh, now this COVID-19, so much freedom I got. I could sit at home and do so many things. Nobody likes to be bonded in work. When you love some work, it's a different matter. Nobody likes to be bonded. Power. When you are in Bhakti, when you are connected to Krishna, the unlimited source of power, you have all the power that you want for his service. His service is executed by his power.

### Sat, Chit, knowledge, intelligence, karma
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1ZErQDx4KV0?t=1224
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In the material world, people cannot experience eternity because they do not know what is eternal. Because they think their body is permanent."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa contrasts the experience of Sat, Chit, and Ananda in the material world with that in Krishna consciousness, explaining that material experiences are temporary and limited by karma, while spiritual experiences are eternal and based on pure knowledge and intelligence.

So you are no shortage. In the material world, people cannot experience eternity because they do not know what is eternal. Because they think their body is permanent. Their nature is to be eternal. So they never think that they will die. There is no freedom in this. There is no Sat freedom experience in this material world. There is no Sat power experience in this material world because if it is there, it is only as per karma. It's not free. You know, it's not freely. Whatever you desire, you don't get. But in the spiritual world, whatever I desire, I get for the spiritual, for the Lord's service. Chitta is the experience of knowledge and intelligence. In the material world, so much, you know, knowledge is gathered through the ascending process, through the senses. They are all imperfect knowledge. There is no purity. It's not pure knowledge because tomorrow they change again. But this descending process of knowledge, knowledge that is coming from Krishna through the parampara, Krishna through the shastras, that is something that is the experience that does not change. It is perfect knowledge. And intelligence. Intelligence is the application of that knowledge. Why do you need intelligence? You need intelligence to act. How to act towards achieving something. That is intelligence. And when you are connected in devotional service to the Lord, then your intelligence is connected to supreme intelligence. He gives us the intelligence to engage in his service.

**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa discusses Ananda as the seeking of love, and explains that only by linking our Sat, Chit, and Ananda with Krishna's can we achieve complete experience or rasa.

Ananda. In the material world. We are always seeking love. We want to love and be loved. Engaging loving activities including sense pleasures. No doubt in this material world all this sattva, chitta and ananda we are after running after. But it doesn't satisfy us. But it gives you a just like a gilded gold gives you an idea what is gold. A gilded gold is fake. Fake gold. But it gives you an idea what is gold. So, in the similar way we are not strangers to satchit ananda. But the satt is fake satt. Chitta is fake chitta. And pleasure ananda is fake pleasure in this material world. Why is it fake? Okay. Experience is an experience. Something you call fake because you expect it to be something but it is not that. So, some experience is there. That's why living in it is running around in this material world. But what do I deeply want? I want eternal sattva. I need pure chitta. I need pure love. Perfect love. And that only God can give. And only satchit ananda. Only our sattva, chitta and ananda links up with Krishna's satchit ananda.

### material energy, forgetfulness, identity, body
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1ZErQDx4KV0?t=1818
**Verse References:** BG 7.5
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When I say that I am not satchitananda I am this body material body. Next what happens once I identify with I am the body."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa explains that seeking independent Sat Chit Ananda leads to association with material energy, causing forgetfulness of our true self and identification with the material body.

So when that when that seeking is there for independent satchitananda we come in association with the material energy. Krishna says in the Bhagavad Gita association material energy is what causes us forgetfulness of our true self as satchitananda self as satchitananda servant of satchitananda lord. We identify with the material body as a soul and experience being the identity I am this body. Here the trap begins. First it was only an attitude of seeking independent satchitananda. You come in association with the material energy because your spirit your satchitananda matter is no satchit no sat no chit no ananda. It is achit asat achit nirananda vastu matter. I come in association with that. When I come in association with that naturally asat is opposite of sat achit is opposite of chit. Satchitananda is opposite of ananda. When I say that I am not satchitananda I am this body material body. Next what happens once I identify with I am the body. When you identify with I am the body. Next. We lose our satchitananda experience completely. Why should you lose? Body is achit and nirananda. If I think I am the body the body doesn't exist eternally.

### eternity, material energy, three gunas, lording attitude
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1ZErQDx4KV0?t=1926
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Loving serving consciousness of the living entity transforms into lording experience. That means independently I want to experience these things."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa explains that identifying with the body leads to a loss of the feeling of eternal experience, and that material energy traps consciousness in the three gunas, leading to a lording attitude.

Therefore I have to feel I am not you know I feel a lacuna in my feeling of eternal experience. With little intelligence I can realize that see there is not eternal experience. I took birth in this body I will leave someday. And what is the alternate we get? We get original attitude wanting independence satchitananda. Once material energy captures us our consciousness is trapped in the three gunas material three gunas. Then the characteristic of three guna is what? To independently seek satchitananda. That is called lording attitude. Lording means independently. When actually when you are connected to Krishna satchitananda you don't have to work for satchitananda. Everything is lovingly you know shared. Krishna sattva Krishna s chitta Krishna s ananda. Our satchitananda is linked together. That is serving attitude. It is a serving consciousness. Loving serving consciousness of the living entity transforms into lording experience. That means independently I want to experience these things.

### Sat Chit Ananda, nature of the soul, Krishna consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1ZErQDx4KV0?t=2027
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna consciousness is giving people something that they are all chasing after."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa explains that in the material world, we chase after perfect Sat Chit Ananda but never achieve it, while Krishna consciousness provides what everyone is seeking.

But in the material world in reality what happens? Deep in our heart I cannot change what I am. My nature I cannot change. My nature is to look for sattva and chitta and ananda. Of the quality that I want. But I don't get it. I can only chase for the perfect satchitananda but never get it in the material world. So it is a fact. Everybody is chasing for power. Everybody is chasing for freedom. Yes or no? This is very important to understand. Nature of the soul. This is what I am. I need freedom. I need power. I want freedom. I want my free will. And I don't want to die. I want permanency. Just like light has got, fire has got the quality of light and heat. This is dharma. Dharma means quality of a substance. Similarly dharma atma is what? Sattva. You have a property. You don't want anybody to grab your property. You want the property to be yours. You want loving people around you. You don't want them to really grow old and die. But they will grow old and die. You also will die. Your identity as a lover and the loved people's identity both vanish. The souls will be somewhere else and souls don't remember this at all. Only when they were relating there was an erasa. But that's not what we want. We want eternal relationship. We never get in this material world. So, we simply chase this thing in the material world. But we don't get it. So, actually Krishna consciousness is giving something that everybody is seeking. Isn't it amazing? Krishna consciousness is giving people something that they are all chasing after.

### foreground japa, background japa, subconscious mind
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/52GOqddWIj8?t=388
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Background is about feeding your subconscious with these truths and these truths transform into feelings and they become thought feelings or bhavanas."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa differentiates between foreground and background japa practices, explaining that foreground japa involves following Prabhupada's commands, while background japa involves feeding the subconscious with truths that transform into feelings.

### relationship ego, doer ego, consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/0ZKatj4q1Bg?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So this kind of a ego is called relationship ego, relationship ego."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa introduces the concept of relationship ego and doer ego, using the example of a teacher switching between different roles and moods.

Welcome all of you for the Soulful Japa summary session 2. So we are going to start today, explaining the first cue card. Every cue card is a feeling. The first cue card is also a feeling. But what kind of feeling is that? The feeling of being, being the servant of the holy name, being. Being is a potential of the soul. We have got, the soul has got the great facility or has got the great faculty. That's the feeling of being in relationship with different situations, being in relationship with different people, being in relationship with different situations. That is a fundamental thing in the consciousness. Whenever we are in relationship with somebody in order to be effective in that relationship, I gave an example earlier of a teacher. If the teacher in the classroom does not be in the feeling of a teacher, let's say the teacher also has, you know, let us say he is in the mood of his house, let's say he is playing with his children, so then he is in the mood of being the father. Being the father, when he goes before the classroom, when he goes into the classroom before the students, if he is in that mood of being the father, can he perform well? No. So he has to be a teacher in front of the students. So this kind of a ego is called relationship ego, relationship ego.

### relationship ego, doer ego, japa
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/0ZKatj4q1Bg?t=259
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the teacher example, introducing the concept of 'doer egos' and applying the relationship and doer ego framework to japa, emphasizing the importance of loading the 'Nama Sevak' ego during chanting.

And let us say he stands as a teacher before the students, as a teacher, he can do many things. One is, he can explain some concept to you. That means he is teaching. Another is, as a teacher, he can also ask you questions to test you, examiner. A teacher can be an examiner, a teacher can chastise a student, he can be a chastiser, he can be an examiner, he can be an explainer. Here I am leading towards some understanding. That is why I am putting these things before you. The first is he switches over from his father ego or a husband ego or let us say just before entering the classroom, he is talking to another colleague who is a teacher. So there is a colleague. There is a colleague ego, friendship ego. As soon as he enters the class, he switches over to his teacher ego. And as a teacher ego, he can do different things in the classroom. He can teach, he can explain, he can examine. He can chastise. So these are all called different doer egos. So first there is a relationship ego and there is a doer ego. This is there in anything. If you do not load your relationship ego and the respective doer ego, it is not possible for you to develop concentration to do something. It does not develop any meaning. Your doing does not develop a meaning. When you get on to a bicycle, there is a bicycle rider ego is there. Once that is loaded, then it knows how to handle the direction, the balance and all these things. So anyway, coming back to the teacher. This is a good example. So a teacher, a person can have different relationship egos. I can have a relationship ego with my employees. I can have a relationship ego with myself, employees I can have a relationship ego with my godbrothers I can have a relationship ego with my friends with my father with my mother with my children with my sisters with my brothers so many relationship egos are possible. Now remember we said japa is a relationship with the nama prabhu. Japa is a relationship with nama prabhu what is this relationship so we should define this relationship we should understand this relationship and then that relationship mind slice should be loaded that relationship ego and the mind slice of that relationship in relationship with the nama the japa how the the soul does japa that means there is a relationship ego that I am the servant of the nama prabhu a nama sava as much as the vishwa if it is the spirit of nama inward comment impossible.

So, the entire japa is about being in relationship with the Nama Prabhu. You should know your power bhavan shakti, being. And being in relationship means it's for a relationship. Relationship is for what? Relishing. relationship. And next two hours I am going to relish relationship with my Nama Prabhu. Just like you go into the classroom next, you know, one hour I am going to be, you know, I am going to relish teaching my students. Or let us say, a lover, he is meeting his loved one. Oh, next few hours I am so happy I am going to be in the association of the loved one. And he will be completely, he will be that lover and enjoy the relationship with the loved one. Of course, in our case, we enjoy serving the Nama Prabhu. I relish serving the Nama Prabhu. I relish calling out the name of the Lord. I relish feeling the unmanifest presence of the Lord. I relish the sound, the sound of the Shakti Avesha Nama, the empowered sound. I relish bead after bead giving priti, offering my priti to the Lord in reciprocation to his priti to have come down on my tongue. I relish my relationship with the Lord. I want to be in relationship with the Lord of, with the Lord Nama Prabhu of being helpless. I want to be in relationship of expressing my gratitude to Nama Prabhu. So it's all about being all 16 rounds is about being Nama Sevak. So the first round when you load your relationship with the Lord. When you load your relationship, you go that you are a Nama Sevak, your eternal Nama Sevak. Krishna is the infinite person. I am the infinitesimal person. See very important in japa, in the case of, in the case of the example I gave you, the teacher simply he's in bodily concept of life. I am a husband. Or I am, just before speaking to, getting into the classroom, his wife is, he's talking on his mobile to his wife and continuously last 15 minutes he's been talking. So he's in his husband being mode, cuts the call, then he goes inside, then he gets into a teacher being mode. Both these beings, if you see, the identity is I am the body. I am the body. I am the body. I am the body.

I am his infinitesimal person. Krishna is the supreme spirit soul. I am the atomic soul. I am not this body. I don't want to chant in, with the social concept of self. Madhu Pandit Das does not want to chant now 16 rounds. That ego is what the society has given me. The society looks at me like this, who am I myself? Most 90% of the time, our sense of identity comes from people who, how the world sees you. You also think you are that. How does the world see you? The world sees you as your body and you also think I am that. So this is called social ego. But when it comes to japa, it is not, we have to switch over from the social ego to nama sevak ego. Nama sevak ego and not bodily sevak, you are spirit soul. And Krishna is the supreme spirit soul. He is the supreme spirit soul. He is the supreme spirit soul. He is the supreme spirit soul. He is the supreme spirit soul. He is the supreme spirit soul. So Krishna is eternal. I am eternal and not this temporary body. Next, Krishna is like the fire. I am like a spark and not this body. This is an example that actually Chaitanya Mahaprabhu gives about this definition of soul. Soul is like a spark and Krishna is like a fire. Krishna is like a fire. Krishna is a supreme lord. I am his eternal servant. Krishna is in my heart. I am also in the heart of this body. So I am not in this body. I am not this body, but I am in this body. Krishna is with me. He is my eternal master. I am his eternal servant. And from that, I being the eternal servant, I have a relationship ego of relationship with Krishna's nama. And I know Krishna's nama is not different from Krishna himself. So he is before me and I am in relationship with nama as a nama sevak. And as a nama sevak, particularly I am in the japa doer ego mode. As a nama sevak, I can have different roles. I can do Sankirtana. I can write a module in soulful japa. I can do japa itself. I can preach the glories of the holy name to the people. So the nama sevak itself can have different doer functions, doer egos, secondary egos. First, I am the servant of Krishna. That is your real ego. Now the real ego takes on a relationship ego with the name of the Supreme Lord. So I am a nama sevak. And the nama sevak, particularly at japa time, he has to load the doer ego. And the doer ego is the concurrent chanting, come hearing ego, the mind slice. We have already trained that. It is already there. So unless these things, these are this is a mood. This is a being mood during japa. This is a being mood. Not that I am in the social concept of life, bodily concept of life. Not that I am in the social concept of life, bodily concept of life. Of course, the somebody is those who are not engaged in self-realization. By default, they don't know anything beyond their body. Doesn't matter, let them also chant Dharakrishna and begin. They begin like that. But eventually, we have to understand that the taste is when the real japa doer is a Nama Sevak. That means that being Rasa has to be there. We have to do japa with Rasa of being the Nama Sevak of the Lord. For that, the background is required. As I said, Krishna is eternal. I am eternal. Krishna is like the fire. Krishna is Supreme Master. Krishna is in my heart. Krishna is the Supreme Spirit. Krishna is infinite. I am infinitesimal Nama Sevak. So, the first round when you load your relationship, you go that you are a Nama Sevak.

Shraddha is a quality of the soul. So in this way. If you see, if you understand these things. That is why I call the first round as anchoring round. What is the key for anchoring? Key for anchoring is loading the relationship ego. If you are unable to load the relationship ego. That's all. If you are unable to load the relationship ego. That's all. It's like that. He may be less learning how to be a husband, a lot of does have mistakes and then he learns how to be a husband. But here we need to tell all these things because you know, it is an unseeable thing nama, but at the same time, he is present. So all this relationship are genuine. So be patient. Simply let him develop his chanting, his normal like how we do. But now you have come to a graduate level where you want to do soulful japa. And we know devotion service is that of the soul, not out of social ego you do devotional service. Social ego is I am this bodily concept, I am this body. And whatever I do is for the interest of the body or the extended body. But here I am not doing my japa for my bodily ego, social ego, I am doing it for my soul. When I do for my soul, automatically, the needs of the bodily ego, all these things are included in it. But if I do my japa for the benefit of my bodily ego. ### bhakti rasa, absolute thoughts, eternal relationship
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/0ZKatj4q1Bg?t=2257
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "We are talking about bhakti rasa. We are talking about eternal, absolute rasas."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the experience of relishable japa, where time flies due to the rasa, or exchange of bhavanas. He contrasts this bhakti rasa with illusory material rasas, emphasizing that bhakti rasa is based on absolute thoughts and the philosophy of Krishna consciousness.

If you don't look at your watch, you are not habituated to look at the watch. You will feel that, in half an hour I finished, 15 minutes, half an hour I finished 16 rounds. That is a sense of time. When something is relishable, time flies. Just like two people in love. They talk and they talk and they talk and they talk and they talk and they talk from morning till night. And they don't realize, my God, whole day we have been talking. Or whole night we have been talking. Especially these mobiles now. Talk, talk, talk, talk. You don't feel time going. Why? Because rasa is there. In the same way, when there is rasa, rasa means what? Bhavanas. When there is exchange of bhavanas in japa, how time flies, you will not know. And this is not some stupid rasa of some girlfriend, boyfriend, all these things. They are all illusory rasas. We are talking about bhakti rasa. We are talking about eternal, absolute rasas. Rasas that come, each of these rasas that we are talking about, 16 rasas, they are coming out of absolute thoughts, of philosophy of Krishna consciousness, absolute thoughts of Krishna, of his devotee, Krishna's glories. You know, all these things, it is coming from the source of these bhavanas, are guru, sadhu and shastra. These are not concorded feelings. When a feeling is out of concorded thoughts, it is called concorded feeling. Feeling is feeling. But if the base is illusion, then it is called illusion feeling. Just like feeling. I love somebody in this material world. I love this feeling. But the foundation of that is what? I am this body. She is this body. And then I love her. You love her. You love me. So, love experience is that of the soul. But it is false. Because it is not based on absolute thoughts. You are neither the lover, neither the other one is loved. It is only a drama. As long as she is in that body, she is my lover. As long as I am in this body, I am her lover. But this is not like that. Therefore, the Namasevak is not this body, not this social bodily ego, not this social ego. The Namasevak is the eternal ego. I am the eternal spirit soul. I am eternal servant of the supreme spirit soul. It is a relationship between the tiny spirit soul and the supreme spirit soul. It is a relationship between the tiny person and infinitesimal person and the infinite person. It is a relationship between the maintainer and the maintained. It is a relationship between the provider and the provided. It is a relationship between the master and the servant. It is a relationship of The one who is surrendering and who receives the surrender. It's a relationship of the helpless person and the saviour. And all these are eternal relationship.

---

This is an eternal relationship. You cannot say, none of us can say, this life we are chanting, first time you are chanting Hare Krishna Mahamantra. Maybe you are all people, we are all people who just got some little darshan of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu 500 years back. Lord Chaitanya walked all over India, Supreme Personality of God had walked all over India. That is how golden period, golden age is going to come. All the devotees, half chanting, three-fourth chanting, whatever it is, all the devotees, they will all continue their journey and their desires and therefore this world will be filled with preachers of Krishna consciousness, generation after generation it's going to multiply. Vaishnavism is going to spread, Krishna consciousness is going to spread. That is how this golden period is, golden period of predicted by Lord Chaitanya is going to come. Life after life Lord Chaitanya is preparing us. 500 years back, he started his mission through the parampara, he is training so many. So today, by Prabhupada's mercy how many people all over the world are chanting their 16 malas. They all have a desire to spread Krishna consciousness, they all want to see Krishna consciousness, they want to see the spread the glories of Krishna consciousness, it's going to multiply. 20 years from now, 50 years from now, it's going to multiply. People think, oh, I don't have money. Why is it that devotees, you know, are all struggling for money? They don't have money to preach Krishna, the money there, so much can be done. It's a fact. If you have such a desire, next life you will be another Bill Gates, Vaishnava Bill Gates. You will say all my money I am dedicating. What's the problem? I am dedicating. I am dedicating. I am spreading Krishna consciousness. Let there be a temple in every village, every town. I am ready. So you will get the seva opportunity. This is a wonderful thing. Nama Leela, Nama Seva Kriya. That is why I said in the beginning of Soulful Japa, we are very fortunate to be born in this particular period, in this particular Kali Yuga. After Krishna appears, Lord Chaitanya appears. We are actually contemporaries of Lord Chaitanya and Lord Krishna, we are contemporaries. In the eternal time scale, 5000 years back is nothing. Okay, yesterday Krishna disappeared, today I was born. All initiates of mind must strictly follow the four principles of no meat, no fish or egg eating, no gambling, no intoxication of any kind and no illicit sex. Now next is very important. They must chant 16 rounds a day minimum. With attention, clearly chanting the mantra and listening very carefully. This is recommended process for purifying the heart. Then Abhyasa Yodha. You must, you practice this. Therefore you must chant 16 rounds. This is Abhyasa Yodha. When one becomes attached to chanting he doesn't require to be under discipline. But so long he is not practiced he must be disciplined.

### attention, clear chanting, careful hearing

In the Bhagavad Gita, Krishna says, Now what is the soul's involvement in all these things? Bhagavad Gita, Krishna in the 13th chapter says that the soul is the Kshetrajna. Kshetrajna means he is a knower of the field of activities. And Kshetra is a field of activities. So Kshetra and Kshetrajna. We are all trying to find out who am I? I am the Kshetrajna. I am the one who knows this body. I am the one who knows this mind. The knower of the mind, knower of the body is me. The body and the mind is not me.

## Special Lecture on Soulful Japa | Sri Madhu Pandit Dasa | 26.12.2024
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

### japa, mindfulness, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/v0Yn8K-s-LY?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When you do japa, by putting our total self, to chant and hear and worship the Lord's holy name, that is soulful japa."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa introduces the concept of soulful japa, contrasting it with mechanical and mindful japa, emphasizing the importance of engaging the heart with feelings of appreciation, gratitude, and love towards the Lord.

So, before we continue with our japa yajna, I will give an orientation on doing soulful japa. Soulful japa. When you say soulful, we have our body, we have our mind, we have our soul. When you do japa, by putting our total self, to chant and hear and worship the Lord's holy name, that is soulful japa. If you give only your tongue, that is mechanical japa. If you give your mind to hear the sound, that is called mindful japa. If you give your heart, along with the tongue, mind, you give your heart to chant and hear with feelings towards Nama Prabhu, feelings of appreciation, feelings of gratitude, feeling of love. Then, that is called soulful japa.

---
### japa, mindfulness, progress
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/v0Yn8K-s-LY?t=118
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If you do mindful japa, you will automatically advance to heartful, soulful japa."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that training the mind to hear the chanting is crucial for progressing from mechanical to mindful japa, and that mindful japa naturally leads to soulful japa, while mindless japa is offensive and leads to stagnation.

So, if we don't train our mind to hear what we chant, we do not even advance from mechanical japa to mindful japa. Mechanical japa to mindful japa. Mechanical japa, once you learn how to do, your tongue can finish sixteen rounds and your mind can be roaming everywhere. If you have some problem, particular problems, let's say some serious problems, that day, then you can chant sixteen rounds with your tongue. Your mind will be solving those problems, thinking of that, this, that. Or if you have some emotional showdown with someone, then that will not go away from the mind. Mind will be thinking, why did I do that, why did I, what did he do, she do, not fair, this thing, that thing, all those things because emotion comes. Doesn't stop analyzing, then gone, you are not able to hear. And if somehow or the other, we train ourselves for mindful japa, which is meaning to hear. Mindful japa means, already shabda has gone inside, through the ears. After quite some time, if you go on doing, mindless japa, if you do mindless mechanical japa, that will not, there is no guarantee that you will go to the next level. Whole life you can be doing that. But if you do mindful japa, you will automatically advance to heartful, soulful japa.

---
### japa, mindfulness, offense
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/v0Yn8K-s-LY?t=264
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If you do mindless japa, because mindless japa is offensive japa. You are neglecting the Lord."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa emphasizes that mechanical japa does not automatically lead to soulful japa, as it can be offensive due to neglecting the Lord, while mindful japa allows for the natural development of feelings for Krishna and surrender.

It is not that you do mechanical japa, one day you will do mindful japa. And you go on doing mindful japa, one day you will do soulful japa, after purification. No. If you do mindless japa, because mindless japa is offensive japa. You are neglecting the Lord. There is no question of, you will stagnate there. If you do mindful japa, then soulful japa will automatically rise up, when your feelings for Krishna is awakened in your heart gradually. Feelings of surrender, all those things. So in, what is the difference between mindful japa and mechanical japa? In mindful japa, mind is there. In mechanical japa, mindless, mindlessness. There is no mind at all in chanting and hearing. Automatic, mechanical. Anything you repeat, you make your body repeat, it learns. So it is, mindless japa is impersonal japa. Actually, japa is a relationship. With the naam avatara. So bead after bead, every bead is a relationship with the sound incarnation of the Lord. He is before us. So mindless means, we are neglecting him.

---
### japa, mindfulness, hearing
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/v0Yn8K-s-LY?t=370
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Chanting means, invoking the incarnation. Hearing means you are taking the incarnation into your heart."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that mindful japa involves chanting in the same sound one hears, as instructed by Srila Prabhupada, and understanding that the Lord incarnates into that sound, making it Shakti Avesha Nama, where chanting invokes the incarnation and hearing takes it into the heart with respect, appreciation, gratitude, delight, surrender, humility, and a begging for mercy.

So mindful japa means, each bead, you chant in exactly the same sound you hear. And this is the diamond command of, golden command of Srila Prabhupada. Chant Hare Krishna in exactly the same sound you hear. Also you should understand that when we chant, the Lord incarnates into that sound and it becomes Shakti Avesha Nama. Shakti Avesha, Avesha means entered. It becomes Shakti Avesha sound. So chanting means, invoking the incarnation. Hearing means you are taking the incarnation into your heart. Hearing respectfully, hearing appreciatively, hearing with gratitude, hearing with delight, hearing with surrender, hearing with begging forgiveness, hearing with feeling his shelter, foremost hearing with humility, hearing with begging for mercy, Kripa Apeksha, hearing with kindness, hearing with kindness. That is the, then it is soulful japa because your soul is involving in feeling the presence of the Lord before you, feeling his mercy, feeling his compassion, feeling appreciation for the Lord's merciful appearance before you, for your association.

---
### japa, commitment, obstacles
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/v0Yn8K-s-LY?t=498
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The key to mindful japa is to ensure that you are hearing the chanting."
**Summary:** The speaker acknowledges the value of mechanical japa as a commitment to one's spiritual master, but emphasizes that Prabhupada wants devotees to progress to pure devotional service, which requires feeling Krishna's presence; the key to mindful japa is hearing the chanting, and the main obstacle is thinking and thought processing.

So this mechanical japa to just finish sixteen rounds, it's okay. At least there is one commitment I have committed to my spiritual master, I should finish sixteen rounds. But Prabhupada does not want us to always remain neophytes. He wants us to learn how to offer pure devotional service. Okay, in mechanical japa, as far as Krishna is concerned, we are not even feeling his presence. At least if you feel commitment to Srila Prabhupada, I am obeying Srila Prabhupada, so that much bhakti is there, even in mechanical japa. No, no, I have not finished my rounds. Tuck, tuck, tuck, tuck, you finish mechanically. There is something there. So, God will bless you and Prabhupada will bless you. But Prabhupada will not expect, Prabhupada expects you to go beyond inattentive japa. So, the key to mindful japa is to ensure that you are hearing the chanting. And what is the obstacle for hearing the chanting? Thinking, thought processing. When you are thinking, within the mind you are chattering. Mind and intelligence are discussing.

---
### mindfulness, hearing, chanting
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/v0Yn8K-s-LY?t=617
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Chanting is a doing. Hearing is a knowing."
**Summary:** Thinking involves the mind and intelligence iterating through thoughts, which are subtle sounds that interfere with hearing the chanting; attentive chanting involves giving attention to knowing the sound of the mantra, where chanting is a doing and hearing is a knowing.

What is thinking? You throw up, mind throws up one option. Intelligence will analyze. No, no, not that. So, permutation combination. You are going on iterating your thoughts. That is, the... Because it is a tasking activity, you are tasking something. You are tasking to solve a problem or you are tasking to understand the philosophy, all these things. When you are tasking, then your mind is involved in tasking, so it cannot hear. And thoughts are nothing but subtle sound. That means if you hear those sounds, then you cannot hear this sound. So, attentive, giving attention to knowing the sound that you are chanting, Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare. As each shabda, there are only three shabdas, Hare Krishna and Rama in the Mahamantra. As these shabdas, the speed with which you chant, you should catch them by hearing them and sending inside, by knowing this sound. Hearing means what? Chanting means you move your tongue. In hearing, what you do? It is knowing. It's a jnana indriya. Hearing is not a doing. Not a doing. Hearing is to know this sound. Now Hare is on my tongue, means continuously be aware, Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama. To know the sound. Know the sound with feeling, with appreciation, with all the different bhavanas. So chanting is a doing. Hearing is a knowing.

---
### mindfulness, hearing, tasking
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/v0Yn8K-s-LY?t=759
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Stop all tasking. Task only repetition."
**Summary:** Hearing also involves ensuring that no other hearing or tasking is done, as the mind's nature is to task; one should stop all tasking and only task repetition of the mantra, piling up hearing over hearing without thinking in the gaps, which has a powerful effect on consciousness.

And also a doing because you have to ensure that no other hearing is there. Only one particular knowing and a particular knowing and not any other activity should be done and tasking should be done. Mind's business, mind's task is mind's nature is to task. Every moment we are always tasking something. Stop all tasking. Task only repetition. Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare, Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare. You should pile up just like you know if you have a brick on your hand. You have brick on another hand. The pressure on each hand is one brick load. Suppose instead of the second brick, I put the second brick on the same hand. Third brick on the same hand. What happens? Weight is more. Weight is more, right? Similarly, if you pile up hearing over hearing, that means without any thinking in the gap, hearing over hearing over hearing over stacking, stacking one brick over the other like that. Hearing over hearing over hearing will have fantastic effect of like iron put in the fire, becomes red hot. Our consciousness, you can immediately, immediate effect it is.

### hearing, chanting, experience
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/v0Yn8K-s-LY?t=882
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "If you teach them how to stack up and chant, even a new person, first time chanting, if he does that without thinking, hearing, hearing, hearing, hearing, hearing, hearing, he will be shocked."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the immediate effect of Hare Krishna chanting is often not felt because people are not taught to hear properly; stacking up hearing while chanting, even for a new person, can lead to a profound experience that makes them want to chant every day, touching Krishna's sound incarnation.

Sometimes people say, like Hare Krishna chanting, you are not able to give people any immediate feeling effect. Just like somebody gives some some yogic process, so immediately they are feeling some peace, immediately something. Here also, because we don't teach people to hear, that is why they don't get immediate effect. If you teach them how to stack up and chant, even a new person, first time chanting, if he does that without thinking, hearing, hearing, hearing, hearing, hearing, hearing, he will be shocked. He will get such an experience that he would want to chant every day. And by such soulful japa, you are, you are touching the holy name, touching Krishna's sound incarnation. Krishna, who is before us is Krishna, himself is before us. If you want Krishna's form before us, you need to qualify so much. The sound name of Krishna is very merciful, so therefore, Krishna himself is there, but it's in the name form. So in the name form, if we worship, slowly we get purified, then we even see all other aspects of Krishna. Krishna is not only name aspect, Krishna has form aspect, Krishna has got quality aspect, Krishna has got past time aspect, they are all Krishna. All that will be revealed from the, by Nama Prabhu.

### nama, chinmaya, sound
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/v0Yn8K-s-LY?t=999
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna's name is Krishna's consciousness."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the holy name is called Nama Prabhu because it is Chinmaya Shabda, a conscious sound, unlike the Jada Shabda of the material world; Prabhupada's lectures and the Bhagavad Gita are also Chinmaya Shabda, and Krishna's name is Krishna's consciousness.

Why we call this Nama, Nama Prabhu? Because this sound is Chinmaya Shabda. There is Chinmaya Shabda and there is Jada Shabda. All the Shabdas in this world are Jada Shabda. There is no person involved in that sound. But when it comes to sound that let's say Prabhupada speaks, it's Chinmaya Shabda. Today you hear Prabhupada's lecture, Prabhupada is not separated from that. Eternally, he himself is speaking. Anytime you hear him, he is speaking as he gave the lecture, when he was sitting and giving lecture pre-1977. It is all Chinmaya Shabda. Bhagavad Gita, Krishna spoke, it's Chinmaya Shabda. Krishna's name is Chinmaya Shabda. Chinmaya, in normal thing you can say, it's conscious sound. But whose consciousness is it? Krishna's name is Krishna's consciousness. So this is a very primary understanding that should have Shraddha.

---
### nama, chanting, service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/v0Yn8K-s-LY?t=1116
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Doing japa is not only I am chanting, Lord himself is, he is sounding that."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of having Shraddha, faith, that Krishna himself is coming in the sound form and sounding the mantra; chanting is done in service of the Nama, and Krishna is also sounding it, making it a joint effort, and that some people cannot chant Krishna's name due to their offenses.

Shraddha that this is Krishna himself coming in the sound form. Krishna himself is sounding. Now I am sounding this if you take sounding as a verb, I am sounding this sound. I am sounding, therefore this sound is coming out. So in japa, we are actually chanting. Our tongue is chanting. But actually Krishna is mercifully, he is sounding it. That is why in some people's tongue, Krishna Nama does not come because of their offense. So here we are chanting in service of the Nama and Krishna is also sounding. Krishna sounding and Krishna is no different. So we have to always have minimum Shraddha that this is Krishna himself. Roopa Krishna is Purna Krishna. Commonly we understand, okay, okay, okay, now only sound has come. But I saw Krishna. That you feel more complete. This is our conditioning. As much Purna the Roopa Krishna is, that much Purna is Shabda Krishna. We are, we are invoking Shabda Krishna. We are associating with Shabda Krishna. It's Krishna himself. So fortunate. Because if you have that Shraddha, then automatically you will be jubilant in hearing, in jubilant in embracing each sound. You will be delightfully chanting, oh my god, just chanting means Krishna is appearing on my tongue. How much delightful I should be. So one should delightfully chant, joyfully hear. When you realize that Krishna has come on my tongue, on my tongue, my god, himself has come on my tongue to give me association. Automatically you will feel his priti. I am such a insignificant soul. I have got this chance to do japa. As I said, doing japa is not only I am chanting, Lord himself is, he is sounding that.

### nama, transcendental, mercy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/v0Yn8K-s-LY?t=1255
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna gives you the credit for chanting and hearing his Shuddha Nama."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the sound we chant and the Nama from the Lord enter, becoming transcendental sound, Shakti Avesha Nama; Krishna receives service to Shakti Avesha Nama as if it were service to Shuddha Nama, giving the devotee credit for chanting and hearing His Shuddha Nama.

Sound that we chant and the nama from the Lord, he enters, Avesha. What enters? The transcendental sound enters. Just like in a marble deity or something. Marble deity is made of matter. But, Sachidananda Lord enters. So similarly, the Lord enters in the shabda that we make, that we chant and then it becomes transcendental sound. Shakti Avesha Nama. Shakti Avesha Nama is what we and Krishna says, Krishna, the principle is that, if Shuddha Nama had come before you, if you chant and serve, you glorify by singing Hare Krishna, Krishna Nama, Shuddha Nama. How he receives your seva, the same way Krishna is receiving exactly just by Shakti Avesha Nama, by serving the sound that you hear audibly with your ears. Krishna gives you the credit for chanting and hearing his Shuddha Nama. Just like how Krishna, you offer a garland to the deity.

### faith, sound, soulful japa
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/v0Yn8K-s-LY?t=1371
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This is Krishna's sound body, Shabdavatara, sound body."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the need for faith that Krishna takes the garland offered to the visible deity as if it were offered to Him in the spiritual world; the sound heard is not ordinary but Chinmaya, and while the spirit part is unseen, it can be experienced; this is Krishna's sound body, Shabdavatara, with Krishna's transcendental soul in the sound.

You are offering the garland to the deity, but the Sachidananda, it is as good as, because Krishna has decided, I will become this. That faith is required. I offer garland to the visible deity, the deity visible with my eyes. And what is Krishna's promise? I will take that as you are offering to me, Sachidananda in the spiritual world. That is where faith is required, that this is not just the ordinary sound that I am hearing with my ears, but it is a Chinmaya sound. The spirit part of it you don't see, but you will start experiencing. Just like I see your body, but I don't see a spirit. But I still see though, this is, there is a person in this body. And this is Krishna's sound body, Shabdavatara, sound body. In that sound body, Krishna's transcendental sound is a transcendental soul in the sound. So, rapid progress you make in realizing Krishna consciousness, different aspects of Krishna consciousness, if you start with serious mindful japa, and then gradually it will awaken our and plus when you are reading Prabhupada's book, all those things, then in soulful japa, you can start doing soulful japa. Now itself, because we are devotees of the Lord, so much of Prabhupada's books we have studied, it's not that now we can only do mindful japa, we can also do soulful japa immediately.

What is this chanting? What benefit it gives? And when you are having that attitude towards the holy name, towards the sound, naturally, the holy name is pleased. Nama Prabhu is pleased. Oh, this devotee knows who I am on this tongue… on his tongue. He is respectfully receiving me. He is not thinking of something else while I am… while he is chanting. And he understands this avatara. I have come down into this sound that uttered by the material tongue. Into that eye, he is entering. No, he understands the principles. He is feeling my presence. ### japa, bhavanas, mercy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/v0Yn8K-s-LY?t=1580
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When you are chanting and you have this bhavanas, then Nama is called Nama Chintamani. He will fulfill our desires."
**Summary:** The speaker encourages devotees to approach japa with Shraddha, Preeti, Sambhanda, Krishnam, and Haglada, using the sixteen bhavanas provided in cards, offering one on each bead; chanting with these bhavanas makes the Nama Chintamani, fulfilling desires such as better japa and feeling Krishna's presence, and begging for costless mercy to experience Krishna realistically.

So, we should do Shraddha Japa, Preeti Japa, Sambhanda Japa. Just these small small words are there so that to remind us. Krishnam Japa, Haglada Japa, delightfully chant. The sixteen… for sixteen malas, sixteen different main bhavanas are are given in the… in this cards. So, we… we know those things. In each bead, one of them you offer. Oh, I should delightfully chant. Why delightfully? Krishna himself is before. I feel joyfully here. Oh, why? Because Krishna is touching my heart. Joyfully here. Enthusiastically repeat. Why enthusiasm? Oh, accumulate one more, one more, one more, just like a greedy man. More money, more money, more money, more money, more money. I am becoming richer and richer. In the same mood, one more bead, one more bead, one more bead, one more mala, one more mala. Enthusiasm, enthusiasm, enthusiasm. Sambhanda, sam. Every bead is a relationship of surrender and service. Like that different Kripa Apeksha, begging for costless mercy. When you are chanting and you have this bhavanas, then Nama is called Nama Chintamani. He will fulfill our desires. And what is our desire is to do better and better japa. What is our desire is to let me feel Krishna himself. Let me… I know theoretically, philosophically, I know this is Krishna. But I beg for your costless mercy to make me feel exactly Krishna. Just like I am sitting with another person, how realistically I feel this is another person sitting before me. When will I feel like that? Krishna can make us, give us that experience. Kripa Apeksha. All kinds of mercy Nama Prabhu can give. Nama Prabhu can give us detachment from this stupid illusory love, illusory pleasures. He can give Divya Gyan, Nama Prabhu. That means shines transcendental knowledge, who you are, what you are not. All this shines in our heart by the mercy of the Nama Prabhu. So let's continue our 64 rounds japa yajna for the Sekadashi. So morning we started at 6, now we have… From 12th we will continue. Nama Prabhu ki. Shilpa Prabhu paadiki.

## Sri Narasimha Jayanti 2024 | Message by Sri Madhu Pandit Dasa @madhupanditdasaofficial
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

### protection, gratitude, bhakti
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/P_myFEFAUDw?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "By always remembering Lord Narasimha as a protective feature of the Supreme Lord, and surrendering unto him, this is the best way that we can express our gratitude to Narasimha."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes remembering Lord Narasimha as a protector and expressing gratitude through surrender.

The devotees always feel the Supreme Lord is continuously protecting him, especially protecting him from all different dangers and obstacles that hinder his bhakti for the Lord. So, by always remembering Lord Narasimha as a protective feature of the Supreme Lord, who is all-pervading, who is seated in everybody's heart, ito Narasimha, parato Narasimha, eto eto yami, tato Narasimha, bahe Narasimha, rideye Narasimha, Narasimha madhim, sharanam prapadye, and surrendering unto him, this is the best way that we can express our gratitude to Narasimha.

---
### mercy, protection, divine nature
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/P_myFEFAUDw?t=51
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Such is a merciful Narasimha."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the constant and often unseen protection offered by Lord Narasimha and his merciful nature.

By acknowledging his constant protection. And his protection comes in so many ways which we are not even aware of. We may be aware of only few things which manifest to our consciousness, but the Lord being kind, in unlimited ways, even the devotee who is being protected does not know, in unlimited ways he is constantly protecting the devotees. Such is a merciful Narasimha.

And if we remember each one of these merciful incidences of reciprocation of the Lord, the Lord will be very pleased. And if we remember each one of these merciful incidences of reciprocation of the Lord, the Lord will be very pleased. And if we remember each one of these merciful incidences of reciprocation of the Lord, the Lord will be very pleased. And our relationship grows stronger and stronger.

### dependence, surrender, faith
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/P_myFEFAUDw?t=259
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The symptom of complete surrender is complete dependence upon the Lord and giving up all other dependence having faith that nothing protects me other than Lord Narasimha."
**Summary:** This segment discusses how remembering the Lord's reciprocation increases dependence on Him, leading to complete surrender and faith in Lord Narasimha as the sole protector.

The way the devotees can take the maximum benefit in their journey, spiritual journey, benefit out of these reciprocations, is by remembering these reciprocation contemplating how merciful the Lord was. And in this way, our connection, our relationships become stronger and stronger and that there's nothing that pleases Lord Narasimha more than the devotee constantly thinking of him and the devotee as he goes on you know experiencing the reciprocation of Lord Narasimha his dependence upon the Lord goes on increasing more and more and the symptom of complete surrender is complete dependence upon the Lord and giving up all other dependence having faith that nothing protects me other than Lord Narasimha no faith in money no faith in anything else in this world but faith that Lord Narasimha alone is my Rakshaka he is called the Bhakta Rakshaka.

## Srimad Bhagavatam 2.9.31 Lecture by HG Stoka Krishna Prabhu
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

### transcendental form, opulence of the Lord, eternality
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YNXeOFBjf0Q?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This is actually an opulence of the Lord, that you cannot limit him to just one form."
**Summary:** This segment describes the unlimited and identical nature of the Lord's transcendental forms, emphasizing that they are eternal and beyond our current comprehension.

So in the purport of Rampal, he is describing something about each of these personal attributes of the Lord. In this paragraph that I just now read, he is discussing or describing the transcendental form of the bodies of the Lord. He is an unlimited person, so he has unlimited forms. But all the forms are identical with one another. This is difficult for us to conceive, that there is one person with many forms, all simultaneously existing and all identical with each other. The same person, but he is present as Rama, Narasimha, Varaha, Krishna, Matsya. All identical, one Supreme Lord, but he is present in many different forms. This is actually an opulence of the Lord, that you cannot limit him to just one form. Neither he becomes split personality when he expands or when he is present in many forms. He is still the same person, still the same Supreme Lord. His qualities or his activities or his powers do not reduce when he expands, when he is present in many forms. And these forms are not temporary forms, they are all eternal forms. So we cannot understand, in our present condition, what is the actual nature of this form of God.

And while hearing, we try to keep aside all our material conceptions. Material conceptions of the form means, a form is a limitation. Transcendental form means there is no limitation. Transcendental form is the body. There is no limitation. So the form actually has got unlimited power, unlimited varieties, unlimited love, unlimited qualities. But still, the form is something which, when it is revealed, it becomes known to the devotee and it may appear like an ordinary form. Just like the form of Krishna. Now Krishna appears in Vrindavan like a cowherd boy. So, people may think, is this a form of God? Cowherd boy? Even big big devatas, including Brahma, they will doubt whether this is the form of God or it is the form of somebody else.

### Brahma's mystic power, Hiranyakashipu, boon
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YNXeOFBjf0Q?t=282
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So that is Brahma's mystic power."
**Summary:** This segment describes Brahma's mystic power, illustrating it with the example of how he reconstructed Hiranyakashipu's body and granted him a boon, leading to Hiranyakashipu becoming very powerful.

So Brahma knew that actually that form of Krishna as cowherd boy is actually God's form. But still he wanted to test. So he performed one trick. He used his mystic power. Brahma's mystic power is among the greatest in this material world. So Brahma used his mystic power. What is the power of Brahma's mystic power? Any example you can think of? Brahma demonstrates his mystic power sometimes. No example you can think of. Of course, while testing Krishna, he used his mystic power. That you all know. But any other example? When Hiranyakashipu was doing tapasya, what did Brahma come and do? He sprinkled some water. Hiranyakashipu's body was eaten away by ants. It was reduced to bones. The skin, the flesh, everything was finished. Just he sprinkled some water and Hiranyakashipu's body became fully reconstructed. Full luster. So that is Brahma's mystic power. That is Brahma's mystic power. So Brahma gave Hiranyakashipu the boon that you will not die in the day or night. You cannot be killed by human. You cannot be killed by human. You cannot be killed by devata. You cannot be killed by asura. You cannot be killed by any insect or reptile or animal. So Hiranyakashipu became very, very, very, very powerful. So that is Brahma's mystic power. That is Brahma's mystic power. He is very, very powerful. That Brahma is trying to see who is his covered body.

### Brahma testing Krishna, mystic power, cowherd boys, karmas
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YNXeOFBjf0Q?t=428
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So Brahma used his mystic power to test Krishna."
**Summary:** This segment narrates how Brahma tested Krishna's divinity by using his mystic power to steal the cowherd boys and calves (karmas), putting them to sleep and hiding them, but Krishna replicated them, leaving Brahma bewildered.

Trying to test who is his covered body. So what did he do? What did Brahma do to test Krishna? First he took away the karmas, which Krishna had brought for grazing. Along with the karmas he also brought karmas for grazing. And they were a big number. They were not small numbers. Very big number. Number is not mentioned in the Bhagavad Gita. They were a very big number. In Jetani Jetanatha I have made an idea of number. I will tell that later. So Brahma used his mystic power. And while Krishna was enjoying his lunch with the other covered boys, that time Brahma took away the karmas. He stole them. And suddenly one of the covered boys is seeing, Oh, the karmas are not there. Where has it gone? Not even one is visible. So they all started dying. So Krishna knows what is happening. But just to play along with Brahma, Krishna said, told the covered boys, Don't worry. You continue eating your lunch. I will just go look around and bring them back. They just strayed away. This is gone away. Looking for some fresh grass here and there. This is gone away. So Krishna went away, apparently in searching the karmas. Meantime, when Krishna had gone away, Brahma came a second time and took away the covered boys. What did Brahma do? He put those karmas into a sleeping condition and took them away. Next he put the boys into a sleeping condition and took them away. And he is hiding them and coming back and seeing what is happening. So you can imagine how powerful is Brahma. Brahma's mystic power. So powerful. He can put them to sleep and hide them and touch a big number. Never a very big number. Not a small number. He is coming back and seeing all the covered boys and the karmas are as they are in Krishna. So Brahma is giving them away. He is using his mystic power to test Krishna. Brahma is giving them away. And he is trying to see whether the covered boys and karmas are the same or they are different. And Brahma is seeing they are the same, identical. So then he thought for a moment that Krishna has somehow done some other used some other mystic power and brought them back from where Brahma had hidden them. But actually Brahma could see that there are two sets. Not one set. Two sets of covered boys and karmas. All identical. So he has hidden them. They are there. And here Krishna is also hiding. So how is this possible? Brahma is thinking how is it possible?

### yogi's power, Krishna's expansion, independence, Yogeshwara Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YNXeOFBjf0Q?t=673
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** This segment contrasts the limited power of a yogi to expand into identical forms with Krishna's ability to expand into different forms that act independently, highlighting Krishna's supreme power as Yogeshwara.

And not only that because a yogi can expand himself into some other forms up to nine or ten forms. And all forms will look identical to the yogi. But the yogi's mystic power is such that if in one form he lifts his hand all the nine forms the hand will lift it. It's like television screen. There are multiple screens. In the studio like telecast is going on in the studio one actor's person is lifting the hand. In the television screen same action will happen. Correct? All the television screens same action will happen. No independence. Similarly yogi when he expands all the forms will be doing the same action. These are the children they call for independence they march the drill. Everybody will do like this. Like this. Like this. The drill master will keep like this. One, two, three, four. Something like this. They all lift their hands. Correct? Identically. Looks very nice. So like that a yogi cannot independently do different things in different forms. Not possible. Krishna when he expands his forms need not look identical. His forms can look all different. And each form can act independently. So the cowherd boys are playing with Krishna. The cows are there grazing on the field in the forest and they are all independently doing their own thing. Not all are same action. No. So they are Brahma realists. No. This is not the power of a yogi that this cowherd boy is displaying. He is actually my master. Yogeshwara Krishna. He is Yogeshwara Krishna master. So he is able to display this power which is not possible for any yogi. It's not possible even for me. So he is Krishna. He is God.

Very few people knew that this form of Krishna is actually the form of God or Krishna is God. Very few people knew that. Most people did not know that. So even if you see Krishna's form even if you see God's form you will see the way you are seeing here of Krishna and Dharna. Being a Krishna and Dharna what do people think? Statue made of metal. They think it is a statue made of metal and devotees have never heard of it. The statue. But this is not a statue. It is actually Sakshat Bhagwan Krishna. Now how can we understand how can we realize this form actually is not a statue. The same way how Brahma can realize the form of Krishna or Krishna. It is not an ordinary form. How Brahma can realize? When Krishna gives the realization out of Krishna's own mercy. ### devotion, Bhakti, personal relationship, love, reciprocation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YNXeOFBjf0Q?t=1172
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So if there is love, a loving relationship in which one is trying to please the other, then there is possibility that the other person will reciprocate."
**Summary:** This segment explains that to realize Krishna's spiritual form, one must become a devotee and practice Bhakti, developing a loving, personal relationship with Krishna, where both devotee and Krishna are ready to please each other.

So if you also want to see Krishna or you want to realize this form as spiritual form you should actually become a devotee. You should practice Bhakti and that Bhakti should be such that you should please Krishna by your devotion. There is a certain amount of personal relationship involved in this. Personal relationship. It is like in ordinary beings also we try to please a person. Especially mother tries to please the child or husband tries to please the wife. Right? It is more than just simply some activity or some relationship. It is more than that. So what is that special thing about trying to please another person? It is love. That is what we call love. So if there is love a loving relationship in which one is trying to please the other then there is possibility that the other person will reciprocate. And in that reciprocation there is no consideration of who is greater who is smaller who is demanding what. There is no such consideration. There is no calculation being done. There is no mother loves her child. The mother will take care of the child. The child will pass through a neuron. The mother will not think why I should clean? Stupid child. There is no business. Whole day only eating, sleeping, passing urine, passing stool. That's all. What does the child do whole day? Especially small child. Put him there. What does he do? Only do this business. Mother keeps on. Never counts how many times I have done. How long I have to do this. Mother never thinks. So, like that Prabhupada explains when a devotee develops love for Krishna in a loving relationship devotee is prepared to do anything for Krishna in order to please Krishna. And Krishna reciprocally is ready to do anything for a devotee to please the devotee. That is Krishna.

### Bhagavatam process, hearing, qualification, technique of hearing, spiritual benefit
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YNXeOFBjf0Q?t=2031
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The special thing about hearing is, it doesn't require any qualification."
**Summary:** This segment explains that hearing is the easiest of the nine processes of devotion and does not require any qualification, and that the technique of hearing is developed by hearing itself, giving the example of Prabhupada reciting the tenth canto of the Bhagavatam.

So like this, Bhagavatam process especially is expressed in the Bhagavatam. You should hear the devotion. All the things here is the easiest. Other things are not so easy. Remembering, serving the lotus feet, acting as the servant of the Lord like Hanuman did. All that, not easy. Hearing is the easiest. It doesn't require any qualification. The special thing about hearing is, it doesn't require any qualification. Will I read? Anything else requires qualification. You have to speak, or you have to serve the lotus feet, or you have to remember. It requires qualification. Whereas hearing is the only thing among the nine processes that does not require any qualification. But there is a quality to the hearing. The technique of hearing. And for that Bhagavatam says, even the technique of hearing, you develop by hearing only. Simply sit and hear, that's all. In the early days, Prabhupada was travelling. You forgot to put in the belt. Okay, two hours. I'll come close with this example. Prabhupada was travelling with some disciples. And it was Janmashtami. Early days. They were not observing him. Because the temple had not yet been started. So Prabhupada told the disciples, some few devotees, You simply hear, I will recite the tenth canto of the Bhagavatam, which describes specially the birth of Krishna. Because today is the Janmashtami, the birthday of Krishna, appearance and worship. So they were all hearing. They can't understand anything. Prabhupada is reading his answer. Prabhupada said, whether you understand or don't understand, simply by hearing, you get full spiritual benefit. You get full spiritual benefit. So, hearing doesn't require any qualification. You simply have to lend your ear. That's all. And whether you understand or don't understand, the spiritual benefit is there. And, in the beginning, maybe you don't understand. But as you go on hearing, hearing, hearing, hearing, hearing, everything will be understood. Everything will be revealed. Everything will be realized. It's just a question of time. Keep hearing.
