**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of changing our perception through hearing scriptures, enabling us to see people beyond their bodies and recognize the soul within.

Which is duplicated by material energy in this world. So by hearing the scriptures, that is intelligence, one changes his perception, because illusion is a perception. Unless and until we start seeing people differently, we will also be in the bodily concept of life. A spiritualist, a devotee, he starts seeing people. Perception of people is the most important thing for spiritualist. One who is interested, one who is progressing in spiritual life, one who wants to be a Vaishnava, his perception of people is very important. One cannot perceive the others as bodies. One should see, separate the body. A body is simply neutral. Body has actually no consciousness. Consciousness is separate, body is separate. There is no difference between the body and the floor in which you are sitting. It is just an extension of pancha bhuta, that's all. That much pancha bhuta in your body, which is your body, you have control, that's all. But there is no difference on the floor. Because floor also doesn't talk, body also doesn't talk. Isn't it? Floor also doesn't think, your body also doesn't think. Floor also doesn't feel, your body also doesn't feel. The soul in the body thinks, the soul in the body feels, the soul in the body wills. But because we are in bodily concept of life.

### soul, body, illusion

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker explains that our soul's thinking is influenced by the material body, which duplicates qualities of the soul. Identifying as male or female is an illusion founded on false ego.

My soul's thinking is influenced by this yantra. This is very nicely set up pancha bhuta, which duplicates, like a person. Because you see, if you want to duplicate gold ornaments, by just giving a layer of gold, you have to be very expert. Everything that appears there in the real gold should also appear here. All quality should appear here. So that's why maya has created this body, so all quality should appear. What is the quality of a soul? Thinking, feeling and willing. So you think you are the body, and the body is thinking, feeling and willing. I am this body, I am a man, I am a woman. So my moment I say I am a woman, my feeling, my identification, everything is colored of being a woman. I am a man. Immediately your consciousness is colored as a man. So the attraction in the material world, if you see the attraction between male and female, that's the subject that is here, the attraction between male and female has no sound basis in the spiritual platform. Why? Because to say that I am a male is first illusion, to say that I am a female is another illusion. So it is founded on illusory self-concept.

### knowledge, perception, illusion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/0BaWzjCwFlI?t=1442
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "You don't see what your eyes sees. You see with the eyes of intelligence."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that true knowledge is perception guided by intelligence and shastra, not just sensory observation, as Maya expertly creates illusions.

Information at the intellectual level you can have. Knowledge means perception. You don't see what your eyes sees. You see with the eyes of intelligence. If you look at the, with what the eyes sees, it look like gold. Similarly if you look just at what the eyes sees, then okay, because Maya is expert in her job, it look like such is Ananda body only, male, female, everybody. But I cannot see what my eyes sees, I should see what my shastra tells to see, yes, yes, this is a duplication of, this skin deep, all male, female, everybody is skin deep only. Skin deep false personality. The real personality is a, is sitting inside and is, and is haunted by this bodily concept of life. So even a karmi when he is speaking, who is speaking? Soul is speaking. That sense. Speaking means not, soul is experiencing that speaking. But it is speaking in a haunted manner. Suppose you are haunted, how will you speak? You will, haunted means at the identity level, a little replacement has happened. Suddenly you say okay now, so and so suddenly gets haunted. He is not the same person, you will see. Somebody else gets into your body and he utilizes the body. That is called, haunting.

---
### false ego, body, sense gratification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/0BaWzjCwFlI?t=1536
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "All these sense gratificatory, false egos are all like, fly by night operators."
**Summary:** The speaker compares false ego to a ghost that haunts the body, leading to indulgence in sense gratification, like fly-by-night operators who disappear after looting.

Exactly the same way. This false ego, this haunting goes to the problem. Body is not a problem, for spiritualists. Body is not a problem, body can be used in Krishna's service. This ghost, comes into the body, enjoys, afterwards it goes away. Where it goes away? Into the memory. Then your normal self will come. Hey, why did I indulge in this? I should not have. Who is talking now? What happened? Earlier who indulged? Somebody else came inside and indulged. Aiyaram, Gayaram, they said. You know, fly by night operators are called Aiyaram, Gayaram. Suddenly they will come open on business, collect all monies, and suddenly shop is closed. It disappeared, with everybody's money. So all these sense gratificatory, false egos are all like, fly by night operators. They will come in the night, and then they will go away, afterwards. Then you wake up for Mangalarati. Your normal self. Who is that fellow who came inside, looted and went away?

---
### false ego, identity, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/0BaWzjCwFlI?t=1620
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "I am a person, capable of thinking, feeling and willing. I am not a male person, I am not a female person, I am a person."
**Summary:** The speaker advises disassociating from the concept of being male or female and identifying as a person capable of thinking, feeling, and willing, ultimately as a servant of Krishna, using the body in Krishna's service.

So this approach we should take, this identification should not be there. To remove, to deal with false ego over many many years, you should start disassociating with this concept of I am a male, I am a female, that concept itself. That's not. I am a person, capable of thinking, feeling and willing. I am not a male person, I am not a female person, I am a person. First you are a person. What is the characteristic of a person? Somebody who can think, feel and will. Then you get color of a male or a female onto the person. Is it not? So pure person means, I am thinking, feeling, willing. I am a person who is servant of Krishna. Even in gross, gross bodily concept also you can be there. But as long as you remember that I am, this body is servant of Krishna. I am this body, I am the servant of Krishna, no problem. As long as you, just like in the spiritual body, in the spiritual world also, you have a spiritual body. So about this body, that's why you, why do we think we put all the Vaishnava, Tilak all over the body? Because you see we are treating this body like Sachidananda body. That means like in this spiritual world I have hands, I have to use my hands to serve Krishna. So here I have this body, hand is there, I have to use for serving Krishna. So having a body is not a handicap for real Vaishnavas, whereas impersonalists and mayavadis, this is a big problem. For them it's, you know, no form, no fame, no qualities for the body, for the person has no qualities, no thinking, no feeling, no willing. Ultimately I have to eliminate thinking, feeling and willing, I have to become pure awareness, they say. For us pure awareness is a foundation. Then we have got pure senses, we have got pure body, we have got pure activity, pure services in the spiritual world, in the Vaikunthallam. And that is what we learn from Srimad Bhagavatam, that is Bhagavad Dharma. Dharma of personality and...

## Srimad Bhagavatam | HG Madhu Pandit Dasa | SB 3.22.13-14 | 27-09-2016
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

### Vedic marriage, Respect, Culture vs. Wealth
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/CW5cb-l9fBI?t=30
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Therefore, in offering one's daughter to a person, the culture and quality are counted as prominent, not wealth or any other material considerations."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the traditional Vedic marriage procedure where the father offers his daughter to a suitable boy, emphasizing the importance of culture and quality over material wealth.

The general procedure for Vedic marriage is that a father offers his daughter to a suitable boy. That is a very respectable marriage. A boy should not go to the girl's father and ask for the hand of his daughter in marriage. That is considered to be a humbling of one's respectable position. Swaimbhuvamanu wanted to convince Kardvamuni, since he knew that the sage wanted to marry a suitable girl. I am offering just such a suitable wife. Do not reject the offer, or else, because you are in need of a wife, you will have to ask for such a wife from someone else, who may not behave with you so well. In that case, your position will be humbled. Another feature of this incident is that Swaimbhuvamanu was the emperor, but he went to offer his qualified daughter to a poor brahmana. Kardvamuni had no worldly possessions, he was a hermit living in the forest, but he was advanced in culture. Therefore, in offering one's daughter to a person, the culture and quality are counted as prominent, not wealth or any other material considerations. I will read the next verse also.

---
### Brahmacharya, Celibacy, Spiritual Orders
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/CW5cb-l9fBI?t=111
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Only a householder can indulge in sense gratification or sex life, not a brahmachari."
**Summary:** This segment explains the concept of brahmacharya, defining the two types: naishtika (lifelong celibacy) and upakurvanah (celibacy up to a certain age), and its importance in student life and spiritual orders.

aham tvashenavam vidvan vivahartam samuddhi adhyatam atastam upakurvanah pratham pratigrahaname. Swaimbhuvamanu continued, O wise man, I heard that you were prepared to marry. Please accept her hand which is being offered to you by me, since you have not taken a vow of perpetual celibacy. The principle of brahmacharya is celibacy. There are two kinds of brahmacharis. One is called naishtika brahmachari, which means one who takes a vow of celibacy for his whole life, whereas the other, upakurvanah brahmachari, or a brahmachari who takes the vow of celibacy up to a certain age. For example, he may take the vow to remain celibate up to 25 years of age, then with the permission of his spiritual master, he enters married life. Brahmacharya is student life, the beginning of life in the spiritual orders, so brahmacharya is celibacy. Only a householder can indulge in sense gratification or sex life, not a brahmachari.

---
### Marriage, Karma, Respect
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/CW5cb-l9fBI?t=177
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "And all these logic, they are all just like one who rejects an offering that comes of its own accord, but later begs a boon from a miser, loses his widespread reputation, and his pride is humbled by the neglectful behavior of others."
**Summary:** This segment discusses Swaimbhuvamanu's request to Kardvamuni to accept his daughter in marriage, highlighting the reasons and potential consequences of rejecting such an offer, relating it to the intricacies of karma.

Only a householder can indulge in sense gratification or sex life, not a brahmachari. Swaimbhuvamanu requested Garudvamuni to accept his daughter, and since Garudvama had not taken the vow of naishtika brahmachari, he was willing to marry the suitable daughter of a high royal family was presented. He was willing to marry, and the suitable daughter of a high royal family was presented. So Swaimbhuvamanu is requesting Garudvamuni with various reasons, why he should accept his offer, the offer of giving his daughter in marriage to Garudvamuni. So various reasons are being quoted by Swaimbhuvamanu. Different logic is being put forth to Garudvamuni. And all these logic, they are all just like one who rejects an offering that comes of its own accord, but later begs a boon from a miser, loses his widespread reputation, and his pride is humbled by the neglectful behavior of others. These are the intricacies of the laws of karma.

---
### Ashrama, Personal Choice, Spiritual Advancement
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/CW5cb-l9fBI?t=263
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "There is nothing absolute as far as the material condition of the living entity is concerned."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes that the choice of ashrama (brahmacharya, grahastha, etc.) is a personal decision based on internal reasoning and knowledge, not social pressure, and that there is nothing absolute about it.

So, not that Garudvamuni has to, you know, get into this wedlock, because of those reasons, because he was an elevated person, and he was a muni, a brahmana, a hermit living in the forest, and Prabhupada says that he was advanced in culture, so therefore the king was offering his daughter to him. Somehow or the other, by will of providence, Garudvamuni has also decided to become a grahastha. So there is nothing absolute like, you know, one should not become grahastha, one should remain brahmacharya, etc. There is nothing absolute as far as the material condition of the living entity is concerned. He can be in any varna, he can be in any ashrama, but it is up to the person to make a decision how he should, what ashrama he should choose, etc. He should have good reasons. Reasons means with knowledge. Not social reasons, not prestige reasons. Oh, I will be a brahmacharya, I will be a sannyasi, that will be great. Not all those things, for one's own internal reasoning. Not for the society thing, for his own internal reasoning. He may decide either to become grahastha or not become grahastha, remain a brahmacharya.

---
### Krishna Consciousness, Materialism, Spiritual Expansion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/CW5cb-l9fBI?t=366
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The principle is not to acquire things, not to create possessions. Not to have a luxurious lifestyle."
**Summary:** This segment discusses Prabhupada's approach to admitting grahasthas into ashramas, contrasting it with Gaudiya Math, and emphasizes the importance of minimizing material possessions and focusing on internal spiritual expansion in Krishna consciousness.

If one is very serious about chanting the Hare Krishna Maha Mantra, serious about spiritual life, seriously dedicated to Prabhupada's mission, then Prabhupada himself set the precedence of admitting grahasthas into the ashramas, unlike Gaudiya Math, where no grahasthas were admitted into the ashramas. So he created a system and Prabhupada said that if you can live like a brahmana, husband and wife, then you can leave off the temple. But if you want to live like a regular grahastha, have all the, you know, what the shastra says, how a regular grahastha should be, then he should go out, earn money and be that. That also does not stop. If the person is chanting Hare Krishna, that also does not stop. But these are all uphill tasks. To be in the midst of opulence, to be in the midst of acquiring so many things, definitely the quality of one's chanting, quality of one's advancement will come down. So devotees should, whoever has taken this path of Krishna consciousness should understand that it is a path of ultimately catering to the minimum needs of the body and the mind, whether one is a brahmacharya, grahastha, whatever it is. That is the principle. The principle is not to acquire things, not to create possessions. Not to have a luxurious lifestyle. These are all the principles, irrespective of what the ashrama is. Because once a devotee has accepted the path of Krishna consciousness, what is his decision? His decision is maintain my body and soul, my society, connections, all these things to the minimum. Certain things are required, I should maintain those minimum things. And where is my expansion? My expansion is internal. My expansion is to move forward in Krishna consciousness. My expansion is in understanding transcendental knowledge. My expansion is in understanding very clearly difference between matter and spirit. And after understanding, I can move forward.

---

## Srimad Bhagavatam | HG Madhu Pandit Dasa | SB 3.22.15-16 | 04-10-2016
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

### marriage, female psychology, religious regulations
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/m4tGbKgmLvw?t=30
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "That is a great consideration because female psychology dictates that when a woman offers her heart to a man for the first time, it is very difficult for her to take it back."
**Summary:** This segment discusses Kardamamuni's considerations for accepting Devahuti as his wife, emphasizing her devotion and the importance of a first-class marriage.

There were many considerations by Kardamamuni before accepting the daughter of Swayambhuva Manu. Most important is that Devahuti had first of all fixed her mind on marrying him. She did not choose to have any other man as her husband. That is a great consideration because female psychology dictates that when a woman offers her heart to a man for the first time, it is very difficult for her to take it back. Also, she had not married before. She was a virgin girl. All these considerations convinced Kardamamuni to accept her. Therefore, he said, "Yes, I shall accept your daughter under religious regulations of marriage." There are different kinds of marriages of which the first class marriage is held by inviting a suitable bridegroom for the daughter and giving her in charity, well-dressed and well-decorated with ornaments along with the dowry according to the means of the father. There are other kinds of marriage such as Gandharva marriage and marriage by love which are also accepted as marriage. Even if one is forcibly kidnapped and later on accepted as a wife, that is also accepted. But Kardamamuni accepted the first-class way of marriage because the father was willing and the daughter was qualified. She had never offered her heart to anyone else. All these considerations made Kardamamuni agree to accept the daughter of Swaibuwa Manu.

### social order, Kali Yuga, degradation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/m4tGbKgmLvw?t=295
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In the age of Kali, everyone will be like Shudras. The traditional social customs are not followed in this age, although formerly they were followed strictly."
**Summary:** This segment explains how the traditional Kshatriya principles have been abolished due to India's dependence on foreign countries, leading to a loss of social order and everyone being considered a Shudra in the age of Kali.

Afterward, an adjustment was made by the elderly members of the Kuru family. Such marriages were current in bygone ages, but at the present moment they are impossible because the strict principles of Kshatriya life have practically been abolished. Since India has become dependent on foreign countries, the particular influences of our social orders have been lost. Now, according to the scriptures, everyone is a Shudra. The so-called Brahmana, Kshatriya and Vaishyas have forgotten their traditional activities and the absence of these activities, they are called Shudras. It is said in the scriptures, "In the age of Kali, everyone will be like Shudras." The traditional social customs are not followed in this age, although formerly they were followed strictly. Thus in the Bhaktivedanta Parpat.

So as per the way life should be on earth, as created by the Lord, there is a social system by which the society conducts itself. Namesake, some people may call themselves brahmanas, etc. But otherwise, it doesn't exist at all. **Summary:** This segment addresses the solution for Krishna conscious devotees in the absence of the Varnashrama system, highlighting Prabhupada's emphasis on ISKCON as a society of Krishna consciousness and the power of the Sankirtana process to elevate individuals beyond social classifications.

So then what is the solution? At least for Krishna conscious devotees or those who are wanting to be Krishna conscious, what is the solution for them? In one conversation, Prabhupada was asked, what about this varnashrama system? So Prabhupada was saying that, you know, how without varnashrama system, how we can, how we are existing? The question was asked to Prabhupada, and then Prabhupada was talking about varnashrama system. So Prabhupada said that this varnashrama system is not there now. That is why, how the varnashrama system will happen in ISKCON, that was the question that was posed to Prabhupada. Prabhupada said, we are not varnashrama society, we are a society of Krishna consciousness. We are a society of Krishna conscious devotees. Because Lord Chaitanya has given us this Sankirtana process, holy name. Therefore, anybody and everybody is becoming Vaishnava, rising above their varna and ashrama. That is the power of the holy name, that the power of the Sankirtana, Prabhupada mentions. So, obviously, you know, unless everybody becomes a devotee, it is not possible for the original social order of varnashrama system to be brought in. And then only you go to the spiritual world. That means you should learn how to, you should have the culture, everything to be born in a in a world where Krishna is doing his Leela. Krishna's Leela never ends. It's eternal Leela in this material world. Somewhere his Leela is going on. So you won't be born somewhere as a Gopa, Gopi, whatever it is, or a cow or a calf.

## Srimad Bhagavatam | HG Madhu Pandit Dasa | SB 3.22.17 | 05-10-2016
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

### dharma, civilization, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/yeP8kZYxsrU?t=570
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "First you should become a civilized human being, then we can become a Vaishnava."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses appreciating beauty within dharma, emphasizing the importance of becoming a civilized human being before becoming a Vaishnava.

Now, Kharadamuni is describing this. He is not in maya. He is describing it as a fact because now he is appreciating the beauty of the girl who was brought to him for offering in marriage. So when she is offered in marriage, for him to appreciate her beauty is within the dharma, but it is not absolute devotion service. But it is within the dharma. So in the material world, even in illusion, you deal with beauty, you deal with rasa of relationship within the rules and regulations of the shastras. Within the rules and regulations of the shastras, one becomes a civilized human being. First you should become a civilized human being, then we can become a Vaishnava.

---
### Vaishnava, spiritual world, potencies
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/yeP8kZYxsrU?t=643
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In the spiritual world there is super sadācāra. It's a wonderful saccidānanda society."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the qualities of a Vaishnava and contrasts the material world with the spiritual world, explaining the three energies of the Lord: Sandhini, Samvit, and Ghlādini.

Prabhupada says in some place, a Vaishnava is a gentleman because a Vaishnava is one who is learning how to be a perfect gentleman in the spiritual world. First he has to be a gentleman here. Okay. In that Kolaka physical form, he can have an innerblick that he is love for his body, but the other material, which we use, does not have automatically the feeling that it is only the spiritual body which feels proclaiming and living. If he feels anymore, you need to do a bit more to understand it, to understand the nature, he needs to know it is only one who needs it. There is helplessness this way. Emotions there and all this being jokey. To memorizing all this, no, in the spiritual world there is super sadācāra. It's a wonderful saccidānanda society. This is a triguna society. Just like trigunas produce this society, there are three energies of the Lord that produce that society: Sandhini potency, samvit potency, ghlādini potency. Just like raja-guna, tamo-guna, sattva-guna. In the same way, there are three modes in the spiritual world. Sandhini is existential potency. Samvit is knowledge potency. Ghlādini is pleasure potency. So a world is created by these energies of the Lord. These are energies of the Lord. A world is created, a society is created. Created means it exists, it displays. It's not that it's created sometime, but it is active. The three energies are active in creating a spiritual society, in the spiritual world. And the characteristic of that atmosphere is that of giving enjoyment to Kṛṣṇa. The one who can actually take the enjoyment of unlimited living entities. Rasa. He's the center of the rasa.

---
### Krishna, beauty, detachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/yeP8kZYxsrU?t=782
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "You don't hate female beauty, you don't hate females, but you hate your attitude to enjoy female beauty."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Krishna is the center of rasa and that in the spiritual world, everything belongs to Krishna. He emphasizes seeing beauty without coveting and hating the attitude of enjoyment rather than the beauty itself.

He's not an ordinary person. He's another tattva, surrounded by ordinary tattvas like us, who are tiny, who all give ānanda to Him, and whose constitutional ānanda is the ānanda of giving ānanda in the spiritual world. Every living entity, so everybody is in ānanda. So that whole atmosphere is the atmosphere of sacchidā. That's the ānanda of ānanda. So, in the spiritual world, even though there are so many female beauties... When one is attracted to the supreme beauty, or Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa... Then everything becomes compared to that beauty, to that beautiful beauty, to that beauty every other beauty is an actually an offering to that beauty. The beauty and the attitude of the residents of Vaikuntha is that of giving pleasure to Krishna. The attitude of everybody in Vaikuntha is that everything belongs to Krishna. Every person in the spiritual world belongs to Krishna. The owner is the enjoyer of the property of Krishna is the supreme lord who is the enjoyer of all the Jivas, so all the beauty is enjoyed by Krishna. So one does not, one sees beauty in the material world, you can see beauty without coveting. You can see beauty in the material world also without coveting. Coveting means what? Wanting to possess, it's for me, it is for me. But the fact that beauty is not there cannot be denied. In the spiritual world, you may see somebody, you may say, oh she is very beautiful, doesn't mean it is Maya. You appreciate her beauty because it's Krishna's creation, it's Krishna's energy, but you are not coveting. You don't want to enjoy her. You are recognizing it is Krishna's, she is Krishna's servant. You are recognizing it is Krishna's servant. So therefore, within our mind, you cannot get out of this thing that like Mayavadis say, that you know, you should get out of the, you should hate the attitude of enjoying. You don't hate female beauty, you don't hate females, but you hate your attitude to enjoy female beauty. So...

### lust, illusion, knowledge
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/yeP8kZYxsrU?t=1045
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "We have to fear our attitude of enjoyment, we have to hate our attitude of enjoyment there is no point in hating anything or any people in this world, male, female there is no hating you hate our perception."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of poison and anti-venom to explain how regulated sense gratification can uproot lust. He emphasizes hating the attitude of enjoyment and lusty perception, not the people themselves, and attacking illusion with knowledge.

You conduct yourself as a proper grahastha then that lust can be uprooted it will dwindle, it will dindle dindle it's like a medicine. There are many examples even in our physical body also certain ailments to remove a poison you have to put another poison. This anti-venom is also created out of poison the snake bite you get a snake bite, they'll give you anti-venom but anti-venom is created out of venom only they've taken poison out of the snake and they have created that it's also poison that is one example which will cure the poison in the body so anyway what we need to understand is we have to fear our attitude of enjoyment we have to hate our attitude of enjoyment there is no point in hating anything or any people in this world, male, female there is no hating you hate our perception. We hate people's lusty perception. Don't hate people. We hate illusion. Lusty perception means it's illusion. We hate illusion. We attack illusion. We attack illusion in our mind. We attack illusion in others' mind. How do you attack illusion? By speaking knowledge. How it is illusion? So that the hearer will come out of that illusion and see things as they are.

### Mayavada, Vaishnava, attitude
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/yeP8kZYxsrU?t=1160
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker contrasts Vaishnava philosophy with Mayavada philosophy, stating that Vaishnavas recognize the existence of beauty but without coveting it. He emphasizes the importance of surrendering attitudes of controlling and owning to Krishna.

So everything has a relevance in this material world. It is so easy. That is why we say that this Mayavada philosophy is for kindergarten. So easy. Brahma Satya Jagat Mithya. That is their understanding. Jagat Mithya means this all does not exist at all. The way for me to get out of being affected by female beauty is to say that it doesn't exist at all. But what Vaishnava say, here what you are seeing does not exist. What you are seeing now does not exist. In something you are seeing female beauty, there it does not exist. But you cannot say it does not exist at all. Variety is the spice of enjoyment. Spiritual world is full of varieties. We can't enter the spiritual world with controlling attitude, owning attitude, enjoying attitude. We cannot enter. Those attitudes should go away. From controlling attitude, it should come to power, surrendering attitude. From owning attitude, it should come to, one should tell oneself, I am owned by Krishna, I am owned by my Guru. You give your ownership. Don't try to own anything. You give your ownership.

I am not the enjoyer, I am the enjoyed, please enjoy me, my lord enjoy me, enjoy my services, enjoy what? You have created me, all the faculties that I have is meant for you, I offer everything unto you including myself, enjoyment. So our goal is very clear in devotional service, when we do devotional service in this material world, sadhana bhakti, it is meant to practice our attitude, it is meant to practice our servant identity, yesterday we discussed, attitude, from lording attitude, from enjoying identities to serving attitude, from enjoying identities to serving identities, from seeing the world as meant for my enjoyment to perceiving the world as meant for Krishna's enjoyment, from seeking decision making, to seeking the desires for my enjoyment, to seeking the desires for Krishna's enjoyment, what pleases Krishna?

### spiritual body, illusion, Srimad Bhagavatam
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/yeP8kZYxsrU?t=1560
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Because illusion has to perfectly replicate original."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the spiritual body as Ananda, Chinmaya, Sadujjvala, Vigrahasya, and explains that the material body is a duplication of this. He notes that illusion perfectly replicates the original, obscuring the panchabhuta. He concludes by mentioning that Radharani's name is not directly mentioned in Srimad Bhagavatam, requiring one to read between the lines.

That is the description of every spiritual body. Is what it is? Ananda, chinmaya, sadujjvala, vigrahasya. It is not in the material world. This is duplicated through a body of flesh. Panchabhuta body is duplicating. That is the reason when you see a female beauty or a male beauty, vice versa, opposite sex, you don't see the panchabhuta. It is made up of panchabhuta. But you don't see the panchabhuta. Why you don't see the panchabhuta? Because if you see the panchabhuta, illusion will fall apart. Because illusion has to perfectly replicate original. In original, there is no panchabhuta like that. Original is sacchidananda vigraha. Original is sacchidananda vigraha. Original is sacchidananda vigraha. The internal energy of the lord. So basically if our enjoying attitude goes, lording attitude goes, controlling attitude goes, and we get replaced by serving attitude, surrendering attitude, then our consciousness is getting purified to understand higher and higher subject matters which are there, which is described in Srimad Bhagavatam. There is an entire Srimad Bhagavatam also. Radharani's name is not directly mentioned. Just see. You have to read between the lines. Who is this topmost gopi? Her name is, you have to read between the lines.

Yes, we have got two minds. The conscious mind and the subconscious mind, or the unconscious mind. To remove things from our subconscious mind, it is a mechanical process, just like a machine. Programming, deprogramming, how do you do? In the same way, of course this programming is not simply digital programming. It is a programming of emotions. It is a programming of thinking, feelings. It is a programming of thinking, feeling, willing programming. That means you, a particular kind of a situation comes, immediately your subconscious mind will respond with a particular thinking, particular feeling, particular willing into your consciousness. Every moment in our consciousness, consciousness means thinking, feeling and willing. These things just respond. And your current conscious mind is saying, no, this is not correct. But that is pushing. Thinking, feeling, thinking, feeling and willing. Willing is what? Impulse. Impulse. Forcefully your mind is pushing you to act.

## The Ever Fresh Nature of Krishna Katha | @madhupanditdasaofficial
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

### Krishna-katha, dissatisfaction, spiritual taste
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/7EhW7CakYsY?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In the spiritual world, when you are dissatisfied, you are happy."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that hearing about Krishna's pastimes is never satiating, and in the spiritual world, dissatisfaction leads to happiness because of the intense taste.

It is said that the narration of the past times of the Lord is never satiating. Never satiating means one does not become satisfied by hearing the past times of the Lord. One wants to hear more and more and more and more. In the material world, you are happy when you are satisfied. In the spiritual world, when you are dissatisfied, you are happy. So, as far as hearing the past times of the Lord, you are always dissatisfied because it is so tasty that you are never satisfied. Just like if we give you a tasty thing, then you are not satisfied because you want more of that tasty thing. So, this is the nature of the absolute rasa which is emanating from Krishna.

## The Exalted Position of Lord Shiva |  Sri Madhu Pandit Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

### detachment, devotion, material nature
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/x2NZylQwKG8?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Being a greatest Vaishnava, pure devotee of the Lord, He sees this entire material world as it should be seen, to engage it in the service of the Lord."
**Summary:** Lord Shiva, though in charge of the mode of ignorance, remains detached and engaged in the service of Lord Krishna, exemplifying the spirit of a Vaishnava who prioritizes the soul over the material body.

The position of Lord Shiva is that He is in charge of all the living entities in the mode of ignorance. He takes care of them for their material desires. Those who are inflicted by the three modes of material nature, at every opportunity they are trying to get material pleasure for their body, for their mind, for their intellect, for their false ego. Even though He is in charge of the mode of ignorance in this material world, He is unaffected by any of the modes of material nature. He is exemplified by His detachment from this material world. He never accepts luxurious dress, garland, ornament, etc. He is engaged in His own Self. Only one who is engaged in His own Self can actually be free from the three modes of material nature. Means, He understands His own Self as part and parcel, as part of the Supreme Lord Krishna. Knowing that, He is always engaged in the service of the Supreme Lord. This is the spirit of the Vaishnavas also. The Vaishnavas don't want to decorate this body because they know this body is dead. The Vaishnavas want to decorate their soul. Being a greatest Vaishnava, pure devotee of the Lord, He sees this entire material world as it should be seen, to engage it in the service of the Lord.

---

There is somewhere a source where we can get eternal sat, chitta, and ananda. Just like iron in the fire becomes red hot, in the same way, the purpose of Krishna's avatara is to touch us. Just like the iron touched the fire, it became red hot. Soonly when Krishna touches us, we start getting real experience of sat, chitta, and ananda. Spiritual power, spiritual knowledge, spiritual love, spiritual pleasure will flow from Krishna, from Krishna's avatara to us. Krishna is beyond our material senses.

### Material Senses, Atma, Avatara
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/-WqZe_V8VeU?t=59
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So therefore the Lord says, okay, I will become accessible to your material senses. That is avatara, Rupa avatara, Shabda avatara, taste avatara."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses how Krishna takes avataras to become accessible to our material senses, as the soul (atma) can directly perceive Krishna, but the body limits our perception.

He has to take avatara because we are stuck with our material senses. I can touch only what my body touches. I can only see what my body eye sees. I can only smell what my body knows makes me smell, etc. I can only taste what my tongue tastes, body tongue tastes. Originally the atma can taste spiritual taste. Atma's eyes can see Krishna directly. The atma's tongue can taste Krishna. Atma's eyes can see Krishna directly. The atma's tongue can taste Krishna. Krishna prasadam tongue can taste directly Krishna nama. The shastra says that if my spiritual tongue is awakened and spiritual mind is awakened and spiritual ears are awakened, then this body cannot even withstand that ananda, that ecstasy. Now, because we can only see, touch, smell, etc., through this body, this association of Krishna, Satchitananda association, we can receive only through our material senses. So therefore the Lord says, okay, I will become accessible to your material senses. That is avatara, Rupa avatara, Shabda avatara, taste avatara.

Similarly we can never be happy, the Atma can never be happy, the spirit person in this body can never be happy. You, me, cannot be happy unless we get our spiritual body and enter the spiritual world and deep within us, all of us are looking for this. We are looking for permanent love, we are looking for permanent existence, we are looking for pure knowledge.

The fire of Satchitananda is so merciful, you have to put the fire into your iron-like soul through the ears, through the mind. This is the most important example of Prabhupada. **Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/-WqZe_V8VeU?t=586

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker explains that spiritual progress depends on how much the Nama enters the heart through attentive hearing, using the analogy of iron and fire to illustrate the difference between distant and direct association with Satchitananda.

Now you are hearing me. Your mind is carrying your attention to what I am speaking. Attention is a property of the soul. This is the most important. This mic has no attention, but I have attention. But that attention is moved here and there by the mind. This is the most important. By your willpower, you should keep your mind on the sound. Then you are effectively associating. This is the most important. Some people advance very fast by chanting Hare Krishna. Some people don't advance very slowly. They advance. But some advancement is there. Just like you take a piece of iron. Place it 10 feet away, little heat it will get. Five feet away, little more heat. One feet away, little more heat. Then 5 feet away, little more heat. Then 10 feet away, little more heat. 5 feet away, little more heat. 10 feet away, little more heat. 5 feet away, little more heat. away, little more heat. Six inches away, little more heat. Almost near it, little more heat. Put into the fire, big difference. Similarly, do you want the effect of your chanting like ten feet away to five feet away or you want to put your soul completely into the fire of Satchidananda by hearing every Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare.

**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/-WqZe_V8VeU?t=719
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Thinking during Japa is the biggest enemy."
**Summary:** The speaker identifies thinking as the biggest obstacle to the Holy Name entering the heart during japa, emphasizing that stopping thoughts allows one to fully hear and benefit from the mantra.

What I am telling is a very big secret. Does not matter how bad the iron is, how cold the iron is, will get heated. Does not matter whether you have Jnana, you know Jnana, you have read Bhagavad Gita, you have not read Bhagavad Gita, you are a man, you are a woman, you are a child, no difference it makes as long as the fire goes inside, it will become red hot. So all of you are chanting Hare Krishna Maha Mantra, but it depends on you, how much you want to benefit. And the biggest enemy for not hearing is what? Can you all tell, how many times I have spoken, in one word you can say, the biggest enemy for hearing is thinking. Thinking. Thinking. Thinking during Japa is the biggest enemy. I will repeat, what is the biggest enemy of Holy Name going inside, what is the biggest obstacle that is stopping Nama, Avatara from going into your heart, it is thinking. So stop thinking during Japa. Thinking means you are hearing your thoughts. If you hear your thoughts, you cannot hear Holy Name. If you hear me, you cannot hear somebody else. Just like when you are hearing me, you are not hearing so many sounds that are going around. So either you hear the Nama, Avatara or you are hearing your thinking. Your thinking is not fire, your thinking is ice. It will be fire. It will pull you more, it will take you more into asat achit nirananda. Your thinking has no value before the Holy Name who is the ocean of sacchidananda. So, therefore it is left to you. All the real spiritual progress that you make depends upon how many of Hare Krishna Mahamantra has been heard by you and sent inside. According to that, your advancement will increase. According to that, your Sat heat, Chit heat and Ananda heat will be experienced. We are all very deeply craving for this experience of real Ananda, real Sat, real Chit.

### Illusion, Material Pleasures, Mirage
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/-WqZe_V8VeU?t=1142
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Similarly, the material pleasures, material happiness, all these things, it is another kind of illusory happiness."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of a mirage in the desert to illustrate the illusory nature of material pleasures and the endless cycle of chasing after them.

That is nothing, again millions and millions of bodies, I have to live eternally. I have to end this birth and death of material bodies, always life after life, in each body, within each body, chasing, chasing, chasing after power, chasing after love, chasing after, like a mirage in the desert. The desert you stand, you look like there is water, right? Then you go near it, you don't find water. Then you turn this side, oh, there it looks like there is water. Again another mirage you see. You chase, chase, chase, when you go near it, there is no water. Then you are saying, no, where can I, look this side, again mirage, because in the desert, that illusion is created. So you chase, you go near it, then two things happen. You go near it, the mirage keeps going away, away, away, away. That means you can never reach. By going towards the mirage, you cannot reach. You turn around in different directions, you cannot reach, because it is an illusion. Similarly, the material pleasures, material happiness, all these things, it is another kind of illusory happiness.

---
### Hope, Fulfillment, Spiritual World
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/-WqZe_V8VeU?t=1240
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Or do you want real hope plus fulfilment? You hope for love of Krishna and you get love of Krishna. That is spiritual world."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the illusory hope of material happiness with the real hope and fulfillment found in the spiritual world through love and service to Krishna.

That means there is only hope. In mirage, what is there? Hope. Hope for water. When a person is thirsty, when he gets hope for water, does it give him some happiness? Yes. Ah, there, there is water. Ah, hope. So in the material world, all happiness is hope for happiness. All power is hope for power. All love is hope for love. What a life, life of only hope, hope, hope, hope, hope. What is that? Cheating. Because all your energy is going, ah, ah, ah, let me go, walk towards. So much energy you have to give to chase the hope for its fulfilment, but then it never reaches. Material world is like this. Or do you want real hope plus fulfilment? You hope for love of Krishna and you get love of Krishna. That is spiritual world. You hope for happiness of serving Krishna and you really get the happiness of serving Krishna. You hope for meeting Krishna, you really meet Krishna. You hope for love, you really get love.

You have got japa mala. You have started practicing. You are chanting sixteen rounds also. But here, give your mind. Biggest enemy for not hearing. If you don't think, something you will hear. If you consciously hear, then you can push out the thinking during japa. You have to struggle. This is only a struggle. In spiritual life, there is only one struggle. In this Krishna consciousness. All other things easily come. Very easily come. Power to control the senses, knowledge, everything, attraction, everything comes automatically. But one thing you have to struggle. What is that? You have to hear your chanting. That's all. Like a child you have to hear. Mantra after mantra, mantra after mantra, mantra after mantra. At that time during japa, only one work.

Just like every day you get up, you brush your teeth. Automatically trained. Similarly, you are chanting so many days, so many years. Tongue, very easy to get trained. It will chant sixteen rounds. Like tongue, please understand, mind also can be trained. Just like tongue, without missing one Hare or Krishna, sixteen rounds you can chant. Your mind can also be trained to hear sixteen rounds without missing even one round. Without even missing one mantra. Imagine the gift. Imagine how much benefit you will get if you train your mind to hear and do japa. Not think during japa. That's all. One simple thing. For that, you have to use your will power to keep away thinking and then capture. As you are chanting, capture the hearing. As each sound keeps coming, hearing, hearing, hearing. Hearing means what? Knowing the sound. Hare is now before me. Just like Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare. Split into two. There is Hare Krishna part, there is Hare Rama part. On each bead, be aware. Now, I am in, I want to be aware whether I am in Hare Krishna part or Hare Rama part. Okay, automatically attention is coming by monitoring. You are hearing.

### Mind Training, Shabda, Solution
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/-WqZe_V8VeU?t=1686
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "All hope is in that Shabda right there. Why you are hoping for something? Solution to all problems, don't think. Your thinking cannot produce solutions. Your chanting can produce solutions."
**Summary:** The speaker provides practical instructions on training the mind to focus on the Shabda (sound) of the Hare Krishna mantra, emphasizing that the mantra itself is the solution to all problems and that thinking should be abandoned during japa.

Practice this for three, four months. Hearing Hare Krishna part and Hare. Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare. Next bead, Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama. So that means you are recognizing two parts of the mantra. After that next six months, every four words, Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare. Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama Hare Hare, Hare Krishna Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna Hare Hare, Hare Rama Hare Rama, Rama Rama Hare Hare. Mind should get trained. Finally mind will get trained to hear. As Hare comes you will catch. Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare, Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare. So if the mind is on thinking, problem solving, some imagination, some worrying, or delighting, or hoping something in future, all that should stop. All hope is in that Shabda right there. Why you are hoping for something? Solution to all problems, don't think. Your thinking cannot produce solutions. Your chanting can produce solutions. Like that Shabda should be there on the Shabda. What I am trying to do, thinking, all that thing, chhodo. Right now only Nama, Krishna, Avatara is before me. What problems Krishna cannot solve? So practice chanting and hearing exactly the same sound. So we are going to continue our japa. We do 108 rounds. Those who want, welcome all of you to sit as much as possible. 16 rounds, 32 rounds, 64 rounds.

## The Process of Accelerated Spiritual Growth | Sri Madhu Pandit Dasa | SB 4.19.3 | ISKCON Bangalore
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

### surrender, Krishna's instruction, spiritual success
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ZLba48F_3DQ?t=0
**Verse References:** BG 18.66
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "If you are fortunate enough to take the lessons given by Lord Krishna in the Bhagavad Gita, our lives immediately become successful."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes the importance of surrendering to Krishna and following the teachings of the Bhagavad Gita for achieving success in life.

Devotees in Bhagavad Gita, abandon all varieties of religion and just surrender unto Me. I shall deliver you from all sinful reaction. Do not fear. He can immediately make His devotee immune from all the reactions caused by sinful life if the devotee simply surrender unto Him. He is described here in a Sarvatma, meaning that He is present in everyone's heart as a Supersoul. As such, He is the supreme teacher of everyone. If you are fortunate enough to take the lessons given by Lord Krishna in the Bhagavad Gita, our lives immediately become successful. No one can give better instruction to human society than Lord Krishna. Thus ends the Bhaktivedanta purport.

**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

The Supreme Personality of Godhead, Lord Vishnu, is present in everyone's heart. He is present in everyone's heart as a Supersoul and He is the Proprietor of all planets and enjoyer of the results of all sacrifices. He is personally present as the sacrifices made by King Prithu. Amen.

### Karma, Dharma, spiritual evolution
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ZLba48F_3DQ?t=151
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "For the living entities who are in different stages of their spiritual evolution in this material world, larger part of the material world, they are all Karmis."
**Summary:** This segment discusses King Prithu's engagement of Karmis in Ashwamedha Yajna, explaining that most living entities in the material world are Karmis and defining good Karmis as those who engage in Karma according to Guru, Sadhu, and Shastra for material Artha, Kama, Dharma, and Moksha.

So here, this verse says that King Prithu, who was engaged in performing as a King, Ashwamedha Yajna, the utmost fructive activities to bring forth fruits of prosperity within his kingdom. When he does Ashwamedha Yajna, he is engaging all the Karmiminded citizens. They are the one who are organizing, doing everything. Amen. Otherwise, one would ask why Prithu Maharaj is doing fructive activities. He should be teaching them devotional service, being an empowered incarnation of the Lord. The point is that for the living entities who are in different stages of their spiritual evolution in this material world, larger part of the material world, they are all Karmis. Amen. And good Karmis means who engage in Karma according to Guru, Sadhu and Shastra for material Artha, Kama, Dharma, Artha, Kama, Moksha. 

---
### Dharma, Karma, Moksha
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ZLba48F_3DQ?t=274
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Dharma guides the Karmis to do their Artha and Kama, regulated Kama so that one can go closer to Moksha or liberation from this material world."
**Summary:** This segment explains how Dharma guides Karmis in their pursuit of Artha and Kama, ultimately leading them closer to Moksha, and emphasizes that Krishna is the receiver and enjoyer of all activities, whether devotional service or Karma.

Karma heads and Karma guides, sorry, Dharma, Dharma, Dharma, Artha, Kama, Moksha. Dharma guides the Karmis to do their Artha and Kama, regulated Kama so that one can go closer to Moksha or liberation from this material world. So now Maharaj, Prithu is engaging all these people in the fructive activities and they are performing this Ashramedha Yajna. And in this verse it is said that the Supreme Personality of God at Vishnu is present in these sacrifices made by Prithu Maharaj. And it says that ultimately whether it is the same or not, it is the same. Whether it is devotion service or it is Karma, the receiver of all that, is the enjoyer of all that is Krishna, Bhoktaram Yajna Tapasam, Sarvaloka Maheshwara. Being Sarvaloka Maheshwara, he is the one who receives the, he is the Yajna Purusha. Even if you give to demigods or anything, anywhere, anybody gives, offers any sacrifice, the enjoyer of that sacrifice is Vishnu. Because Karma means, you have to sacrifice something and then you gain something, material.

Even Karma is, whole life is not enjoyment, hmm, but their sacrifice is Ugra Karma or the, but however, you know, it is, it produces result. Work has to produce result. Anybody works according to the Lord's sanction, karma neva adhikaraste, ma phaleshu kadachana. He is the one who gives the phala for everybody's work. So work as a sacrifice, yajna artha karma, yajna artha karma. Everybody has to do yajna artha karma. But in this Kali Yuga, nobody is doing yajna artha karma. They are simply doing karma. So therefore, Krishna, He is the Parama Purushottama. Supreme Ishwara and His expansion as a super soul in the material world, as the father of all the living entities, aham vija prajapita, father of all living entities. He is supplying everyone according to their work. This is His arrangement. So in this material world, Krishna, He is the one who gives the phala. So in this material world, Krishna, He is the one who gives the phala. He is the one who gives the phala. The Purushottama, the Parama Ishwara, He is in this material world as the Supreme Ishwara of this material world. So this material world is running according to the scheme of the Supreme Personality of Godhead to give opportunity for unlimited living entities to be free to do what they want according to their karma. Or, they are free. Potentially. Potentially, if they are free to simply engage in devotional service of the Lord, that means to keep Krishna as the center of all their activities, Krishna's pleasure as a center of all their activities, Krishna as the center of, as the enjoyer of all the activities. Krishna's enjoyment is not like our enjoyment. Super Souls' enjoyment is not like our enjoyment. Super Souls' enjoyment is not like our enjoyment.

### Sankirtanayajna, Shakti Avesha, Hare Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ZLba48F_3DQ?t=625
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Supersoul's enjoyment is in his own standards. What is his standard? When the living entities performs Sankirtanayajna, Lord is pleased for us."
**Summary:** This segment explains that the Lord's pleasure comes from the devotional service of His devotees, especially through Sankirtanayajna, and describes how the sound of Hare Krishna becomes Shakti Avesha, transforming material sound into a spiritual force.

Supersoul's enjoyment is in his own standards. What is his standard? When the living entities performs Sankirtanayajna, Lord is pleased for us. Because Lord is completely self-sufficient, fully. When we say we please the Lord, we have to understand that Lord is an ocean of pleasure. And his pleasure from devotional service of his devotees, especially in this material world, is that of the pleasure of the children coming back to the right path. This gives him pleasure. Therefore when we chant Hare Krishna, it gives pleasure to Krishna. It gives pleasure to Krishna because we are sadhakas and we are taking his name. We are taking advantage of his pleasure. This is Avatara, Shakti Avesha. Now Avatara, the sound that we produce with our tongue becomes the Shakti Avesha. Avatara, not only living entities become Shakti Avesha, even sound, even matter sound produced by us with our tongue, it becomes Shakti Avesha. Just see. And then that sound, as long as all the Shakti, Satya, Chittan, Ananda Shakti, enters that sound, it's no longer material sound. Nothing matter remains when Sat Chit Ananda – Avesha enters the sound. So all matter disappears in that sense. No material qualities exist in the sound. In this material world – Hare Krishna, Rama. No material thing, material effect or three gunas exist in the sound. Similarly, no three gunas exist in the Archa-vigraha where the matter-vigraha is Sat-Chit-Ananda-Ist by the Avesha of that Shakti along with the Shaktimaan. Shaktimaan is there as a person who is incarnated.

---
### Shuddha Nama, Krishna's Rupa, spiritual world
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ZLba48F_3DQ?t=763
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Similarly, Shakti Avesha Nama, Krishna's Nama in the spiritual world, Shuddha Nama is a Vekthi, is non-different from Krishna."
**Summary:** This segment explains the non-difference between Krishna and His Shuddha Nama in the spiritual world, emphasizing that Krishna's Nama and Rupa are eternal and that the Nama descends into the material world as Shakti to purify us.

Similarly, Shakti Avesha Nama, Krishna's Nama in the spiritual world, Shuddha Nama is a Vekthi, is non-different from Krishna. The Nama persona of Krishna is same as Krishna. Any persona of Krishna is same as Krishna. So Nama 100%, Rupa Krishna, Nama Krishna all same, Leela Krishna all 100% same. Same Vekthi. In different aspects. So this Nama, Shuddha Nama of the spiritual world, Krishna's Rupa is there in the spiritual world. Similarly, Krishna's Nama is there in the spiritual world. Everything is eternal. So Krishna's Nama is eternally vibrating in the spiritual world. That eternally vibrating Nama in the spiritual world, He takes Avatara, He enters the sound uttered by us. First He enters as the Shakti. We are not qualified to directly even relate with the Shuddha Nama. Therefore first Shakti comes. The Shakti comes, bathes us, purifies us, cleans us, awakens a bit of our Bhakti towards Krishna. To that degree our chanting also keeps Him. To that degree our chanting also keeps Him. So it is the same, elevating its quality, first touching the Shakti, getting purified by realizations, by Sat realization, Chitta realization, Ananda realization. So gradually by Ananda realization we give up all taste for Jadananda or bogus, fake, cheating, false, imitation Anandas of this material world of love, sense pleasure, power, everything.

---
### Sat Chitta Ananda, material world, spiritual suicide
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ZLba48F_3DQ?t=885
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "There is a full realization in the devotees by the mercy or the flow of Sat Chitta Ananda of the entire cheating game and how then there is realization of how I am the loser in this material world, what I am entitled to is pure Sat Chitta Ananda, what I am entitled to in association with Krishna."
**Summary:** This segment uses the analogy of broken glasses and fireflies to illustrate the deceptive nature of material pleasures compared to the pure Sat Chitta Ananda found in association with Krishna, and equates trying to enjoy the material world with spiritual suicide, resulting in a ghost body.

There is a full realization in the devotees by the mercy or the flow of Sat Chitta Ananda of the entire cheating game and how then there is realization of how I am the loser in this material world, what I am entitled to is pure Sat Chitta Ananda, what I am entitled to in association with Krishna. He is pure Sat Chitta Ananda. And all these are like broken glasses, like Dhruva Maharaj says after seeing Vishnu, he says, I was praying for, my prayers were for broken glasses but now I, you have come as a diamond. So all these Asat, Achitta, Nirananda which is, which is, faking as Sat Chitta Ananda in this material world are like glass pieces, which look like a diamond, especially if it is cut nicely. Nowadays there are American diamonds, they look like diamonds, right? So similarly all these Asat, Achitta and Ananda are doing the dance of being Sat Chitta and Ananda. And then the living entity is attracted. And then it is attracted, what happens? Just like a firefly, or insects, they are attracted towards fire, right? Their own insects come, they go, so much attraction, immediately burns itself, suicide. So actually, when we are trying to enjoy this material world, my spirit, soul is committing suicide. Of course, soul is eternal, but at least for the time being, now we are all committed suicide. When somebody commits suicide, what does he get? A ghost body, right? So we are all got this ghost body. This is not our body, it is a ghost body. We have committed suicide from the spiritual world.

---
### Krishna's mercy, Shuddha Nama, spiritual world

**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** This segment emphasizes Krishna's mercy and the potency of Shuddha Nama, explaining that the Lord descends instantly from the spiritual world when we chant, first as Shakti to purify us, and then as Shuddha Nama if we develop attachment to Him.

So, the Lord is very merciful, He is very gentle. He does not ask us to commit suicide. In fact, in Sai lake, we say that all the time, this matter of being alive, we are all getting the same snake, snake. It is the same snake as a man. Actually, we say, it is the only thing we have, and that's the temple. Krishna has so many potency. Shuddha Nama has so many potency just like Krishna is Surya of Satchitananda. His Nama is also Surya of Satchitananda that Nama enters there is no distance for the Lord between the spiritual world and the material world anytime, any point in the material world instantly from the spiritual world, He descends that is why it is said Goloka Vyapremadana Harinama Sankirtana as soon as we utter, we chant with our tongue we generate the sound with our material tongue immediately the Shuddha Nama descends first He descends all the Shakti when we have no relationship with the Holy Name, why He Himself should descend so His effulgent descends and His effulgence is Shakti and then when we get purified by this touch of this effulgence and in separation from Krishna that Krishna, it is Krishna's energy but if I deserve now, if I have developed attachment to immediately Shuddha Nama also will come there to reciprocate otherwise the Shaktis will reciprocate with us.

## The Purpose of the Varnashrama System in Society | Sri Madhu Pandit Dasa | SB 4.14.18 | 16.07.2024
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

### Varna Ashrama Dharma, Duties, Government
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/dE_AegZmFKs?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The state's duty and the citizens' duty are very nicely explained in this verse."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa reads and explains the purport of Srimad Bhagavatam Canto 4, Chapter 14, Text 18, emphasizing the duties of the king and citizens within the Varna Ashrama system, where everyone engages in devotional service to the Supreme Personality of Godhead.

Om Om Bhagavate Vasudevaya. Reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, Canto 4, Chapter 14, Text 18. The king is supposed to be pious in whose state and cities the general population strictly observes the system of eight social orders of Varna and Ashrama, and where all citizens engage in worshipping the Supreme Personality of Godhead by their particular occupation. The state's duty and the citizens' duty are very nicely explained in this verse. The activities of the government head or king as well as the activities of the citizens must be so directed that ultimately everyone engages in devotional service to the Supreme Personality of Godhead. The king or the government head is supposed to be representative of the Supreme Personality of Godhead and is therefore supposed to see that things go on nicely and the citizens situated in the scientific social order comprise four Varnas and four Ashramas. In the Vishnu Purana, it is stated that unless people are educated or situated in the scientific social order comprised of four Varnas, Brahmana, Kshatriya, Vaishya and Shudra, then four Ashramas, Brahmacharya, Grahastha, Vanaprastha, and Sannyasa, society can never be considered real human society nor can it make any advancement towards the ultimate goal of human life. It is the duty of the government to see that things go on in terms of Varna and Ashrama. As stated here in Bhagavan Etnapurusha, the Supreme Personality of Godhead Krishna is the Etnapurusha. As stated in Bhagavad Gita, Bhaktaram, Yajna Tapasam, Krishna is the ultimate purpose of all sacrifice. He is also the enjoyer of all sacrifices. Therefore, he is known as Etnapurusha. The word Etnapurusha indicates Lord Vishnu or Lord Krishna or any personality of Godhead in the category of Vishnu Tattva. In perfect human society, people are situated in the orders of Godhead. They follow the orders of Varna and Ashrama and are engaged in worshipping Lord Vishnu by their respective activities. Every citizen engaged in an occupation renders service by the resultant actions of his activities. That is the perfection of life.

---
### Varna Ashrama Dharma, Civilization, Social Order
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/dE_AegZmFKs?t=307
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Varna Ashrama system of social organization of human society is a system of social organization meant for civilized human beings."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa discusses the importance of the Varna Ashrama system as a social organization for civilized human beings, emphasizing its role in elevating consciousness and maintaining social order, and lamenting its collapse in modern society.

So, the duty of the king and that of the citizens is explained here by the great sages to King Vena. As we have been discussing, Varna Ashrama system of social organization of human society is a system of social organization meant for civilized human beings. A civilized human being, by following the Varna Ashrama system, he elevates himself into higher states of consciousness. The Varna Ashrama system becomes more abundant by nationalizing inbuttocks and nuevos and opportunities. Vendra and Sahaja Yoga promote a realized life under the guidance of Varna Ashrama. This civilization, of course, has collapsed. Our Indian civilization was based on this division of labor among different citizens. And then the king's duty is to see that the system does not break. Everyone stays in his division of labor, Brahmana, Kshatriya, Vaishya, Shudra. And this was the duty of the Kshatriya king.

---
### Punya, Heavenly Planets, Devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/dE_AegZmFKs?t=474
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Heavenly planets is packed with enjoyment because that is a place to encash your Punya that you have performed in the human body."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa explains that following Varna and Ashrama accumulates Punya, leading to enjoyment in heavenly planets in the next life, but this is distinct from devotional service, which involves recognizing the Lord's design and living in obedience to His will.

And if the population, okay, he simply lives according to his Varna and Ashrama, he would accumulate a lot of Punya because he is obedient to the system set up by the Supreme Personality of Godhead. Even if somebody is not, okay, a devotee of the Lord. But if he follows this system set up by the Lord, even though he doesn't recognize, but he's part of such a Varna Ashrama society, he will accumulate a lot of Punya. And accumulating a lot of Punya, what is the result? The next life they will live in a different way. Okay. They will go to the heavenly planets to enjoy the results of those Punyas. Heavenly planets is packed with enjoyment because that is a place to encash your Punya that you have performed in the human body. So that particular thing of the population thinking that if I am following the Varna Ashrama system, I will get a good life, next life to heavenly planets, has no connection with devotion service.

---
### Devotional Service, Karma, Vishnu
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/dE_AegZmFKs?t=590
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Devotion service is when you recognize that I am part of the Varna Ashrama system that is designed by the Lord."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa contrasts karma-kanda with devotional service, explaining that true devotion involves recognizing the Varna Ashrama system as designed by the Lord and acting for His pleasure, while karma-kandis may misuse the system for personal enjoyment, even denying the need for Vishnu.

Devotion service is when you recognize that I am part of the Varna Ashrama system. So, I am part of the Varna Ashrama system that is designed by the Lord. And I live my life in strict obedience to the will of the Lord who has designed this Varna Ashrama system. So that is devotion service. That is called Daivya Varna Ashrama system. In that Varna Ashrama is not that, you know, Daivya Varna Ashrama system, Vishnu is the Ejna Purusha. My living as a Brahmana, Kshatriya in the society, everything is for the pleasure of the Supreme Personality of Godhead. So that is how the great Indian civilization was. And even at that time, it's not that if Varna Ashrama is there, everybody is a devotee. No. Large part of people who are in Varna Ashrama, they are following because they want to enjoy. And in case they are ignorant and they do not follow the orders of the social system, then they go down in their material life into another form. Okay. They go down in their material life into another form of life. The lower systems, lower. So Krishna's purpose of Varna Ashrama, karma khandis may misuse it. There's a philosophy called karma mimamsa. It's always there. Just like Vaishnavism, that's always there. They say where is the need for Vishnu. If I do my karma, I will go to heavenly planet. So, I have to strictly follow the law of karma. If I want to elevate myself to greater enjoyment, they have no concept of pleasing Vishnu by following the laws of Karma.

### Laws of Nature, Varna Ashrama Dharma, Division of Labor
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/dE_AegZmFKs?t=750
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Same way, the Varnashama system is also like that."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa uses the analogy of atheistic scientists utilizing the laws of electromagnetism without thinking of Vishnu to explain how people can follow the Varna Ashrama system for material benefits without devotion. He emphasizes the importance of Daivya Varnashama, where Krishna is the center, and highlights the division of labor inherent in the system.

Okay. But still, they know. See, just like to use the laws of material nature, you don't have to be devotees. Atheists, atheistic scientists, they utilize the law of electromagnetism. They don't think of Vishnu. Similarly, people know that if I do Punya, then I will enjoy my life. Not Punya to please the Supreme Lord. Certain acts are Punya. If I do that, I will get… material nature has to give me result. Just like if I know the laws of electromagnetism, it has to give result. Lord is not directly connected with it. He has set up a system. Same way, the Varnashama system is also like that. So, we have to understand that we are for Daivya Varnashama system, where Krishna is the center of the activities, the activities of Brahmana, Kshatriya, Vaishya, Shudra. And they execute these duties being situated in one of the Ashramas. Because there also, how to exist, what is the lifestyle is given. What is the lifestyle of a Brahmana? What is the lifetime of a Kshatriya? What is the lifestyle of a Shudra, a Vaishya? First one is the division by labor. Then a society needs intelligentsia, needs administration, needs economic activity and needs people to work with their hands and legs.

### Colonialism, Education, Culture
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/dE_AegZmFKs?t=1315
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** General public
**Key Quote:** "Unless we break the very backbone of this nation, which is our spiritual and cultural heritage."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa reads a newspaper cutting from 1835 quoting Lord Macaulay addressing the British Parliament, outlining his plan to dismantle India's spiritual and cultural heritage by replacing its ancient education system with a foreign one, aiming to instill a sense of inferiority in Indians and create a dominated nation.

On 2nd February, 1835. This is 1835 newspaper cutting. So, what speaks in the British Parliament? I have traveled across the length and breadth of India. And, I have not seen one person who is a beggar. Or, who is a thief. Such wealth I have seen in this country. Such high moral values. People of such caliber. That I do not think we would ever conquer this country. Unless we break the very backbone of this nation. Which is our spiritual and cultural heritage. And, therefore, I propose that we replace her old and ancient education system. Her culture. For, if Indians think that all that is foreign and English is good, and greater than their own, they will lose their self-esteem. Their entire, their native culture. They will lose their native culture. And, they will become what we want them. A truly dominated nation.

### Divine Plan, Language, Culture

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

Now, Prabhupada's books are in English language. Now, we are reading this Prabhupada's book in English. In that English language which they, you know, brought into India to destroy our culture. Now, very same English language, Prabhupada is translating this wonderful literature, Vedic literature. Now, we are talking in English. We are hearing in English about Varnashrama Dharma, how to bring back Varnashrama Dharma. So, the Lord is in control, ultimately. So, we preach in English. See how important Bhaktisiddhanta Saraswati's instruction to Srila Prabhupada. He says, speak to English speaking world. English speaking people. Preach to English speaking people. Bhaktisiddhanta Saraswati clearly knew, even in India, ultimately everybody will be English educated. Over the decades, we are seeing that, if anybody is educated in India, is English educated.
