### Culture, Spirituality, Aggression
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/dE_AegZmFKs?t=1849
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Spirit-centered culture is greater because you are spirit."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa responds to a question about convincing Westerners of their culture's inferiority by explaining that Prabhupada focused on revealing the foundation of Vedic knowledge as spirit-centered, contrasting it with the body-centered Western culture. He emphasizes that India's approach is to spread spiritual culture, not to dominate or control like other nations.

No. See, Prabhupada, he told the foundation of our culture. He gave knowledge. Every culture is based on some knowledge. Western culture is based on Western knowledge. Okay, similarly, Indian culture is based on Vedic knowledge. So, Prabhupada went, Prabhupada and our Acharyas, Bhaktivedanta wrote in English, Bhaktivinoda Thakur wrote in English, many things. They went to the foundation of culture and showed that your foundation is shallow, body-centered culture. Ours is spirit-centered culture. Does not matter, Vaishnavism, Mayavadism, Impersonalism, Chodavism, all of them agree spirit. Spirit-centered culture, we are. Spirit-centered culture, spirit-centered culture is greater because you are spirit. Body-centered culture means you are only caring for your life, birth to death. The moment spirit-centered culture means I never die, I am eternal, soul is eternal. So, my welfare means eternal welfare, not my interest in this life, short term. So, that is the difference between, you know, they had a very devious means of dominating. So, we don't have the concept of dominating and then introducing, for that purpose, introducing spiritual culture.

### Spiritual Culture, Westernization, Mythology
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/dE_AegZmFKs?t=2019
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

He notes that modern India is fully westernized, but emphasizes the importance of Prabhupada's books in English for understanding Vedic knowledge, which is essential to counter the perception of Indian culture as mere mythology and superstition.

India is not aggressive to capture more and more geographical areas that they are controlling. That is not, India's thing is to spread spiritual culture. For the benefit of their own self, come to Sanatana Dharma. You are short-sighted about your own existence. Just because you have forgot, you, just because you have body-centered culture, doesn't mean you are not a soul. The truth is that you are spirit. And spirit-centered is Varnashma Dharma. So, the modern world is fully, modern India is fully westernized. But doesn't matter. Make best of the bad bargain. As we see here, these wonderful books of Srila Prabhupada. If Prabhupada hadn't translated these books and all, we all would not have become devotees at all. Where do we have Sanskrit? Where do we have Sanskrit? They have broken Sanskrit, no? Once Gurukul system is destroyed, Sanskrit education is broken. And all our knowledge is coming from where? Is written in what language? Sanskrit. So, the Sanskrit translation of these things, we get to appreciate, Oh God, this is not some mythology. Because moment you become educated, after some time you start seeing, because you have no explanation. What is this worship? What is this? What is this? All this ritual? What is this? You know explanation. And when you don't have an explanation, they will come and put, these are all mythologies. These are all superstitions. And they make a joke of all this. So, the Indian educated person, Vedas. Stay away. There is nothing in it. There is ritual, there is superstition.

**Verse References:** —

**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the power of chanting the Hare Krishna mantra to manifest the Lord and bring welfare, especially in Kali Yuga, highlighting the accessibility of the divine through the Krishna consciousness movement.

Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare. Before that sound is vibrating in the air, there is no avatara. Lord is unmanifest everywhere; everywhere He is there. Avatara means He is manifest in the material element. So we have to make people chant so that we manifest the Lord in our dimension. On Hare Krishna hill, in Rajaji Nagar, in this particular address, with ordinary ears, with an ordinary, material tongue and material ears, we are able to touch the avatar of the Lord. Thousands of people are coming to this temple. They are also adding; they are not only benefiting themselves by going through the chanting mantap, they are contributing to the welfare of the entire Bangalore city. How do we understand? These things you have to see from the perspective of the scriptures. This is a kripa of the Lord. This whole Krishna consciousness movement is an incarnation to give relief for people in Kali Yuga.

**Summary:** The speaker explains the composition of the Mahamantra, comprised of 16 divine sounds, and the significance of Hare, Krishna, and Rama.

So we will talk about the speciality of the divine sounds of Hari Nama. The Mahamantra is made up of 16 Divya Shabdas, divine sounds. A garland of 16 divine sounds, composed of three sounds, Hare, Krishna and Rama. They are the seeds of the Mahamantra. Hare refers to Hara. Hara is Shakti of Hari. Hari and Hara, they descend down from the spiritual world into the sound that you and I utter with our material tongue. They are the seeds of the Mahamantra.

**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

Hara is the seed of the Mahamantra. You can see the sound of the Mahamantra by the sound that is made up of the Mahamantra. Even the material, the sense of the Mahamantra, the sounds or the sound of the Mahamantra is made up of those 10 Signs. These are the three, and the names of these Four. Hara. Hara. Hara. To understand that we are doing the seva, we are offering devotional service to the Lord's Avatara Shabdha. In the Avatara Shabdha, the Avatari, one who is taking Avatara is present with all his Shaktis. Our perception towards this sound should be that of directly associating with Krishna in his sound vigraha. Mantra murti, the Supreme Lord is omnipotent. He can come into this material world in the physical form like 5,000 years back, or He can come online in the sound that you and me chant because he is omnipotent.

### Krishna, holy name, faith

So it is revealed in the scriptures, Kali Kale Nama Rupe Krishna Avatara. So Krishna himself is present in his holy names. The more you have faith in the statement of the Shastra, the more you will feel the presence of Krishna before you. Therefore this is, this aspect of seeing the sound. But the perspective of the Shastra is called Shraddha. Because to your ears, it is, to the plain ears, it is sounding like an ordinary sound. But it is not. Krishna, it's a sound, Krishna himself has become that sound. It's a self-sounding sound.

### self-sounding sound, Avatara Sharira, Shakti Avesha Avatara
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/PRnUMevkufE?t=364
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna himself is becoming that sound and entering the sound that we chant."
**Summary:** The speaker differentiates between ordinary sounds and the self-sounding sound of Krishna's name, explaining that Krishna himself enters the sound we chant, making it the Avatara Sharira, and infusing it with all his shaktis, thus becoming Shakti Avesha Avatara.

Hear properly, self-sounding sound. Hear properly. When I make a noise here, this is not a self-sounding sound. It's a sound that is produced out of clatter of matter, one matter hitting the other matter. Or the air molecules are moving and therefore sound is produced. Yes, the sound I chant, it is produced out of the air molecules. But into that sound enters the self-sounding sound. And who is the self that is sounding that sound? In Krishna's name, Krishna himself. So Krishna himself is becoming that sound and entering the sound that we chant. It's called Avatara. Yes. The sound that I produce with my material tongue is called, is the Avatara Sharira. Into that Avatara Sharira, the Krishna's self-sounding sound, his names enter that sound. When he enters that sound, all his shaktis also enter. Therefore, the sound that we chant with our tongue becomes Shakti Avesha Avatara. Yes. All the Shaktis, infinite Shaktis, it depends on how much we are able to draw that Shakti from the Shakti Avesha Nama.

### Sat Chitta Ananda, Swaroopa, Shabda Avatara
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/PRnUMevkufE?t=476
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "With our material senses, the tongue and the ears, we can access, tangibly touch Krishna through chanting and hearing."
**Summary:** The speaker describes Shakti as Sat Shakti, Chitta Shakti, and Ananda Shakti, explaining that we are trapped in material bodies due to ignorance and forgetfulness of our Swaroopa, which is Sat Chitta Ananda, and that the Lord comes as the shaktiavesha nama shabda, shabda avatara, allowing us to access Krishna through chanting and hearing.

Shakti means Sat Shakti, Chitta Shakti, Ananda Shakti, pure spiritual Shakti. Yes. We are trapped in this material world, in these material bodies because of ignorance, forgetfulness of my Swaroopa, my… what is my real Swaroopa, what is my real substance? My substance is not this body of flesh and bones. My substance is Sat Chitta Ananda. Yes. I remove the coverings of asat achit nirananda, jadananda, unless those coverings are removed, my surupa, my real surupa, which is sacchidananda, cannot be realized. So the Lord is very kind. He comes as the shaktiavesha nama shabda, shabda avatara, so that with our material senses, the tongue and the ears, we can access, tangibly touch Krishna through chanting and hearing.

Okay? And hearing requires mind. So three senses are serving the shabda avatara, the chanting, the hearing and the mind. Without the mind, you cannot hear. Chanting you can mechanically chant. The sound becomes a omnipotent sound. That omnipotent sound is sent into our heart. Okay? And the omnipotency of that sound will burn to ashes all asat, all the lies, all the ignorance about my existence, I am this body, is a false self ego. It is not the real self ego. Chitta shakti gives us transcendental knowledge about the whole samyak jnana. Chitta shakti gives us transcendental knowledge about the whole samyak jnana. Let us look into this material world. Where am I suffering? Where am I always searching after happiness? Where is that happiness? The answers to all these questions becomes very, very clear when the chitta shakti of shakti avesha nama influences my heart. Theелен Chit viewers, this authority, the爞eder in my greatest blessing, because today, I am moving forward backwards for development. Such Gunas should have no space in my heart. Samgava, Samgapura, V авyāwal. Like this Prabhupada gives example, iron put in the fire. Because of association of the fire, the energy of the fire is transferred to the iron and it becomes red hot. Warmer, warmer, warmer, then red hot. Similarly, our heart is now just like there is a wet wood. And you put it in the fire, will it catch fire immediately? No. What happens first? The fire will evaporate the water, the fire will make the wood dry and suddenly when the wood is fully dried, the firewood catches fire. In the same way, Shakti Avesha Nama enters our ears, goes into our heart, purifies our heart first.

That purification is drying the wood. That is the reason why even though the Satchidananda Surya has entered my heart, why I am not in bliss? Because the firewood is wet. Don't worry. Something is happening. You are wet. That soul is getting dried. Dry means, a complete dry means completely free from sinful attitude, sinful tendencies, sinful reactions. Completely heart is free from that. Free from offenses. Then, after that, the very same holy name, with purified our heart, which acted as an Aushadi, will act as a tonic for my spirit soul to wake up. Right now, my Shuddha Chitta, I have a Shuddha Chitta, the spirit person. Beyond this Chitta, material Chitta and material body, there is Shuddha Chitta. The Atma has got Shuddha Chitta. And that is where, the realization that, I am eternal servant of Krishna. I belong to Krishna. I love Krishna. Krishna loves me. Everything is situated in that Chitta, Shuddha Chitta. ### Krishna consciousness, detachment, hearing

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker discusses the process of awakening Krishna Prema, emphasizing the importance of detachment from illusion and attachment to Krishna, and highlighting that the holy name must enter the heart through hearing to be effective.

We have awakened, little we have awakened, that's why we are in Krishna consciousness. But waking is a slow process, fully awakened is a different thing. So, when we are ready, then the holy name will give us everything. Total detachment, from illusion, and attachment towards, Krishna. So, coming to, practice of japa, soulful japa, for all these things to happen, having heard, what I said, how it affects, how the change happens, the most important thing, to get the full benefit of the holy name, is to ensure that the holy name goes into your heart, and the only way the holy name can go into your heart, is, through hearing, the shakti, avesha, nama. You can chant any nonsense sound, and you can hear, nothing will happen to your heart, chai, chai, chai, chai, chai, in the railway station you can see, it doesn't get self-realized. Whole days, eight hours, ten hours you will chant, chai, chai, chai, chai, chai, chai, that shabda is clatter of, what?

### Avatara Shabda, Shraddha, mindful japa
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/PRnUMevkufE?t=1076
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker contrasts ordinary sounds with the Avatara Shabda of Krishna's name, emphasizing the importance of shraddha and mindful japa, which involves being mindful of one's own mind.

Air molecules. But Krishna, that shabda, Krishna has come. The limitless Satchidananda, ocean of Satchidananda is before you. So this is avatara shabda. All others are ordinary shabdas. This also, tongue, your tongue only produces this sound, like chai. But it becomes avatara shabda. Krishna's avatara sharira. And there's no difference between Krishna and any of his sharira, once he takes up, once he decides to descend. Associating with his avatara sharira, he is associating with avatari. So it all depends upon your shraddha. You see this sound with shraddha. What your eyes cannot see, what your ears cannot see, Satchidananda you cannot see with the materialized, material senses, but it is there. The surya of Satchidananda is there. So, first thing is, mindful japa. As I said, you cannot hear if the mind is not on the sound. That means mindful japa means you have to be mindful about your mind. What is mindful japa? You have to be mindful about your mind.

### mind, attention, hearing
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/PRnUMevkufE?t=1180
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "No other tasking than hearing."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on mindful japa, explaining that it involves watching the mind, anchoring it in the sound, and locking the attention on the mantra, emphasizing the importance of feeling the thought that Krishna is before you without processing other thoughts or tasks.

Where is the mind? Usually we use our mind. When you use our mind, is one thing. Being mindful about using the mind is another thing. That means you have to watch. Mind has gone away from the holy name into thinking. Unless you are mindful, you cannot catch it and bring it back. So, mind has to be anchored. The rascal mind goes away into thinking. It has to be anchored, locked in the sound. Mantra after mantra after mantra. So, locking the attention. Where you hear the chanting without thinking anything. Thinking is different. Thought is different. Thinking means you are processing thoughts. Chant and hear and feel. Feel the thought, Krishna is before me. Don't process. Process means you have a problem. You are thinking about that, how to solve that. That is one thing. Or process means you are analyzing the philosophy or of the holy name. No analyzing. Krishna is before me. It's a thought. Krishna is Surya of Satchitananda. It's a thought. So, thoughts with feeling should be there, but not thinking. Thinking means you are tasking something else. We think in order to task something, either problem solving or this or that. So, no other tasking than hearing.

### tasking, attention, yajna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/PRnUMevkufE?t=1317
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "On each bead there are sixteen spoons of ghee."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that hearing involves knowing the sound and tasking the attention to move along with each word of the mantra, describing intra-mantra and inter-mantra tasking, and comparing it to a yajna where attention is offered like ghee.

Hearing means knowing the sound. Knowing the sound means you have to catch your mind and you have to task it. Means now Hare, Krishna, Hare, Krishna, Krishna, Hare, Hare. You have to know now which sound you are on. And you have to task. Task is a work, isn't it? You have to task your attention. You have to take your attention and task your attention to move along with each word. Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare. Initially it sounds difficult, but our attention is very fine. Once you train your mind, on each sound it will run. As you are chanting, your attention will run with each sound. So that tasking, you cannot do that if you are thinking. Because then your thinking means your attention is on processing the thoughts. So mantra after mantra, you have to do tasking. Two kinds of tasking is there. One tasking is intra-mantra tasking. That means within the mantra there are sixteen names. You have to run your attention from first name to the last name. Then there is inter-mantra tasking. What is that? After you finish one mantra, no other thinking, but next mantra starts. Uninterrupted tasking. Then only it is a yajna. Yajna means repeated offering. Just like you pour one spoon of ghee, Swaha, Swaha, Swaha, Swaha. Similarly, on each bead is one Swaha, one Swaha, another. On each bead. On each bead there are sixteen spoons of ghee. Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare, Swaha. This is the japa yajna. Tasking. You are tasking.

---
### attention, energy, relationship
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/PRnUMevkufE?t=1477
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that attention is the most valuable energy of the soul, which Krishna desires, and that attentive chanting is more pleasing than mechanical chanting, comparing it to calling a loved one with attention.

The work that you are doing, taking the spoon, taking the what, ghee, putting it inside, uttering the mantra, isn't it? Similarly, this is a task of uttering the mantra and giving the attention. There you give ghee. In your other yajna, you give ghee. Here you offer attention. Attention is the most valuable energy of the soul. So Krishna wants that. That attention shakti is the most valuable. That is why mechanically you chant Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna. You go on chanting mechanically. Not very pleasing. It will help you. But attentively you chant. Give your attention and chant. Just like you call your son, call your daughter, you are calling with attention, no? Not mechanically. So call with attention, hear with attention, cry with attention, crying the names with attention. So this is very important that japa is to be understood to be tasking, not simply shravanam and kirtanam.

**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that japa involves effort and work, and that it is a relationship where Krishna receives our seva, not because He is starving for it, but because He is pleased that the soul is being purified and coming closer to Him.

There is something else happening. Just chanting and hearing each mantra. You can say that is shravanam and kirtanam. No. There is an effort involved in offering our work. It's a work. I will not do any other work than chanting and hearing 16 into 108 malas. 108 into 16. I will not do anything else. This is a work I am going to do now. My yajna is this. And always have, when our japa stops being mechanical, when you know it is a relationship, Krishna has come down to receive my seva. Why my seva pleases Krishna? Not because he is waiting, he is starving, nobody is chanting my name, oh here one, let me go and catch that name. Krishna is pleased when you chant because now Krishna knows, my this soul is going to get purified and one more step it is, he is coming towards me. Every chanting pleases Krishna because every bead of japa will take us closer to Krishna and Krishna will never forget every extra bead of japa that you do.

So it is pleasing to Krishna because Krishna loves each of the jivas and any attempt of the jivas to go back, he is pleased for us because we are going to stop suffering in this material world due to forgetfulness. So chanting and hearing and tasking, japa means tasking, chanting, hearing, tasking. What are the two kinds of tasking? Intra mantra chanting. Sounds very... Intra mantra tasking and then inter mantra chanting. That means you have a job to do within the chanting of one mantra and you have a job to do between the mantras. What is that? Push out every other thought and go jump to the next mantra, next mantra, next mantra. Unless you are determined, unless you are ready for this yajna, you will not succeed in hearing japa, pure hearing japa.

Our mind should be immersed in hearing the japa. What is the use of any thoughts? They are all useless. Krishna is a reservoir of all thoughts. The shabda is before you. Just hear him. All realization, this shabda will speak all chit into your heart. You don't have to analyze at all. Like a dark room, light switch is put on, immediately light will come. In the same way, all knowledge will come into our heart. We should become attached to this Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare. And if we are chanting on each bead as a relationship, I am the worshipper, the worshipped is the Nama Prabhu and the process of worship is chanting, hearing and tasking and execute it properly. Each bead, Nama Prabhu is pleased. Each bead is ananda giving, one ananda giving to the Nama Prabhu. One more ananda giving to Nama Prabhu. One more ananda giving to Nama Prabhu. The goal of each bead is to please Nama Prabhu. And therefore you have to know that Nama Prabhu, the person is before you. You are a person. You are a person. God is a person. And it's a process of relationship, chanting and hearing. Because the Lord's name, Shabda. Shabda. You can do two things only with the Shabda. Chant and hear. And continue chanting and hear. This is all you can do with the Shabda. So Shabda Avatara means, we should give our attention for these three things. That's all it is. 100%. Give 100% mind, 100% ears, 100% tongue. And the yajna is successful. And yajna pleases the Lord. It's not an ordinary thing.

Then you will see, when you are chanting, Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Ram Hare Ram Ram Ram Hare Hare, background, Oh, Krishna is before me. All these thoughts will come. This is Krishna. I am worshipping Krishna. I am sevak, sevak. The whole situation, everything will come behind. So, you are not only chanting, you are, in your background, there is knowledge, there is feeling. A relationship is going on, bead after bead. You are feeling, Krishna is happy with one chanting. Krishna is happy with second chanting. You have the feeling that, I have to please Krishna on each bead. I have to cheerfully chant. God comes before me, I cannot be morose. I should be cheerful. ### Hare Krishna Maha Mantra, Sound incarnation, Veda Shabda
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TG64XRHZE5o?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Hare Krishna Maha Mantra consisting of 16 divine names, Hare, Krishna and Rama, three divine names arranged as 16 word mantra, is an incarnation of Krishna himself, Krishna's sound incarnation."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the Hare Krishna Maha Mantra as an incarnation of Krishna and the crown jewel of all Veda Shabdas.

The Hare Krishna Maha Mantra consisting of 16 divine names, Hare, Krishna and Rama, three divine names arranged as 16 word mantra, is an incarnation of Krishna himself, Krishna's sound incarnation. Because of this, it is said in the scriptures that all the Veda Shabdas, Vedas are in the form of Shabda, right? All the Veda Shabdas, of all the Veda Shabdas, this Shabda is a crown jewel. You consider them as jewels, gems. All the… it is also said that all the Upanishad gem-like verses of Upanishads worship the lotus feet of this Maha Mantra, this crown jewel of all the Veda Shabdas. That is why the scriptures also say Hare Nama, Hare Nama, Hare Nama, Hare Nama, Hare Nama, Hare Nama, Hare Nama, Hare Nama, Hare Nama, Hare Nama, Hare Nama, Hare Nama, Kalo, Nas, Deva, Nas, Deva, Nas, Deva, Gati, Ranyata.

### Mindless Chanting, Offense, Attention
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TG64XRHZE5o?t=352
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker defines mindless chanting as having the mind elsewhere while the tongue chants automatically, and states that it is an offense to God.

Mindless chanting and hearing means our mind is somewhere else. While your tongue has been trained to chant, automatically chanting goes on, but the mind is on something else. Mind is thinking of some problem solving, mind is thinking of some philosophy, mind is thinking of some emotional problems, mind is thinking of some creative things and meanwhile your tongue is going on chanting. So that chanting, mindless chanting, mindless, only tongue is involved, mindless chanting. So mindless chanting is an offence. Naturally if God comes before you, if you don't give your mind to Him, is it not an offence? It is an offence.

Because if that remembrance is there, it is Krishna before me, this Nama is Krishna Himself. Then naturally there is no question of mindlessness, your mind is on Krishna. So, we are developing this quality of worship of the sound incarnation of the Lord. Today Krishna is on this earth, doing His Leela in His sound incarnation. Because He is doing this Leela as a sound incarnation, that is how millions of people are making advancement in spiritual life, becoming devotees of Krishna and getting attached to the holy name of the Lord.

### Nama Prabhu, Satchidananda, Electrified Sound
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TG64XRHZE5o?t=536
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Similarly, when our heart touches the Satchidananda iced sound, we will get Satchidananda iced."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that touching the holy name with the tongue and hearing with devotion allows one to touch Nama Prabhu, who is Krishna Himself, and compares the holy name to an electrified wire with Satchidananda electricity.

First you touch the holy name with your material tongue, the Shakti Avesha Nama Avatara. And then that sound, when it enters our heart, when we hear with devotion, then we touch Nama Prabhu. Because we are not the body, we are seated in this heart, the soul, the consciousness, the awareness. So by hearing, we touch the holy name of the Lord, Nama Prabhu. And Nama Prabhu, being Krishna Himself, and as we said this, repeatedly, Nam Nam Kari Baudha Ninja Sarva Shakti, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu has declared that in that name that you chant with your tongue, all His unlimited powers are invested in it. So this sound is not ordinary sound. Just like an ordinary wire and electrified wire, both look same. But in one wire there is electricity, in another wire there is no electricity. So this Nama Shabdha's Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare is electrified sound. Electrified means not our normal electricity, Satchidananda electricity. The Satchidananda iced sound. When you touch electrical wire, which is live, you will get electrical shock. Similarly, when our heart touches the Satchidananda iced sound, we will get Satchidananda iced. Sat Chitta Ananda. Sat means spiritual power you will get. Chitta means pure knowledge. You will be able to experience Ananda, pure bliss, pure happiness.

**Summary:** The speaker explains that mindful Japa will cleanse the heart and lead to spiritful Japa, which then graduates into soulful Japa, where every bead represents a relationship with Krishna.

This automatically by learning how to do Mindful Japa, it will automatically graduate into Soulful Japa. Mindful Japa will cleanse our heart. For long time it will be only hearing, don't think of anything, hear, hear, hear. Automatically from the sound, the presence of Krishna will be felt when you are chanting and when you feel the presence of Krishna, then you will Spiritfully do the Japa. Enthusiastically you will do Japa. Not like a burden. Oh, I have to do 16 rounds. No. Happily you will do. Joyfully you will do. Enthusiastically you will do. That is Spiritful Japa. We have to train our mind towards this, from Mindful to Spiritful. From Spiritful, later on, it becomes Soul… it starts as Soulful Japa level one. Soulful Japa, where every bead of Japa is a relationship with the Nama who is before you. Relationship of surrender, relationship of worshipper, worshipped and worshipped. And relationship of begging for mercy from the Lord. Relationship of accepting with gratitude that Krishna alone is protecting me, Krishna alone is maintaining me. Relationship of begging forgiveness for offences. Relationship of expressing gratitude to Nama Prabhu. The relationship of Seva Apeksha, begging Krishna to give you more and more Seva. The privilege of doing more and more Seva.

### Satchidananda Nama, Mindful Japa, Analogy of Fire
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TG64XRHZE5o?t=1121
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Mindless Japa is like standing near the fire. Thousand rounds of Mindless Japa is equal to one round of Mindful Japa."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of iron and fire to illustrate the difference between mindless and mindful Japa, stating that mindless Japa is like standing near the fire, while mindful Japa is like putting the iron in the fire.

So that Satchidananda Nama has to be, when you start hearing, then it is Mindful Japa. Not hearing means Nama is before you, but there is a distance between your heart and the Nama. Just like if you hold a piece of iron outside the fire, near the fire, you will get some heat. If you put the iron in the fire, you will become red hot. So Mindless Japa is like standing near the fire. There is no comparison. Standing outside the fire and inside the fire, big difference. Similarly, thousand rounds of hundred or thousand rounds of Mindless Japa is equal to one round of Mindful Japa. Because if you keep outside, you may little more than room temperature, the iron piece will get heated. Inside it goes, it becomes red hot. Difference is not little between Mindless and Mindful Japa.

### Harinama Prabhu, Shuddhanama Shabda, Nama Avatara
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TG64XRHZE5o?t=1754
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "You should always bring, start japa, by knowing that Harinama Prabhu is complete, Satchidananda Krishna Himself, who manifests His sound form as Shuddhanama Shabda in the spiritual world."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that one should start Japa knowing that Harinama Prabhu is complete, Satchidananda Krishna Himself, who manifests as Shuddhanama Shabda in the spiritual world, and that this Shuddhanama enters the sound we chant, becoming the Nama Avatara.

It will take you about 10 minutes. Then slowly, this understanding will go into your consciousness. For instance, you should always bring, start japa, by knowing that Harinama Prabhu is complete, Satchidananda Krishna Himself, who manifests His sound form as Shuddhanama Shabda in the spiritual world. Krishna's Rupa is there in the spiritual world, Krishna's Nama is there also in the spiritual world, Satchidananda Rupa, Satchidananda Nama, and that Nama is called Shuddhanama Shabda in the spiritual world. This Shuddhanama of the spiritual world, enters the sound that you and me chant. And that is, that Shabda becomes the Nama Avatara. So, Shuddhanama enters the sound you chant and becomes the Nama Avatara, Krishna Himself. And that Nama contains all the Shaktis of Krishna.

### Shakti Avesha Nama Shabda, Mantra Murti, Mercy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TG64XRHZE5o?t=1842
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So, who kindly descends into this material world, in the chants that I utter, as the Shakti Avesha Nama Shabda, which is Shuddhanama's Mantra Murti."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Krishna descends into the material world as the Shakti Avesha Nama Shabda, which is Shuddhanama's Mantra Murti, and that this is more accessible than Archa Murti due to its merciful incarnation.

So, who kindly descends into this material world, in the chants that I utter, as the Shakti Avesha Nama Shabda, which is Shuddhanama's Mantra Murti. Just like Archa Vigraha, that you see with your eyes, is the Archa Murti of Krishna's Satchidananda Rupa. Similarly, this Mantra Murti, is the, it is the, this Shakti Avesha Nama, is the Mantra Murti of the Shuddhanama. Because we cannot chant and hear Shuddhanama, with our material tongue and material ears, therefore the Lord appears in the Mantra Murti. Because we cannot see the Lord, with our material eyes, therefore the Lord comes as Archa Murti. So, Shabda Mantra Murti, is more accessible to us than, Archa Murti. So, the very merciful incarnation. Now, this Nama Shabda, Shuddhanama Shabdas, the Shakti Avesha Nama Shabda, the Nama Avatara, is the crown jewel of all Veda Shabdas, whose toes are worshipped by the brilliant, spiritual, radiant, gem-like verses of Upanishads. The Upanishad Shabda, they all worship this, Hare Krishna Maha Mantra, as the supreme, because, the purpose of all the Upanishads, is to show us God. And here He is, Hare Krishna Maha Mantra, who has most kindly descended, through the Parampara, into the sounds that utter. We are chanting

### limitation, material world, interaction

**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Day and night we are interacting with the limited."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts our daily interactions with limited people and things to the unlimited nature of Krishna.

Japa Seva means it's our meeting with Krishna to give him Seva in this world. 24 hours we are continuously encountering, continuously interacting with different people, different situations, different things. These different people, different things, different situations, they are all characterized by one common character, one common quality, that they are all limited. In other words, day and night we are interacting with the limited. Let's say I talk to you, I am limited, you are limited, we are in a limited situation, we deal with limited things, limited people. Now get a feel of this.

The finite is included in the infinite. The infinite is not included in the finite, and infinite is not there. So, this we should always remember that when we are starting doing japa, you are relating with the unlimited. ### spiritual senses, Krishna's kindness, service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/E2I2z2tu5sM?t=473
**Verse References:** BG 9.34
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is so kind, knowing our limitation to touch him transcendentally, He takes Avatara in the sound that we chant."
**Summary:** Because our spiritual senses are dormant, Krishna appears in the sound of the holy name, allowing us to serve Him through our senses and consciousness.

But because now our spiritual senses are all sleeping, our original consciousness is also sleeping. Therefore even though he is there within this sound, it is only by faith that we have to feel his presence. We cannot feel the presence of Shuddha Nama with our tongue, with our ears now. Krishna is so kind, knowing our limitation to touch him transcendentally. He takes Avatara in the sound that we chant, which is full of all the Shaktis of the Lord, the Chittananda Shakti. And through this Shakti Avesha Nama, the Shuddha Nama, Krishna receives our service of engaging our sense tongue, engaging our mind, engaging our ears to serve him with our consciousness. Krishna says in the Bhagavad Gita, Man Mana Bhava Madbhakto. Madhyaji Maam Namaskaru, Mame Vishnu Satyam Dev, Pratijnani Piyo Simhi. The biggest secret is to always remember Krishna, Man Mana Bhava Madbhakto. Keep Krishna always in one's mind, in one's Chittam, consciousness as the greatest truth. And that truth we can easily practice by always keeping Krishna on our tongue, ears and mind. That is the biggest secret. That is the biggest secret. That is the biggest secret. That is the biggest secret. That is the biggest secret. That is the biggest secret. That is the biggest secret. That is the biggest secret. That is the biggest secret. That is the biggest secret. So Chaitanya Mahaprabhu taught us how to practice Man Mana Bhava Madbhakto.

And the output also will be limited. That is the world of karma. But the moment you start japa, you know that no, I am now next two hours I am going to interact with the all loving, limitless, supreme personality of Godhead Krishna. Our mind has to understand that. The sound is unlimited, form is unlimited, his activities are unlimited, his shaktis are unlimited. And that is what now I am touching, that is what I am connecting myself to. Unlimited intelligence, unlimited beauty, unlimited power. Whatever, there is no difference between Krishna and Krishnanama. All Krishna's wonderful qualities are also qualities of Krishnanama. In fact, Krishna himself tells Arjuna the glories of his holy name. When he comes as holy name, he is more merciful, more compassionate. Just simply by hearing, you are able to touch him. There is no difference between Krishna's sound body or physical body. He can take unlimited forms. So Shabdakrishna is Purna Krishna. Like Rupa Krishna, naturally we easily accept it is Purna Krishna. Here is Krishna. But because of our conditioning, when it comes to sound, we don't feel that, Oh, here Krishna is before me. So once you have that Shraddha, that feeling, then automatically respect will come. Automatically Preeti will come. Automatically Utsaha will come. Automatically you will chant and hear from your heart Hrith Purvaka Japa.

---
### bhavanas, devotional service, Krishna's mercy

Various bhavanas that you are all aware of. The sixteen bhavanas which we can... The beautiful relationship of the soul with the Supreme Lord. Each of these bhavanas are beautiful feelings for the Supreme Lord Krishna. We serve Him with bhajatam Preeti Purvakam. We serve Him with Preeti. We serve Him with gratitude. We serve Him with apeksha. We serve Him with recognition that I am the recipient of His mercy. I am kripa patra. I am the recipient of His protection. I am the recipient of his taking care of me, my life, bhartha patra, already not you asking him future, already I am a recipient of your kripa, I am already a recipient of your raksha, I am already recipient of your bhartha, patra means you are the recipient, you are the kripa patra, a devotee who chants 16 rounds his life is beyond his karma, everything good and bad materially measured is all gift from Krishna, everything is prasadam for Vaishnava beyond karma, so that is the kripa patra.

## Why Mechanical Japa Block Spiritual Growth | Sri Madhu Pandit Dasa | Ekadashi Soulful Japa Training
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

### Nama Avatara, Shuddha Nama, Shakti
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9jDvyYC8mUQ?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So what is before us when we chant the holy names of the Lord is Krishna Himself."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the concept of Nama Avatara, stating that the holy name of Krishna is non-different from Krishna Himself and is imbued with all His shaktis.

He descends on our tongue in the sound that we utter as the Shakti Avesha Nama, Naam Naam Kaari Baudha Nija Sarva Shakti. All shaktis of Krishna enter the sound we utter. And the sound we utter is no longer a material sound. All the shaktis of the Lord have entered. These shaktis are the effulgence of the Shuddha Nama of the spiritual world. So therefore we call this as Nama Avathara. The name of the Shuddha Nama is Nama Avathara. The name of the Supreme Lord incarnates into the names that we utter. He invests all his shaktis into the name. So what is before us when we chant the holy names of the Lord is Krishna Himself. Because Krishna and Krishna's name are non-different. Just like Krishna only becomes His name, and His name is Shuddha Nama. Just like Krishna is all effulgent, sat-Chidanananda effulgence, similarly Krishna. Similarly, Krishna's namah, Shuddhanama, is also Surya, Son of sat-Chidanananda effulgence, Chittananda effulgence. Sat Chittananda, these energies enter the sound that we utter. So before us the Shakti Avesha Nama and the Shuddha Nama. Shuddha Nama's incarnation into this world is the Shakti Avesha Nama. He is incarnating into the sound that you and me utter. So it's called Shabdha Avatara. Nama Avatara of the Shuddha Nama is before us, which we can hear with our material tongue.

---
### Satchitananda, Holy Name, Association
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9jDvyYC8mUQ?t=265
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So by receiving the causeless mercy of the Lord in the form of the Holy Names into which He invests all His Shakti, the Shuddha Nama and His effulgence are non-different from each other."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the Satchitananda nature of the holy name and how chanting attentively allows one to associate with this pure energy, burning away material coverings.

So by receiving the causeless mercy of the Lord in the form of the Holy Names into which He invests all His Shakti, Naam Naam Kaari Baudha Nija Sarva Shakti, the Shuddha Nama and His effulgence are non-different from each other. So His effulgence enters this sound, Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare. So this Satchitananda energy that is there in the names that we utter, when our attention touches or when we hear these names, then we are touching the ocean of Satchitananda. Just like Prabhupada gives the example of how when an iron piece is put in the fire, the iron by association of the fire, the fire's energy heat is passed on to the iron piece and the iron gradually becomes warmer and warmer and warmer and finally it becomes red hot fire-like by transfer of energy. Similarly, by chanting and hearing, attentively, the Shakti Avesha Rama, our consciousness also gets Satchitanandaized by flow of Sat energy, Chit energy and Ananda energy. And the covering of this present soul, our present condition, the covering is Asat, Achit and Nirananda. So the Sat, Chit and Ananda energy that is coming from the Shakti Avesha Rama, the fire of Satchitananda Rama, Shakti Avesha Rama, will burn to ashes Asat, Achit and Nirananda. Because of these three coverings, we have forgotten who we are, what is our relationship with Krishna, we have forgotten what is real Ananda because of this covering. And the Lord is so kind and merciful that He comes down to give us His Satchitananda association. Just like Sun is surrounded by the Sun, the Sun is surrounded by light and heat, so much of effulgence. Similarly, Krishna is Satchitananda Vigraha. His name is Satchitananda Vigraha and they are like the Sun from whom unlimited Satchitananda effulgence is emanating. So associating with Nama means associating with the Sat, Chit and Ananda energy, pure energies.

---
### Nama Chintamani, Mind, Hearing
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9jDvyYC8mUQ?t=459
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Only when you hear, the mind becomes pure."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the holy name as Nama Chintamani, a fulfiller of all desires, and emphasizes the importance of attentive hearing in purifying the mind and connecting with the Lord.

So Nama Chintamani, Krishna Chaitanya Rasa Vigraha. So the Nama is Chintamani, a fulfiller of all desires. Krishna Chaitanya Rasa Vigraha. This sound is a vigraha of all rasas. Krishna Chaitanya Rasa Vigraha. This sound is just oozing all pleasure, all kinds of relationship with Krishna is a reservoir. Rasa Vigraha, poornashuddha nithyamukta poornashuddha means completely pure. That means there is no Asatha Chitnirananda. Purnashuddha nithyamukta. Life is finite to all forms that matter. That is why Raara is must. Ananda Incorporated with Nama. Yoga prostmens himself to all many forms of birth and death where there is no Prana, no river, no stream of water. Ananda Hrib seat else. Ananda Hrib antena puush with this. Coming here with Matthashiva, if you are a defector or anything you should keep it away. But look at the shot because here there is no eternally transcendental, beyond the three modes of material nature, abhinatva nama naminoha. There is no difference between the nama and the one whose name it is. So the Lord through the nama avatara gives us association. And what does association mean? Association means just like iron put in the fire, there is a flow of fire from the fire to the iron, flow of heat. In the same way, there is a flow of Satchitananda provided we hear what we chant. By hearing, by hearing we need to give the mind. Only when you hear, the mind becomes pure. Mind means chittam. Mind means manas, buddhi, ahankara. Three layers. Ego, intelligence, mind. They all have to... Asat, achitta and nirananda are sitting in these three places. And when we attentively hear, this shakti avesha nama is touching all these three. When you hear, mind is required. When you chant, your ego is required. I am chanting. I am hearing. So in this way, attentive hearing brings tremendous difference to this self.

### Kali Yuga, Nama Avatara, Personal Relationship
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9jDvyYC8mUQ?t=653
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Lord has come for me."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the fortunate nature of the Nama Avatara in Kali Yuga and the importance of developing a personal relationship with the Lord through the holy name.

Just like Prabhupada gives example, if you put hand in the fire, child or no, adult or anybody, it will burn. In the same way, very fortunate that the Lord has decided in Kali Yuga to descend in His holy names. So it is... That is called nama avatara. Should take advantage of this. Avatara means Lord has become accessible to us in the form of this secret we should understand. If you understand this secret, from this you can derive everything else. You will know why you have to be attentive. You know why you have to be respectful. You know why you have to be joyfully chant. Lord Himself is before me. If that fundamental shraddha is developed, for shraddha also, these are all the wonderful analogies that the acharyas have given in order to build our shraddha. That Lord is before me when I chant and He is before each one of us personally. It is not that the Lord does not care. The Supreme Lord cares for every amsa, every one of us. So you have to understand that the Lord is very personal when He comes on our tongue. But we are impersonal. So we should also be personal. The Lord has come for me. He comes for everybody. That's a different matter. But the fact remains that if He comes for everybody, He doesn't become diluted in His personal appearance for each one of us.

### Personal Relationship, Causeless Mercy, Nama Tattva
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9jDvyYC8mUQ?t=783
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If you understand this Nama Tattva, what is actually happening, you are touching the Supreme Lord Krishna in His Nama Avatara, who is supremely pure."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts Krishna's personal relationship with each individual to that of a father with many children, and highlights the causeless mercy that allows some to serve the Holy Name.

A big man comes or a father is having ten children. His attention is divided into ten children. Is it not? Even though, they are all my children, He says. But Krishna is not like that, the Supreme Father. Though everyone has a direct relationship with Him, in a general sense, oh, they are all my... mamaya vamsa jiva loke jiva bhuta sanatana all are your amsas, my amsas. He doesn't leave it at that. He has a direct relationship with every living entity. So we have... That is the reason why He has come on our tongue. No doubt, some people get benefit of this avatara. Some people don't get the benefit of this avatara. That is due to the causeless mercy of the spiritual masters, Srila Prabhupada, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's causeless mercy that some of us get this opportunity. Everybody can get, but some of us have already got this opportunity. Where we begin to serve the Holy Name, Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare. And some are even more fortunate. If you understand this Nama Tattva, what is actually happening, who is this sound, what is this sound, what happens when you chant, you are touching the Supreme Lord Krishna in His Nama Avatara, who is supremely pure, Pavitram. So even more fortunate. But this is, this transcendental understanding of what is going on during japa, what is going on in the world, the Lord descends into the sound. It is very important to have this knowledge in the background.

### Mananam, Subconscious Mind, Truth
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9jDvyYC8mUQ?t=937
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker explains the importance of repeatedly reflecting on the Nama Tattva (Mananam) to impress the truth upon the subconscious mind and change default perceptions.

The understanding is required in the background. How does this understanding come? You have to repeatedly, you have to think about it. You have to do mananam. There has to be on these simple things, what few things that just now you heard. When you hear, everything feels good for you, yeah, I understood it. But it has to become part of your moment, you utter Hare Krishna. This perception has to come, that He is before me. So for that, why He is before me? All these things is what I explained. You should do mananam. Yeah, yeah, I can understand. I can believe. I don't qualify, but He is so all-merciful, He can come. And the shastra says He comes. So He comes, so He is there. I have shraddha, I have faith. That, many times you should think, because your conscious mind is fine, you understand that. But you have a subconscious mind, which has its own ignorance. This has to go inside there. Because normally when we hear any sound, it is ordinary sound. Now if our default perception has to change, that this is not ordinary sound, it is Krishna, you have to do mananam. Many times you have to tell your subconscious mind, again and again, this is the truth, this is the truth, this is the truth.

### Mananam, Dhyanam, Emotional Contemplation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9jDvyYC8mUQ?t=1050
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When you do dhyanam, it is emotional contemplation of the factual relationship between me and Nama Tattva, Nama Prabhu."
**Summary:** The speaker differentiates between Mananam (rational reflection) and Dhyanam (emotional contemplation), emphasizing the need to emotionalize one's relationship with Krishna.

Not only mananam, mananam is intellectually, you are making your belief stronger and stronger. We also have to do dhyanam. Dhyanam. Mananam is, what is truth. Dhyanam is, what is your relationship with that truth. Krishna comes for me. Yes, I belong to Krishna. Krishna is so kind. All these, when you do dhyanam, it is emotional contemplation. When you do mananam, it is the rational reflection, repeated rational reflection of the truth about the Nama Tattva. When you do dhyanam, it is emotional contemplation of the factual relationship between me and Nama Tattva, Nama Prabhu. Though we say, yes, I am eternal servant of Krishna, but it is not emotionalized. It is only philosophy. So, it is very important that, that emotional contemplation also is required. Emotional contemplation of relating with Nama Prabhu.

### Emotional Contemplation, Shravanam, Mananam
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9jDvyYC8mUQ?t=1166
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "For any relationship to become deepened, first is emotional contemplation."
**Summary:** The speaker uses analogies of marriage and a new job to illustrate the importance of emotional contemplation in deepening relationships, and connects it to Shravanam and Mananam.

Just like you see, someone is, man and woman are fixed for wedding, to be wedded. Then, they are in emotional contemplation. Imagine, living together. Relationship, nice, love, this, everything. Isn't it? Emotional contemplation. Or, you have got an appointment letter for a good job, very good job, big money, big salary. So, you are not enjoying the job, but you indulge in emotional contemplation. Oh, how nice it will be, my future, when I get this salary, when I get this, all these things. Emotional contemplation. Similarly, we should also have, for any relationship to become deepened, first is emotional contemplation. First is the truth about the relationship. That is rational reflection. That is coming from Shravanam. Shravanam, first you hear, and then, you, what you heard, you engrave in your subconscious, that is called Mananam. First you hear, Krishna has come down, Krishna is this, Krishna is that, no difference between His sound and Himself, all the Shaktis are entering, this is Shakti-Vajanama, the Lord is before me, but I cannot taste Him, I cannot experience Him, but I can experience His Shaktis, etc., etc., etc., all these things, you hear, what I said. In summary, Namatattva, you hear. Shravanam.

### Shravanam, Mananam, Dhyanam, Anushilanam, Soulful Japa
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9jDvyYC8mUQ?t=1286
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When you chant, with right belief, about Nama Prabhu, and feeling for Nama Prabhu, that becomes soulful Japa."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces Anushilanam (practice) as the next step after Shravanam, Mananam, and Dhyanam, and defines soulful Japa as chanting with the right belief and feeling for Nama Prabhu.

Now, if you don't do Mananam, it will just go away. Or a hundred times, you hear, different, different times, slowly, it deficits into your subconscious mind. So, you have to do Mananam. And then, Dhyanam. This is not during Japa. But, then there is one more step. Just like I gave example, then, when you actually get into the job, a good job you have got, then not all emotionally, emotional contemplation, with full belief and everything, you are now actually engaging with that experience. Earlier, you were contemplating that experience. Next, you are engaging with that experience. You started going for the job. You started getting the salary. You got, getting, everybody in the, you know, community is praising you. Oh, now you are this, you are that. That, that is called Anushilanam. Anushilanam means, practice. That means, you have to practice that experience. So, Shravanam, Mananam, Dhyanam, Anushilanam. Anushilanam is what we are doing now. Practicing soulful Japa. Practicing. Practicing Japa, with Shraddha, faith, that background faith. Chanting, hearing, you are doing with your foreground. Chanting, hearing, tasking, moving the Mala, increasing the number of beads, and Japa, towards the target. That is tasking. Chanting, hearing, tasking is the activity. Three are activities. Action has to be backed with feelings. Thought feelings. Bhavana. Belief and feelings. So, when you chant, with right belief, about Nama Prabhu, and feeling for Nama Prabhu, that becomes soulful Japa.

### Soulful Japa, Shravanam, Relationship Ego
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9jDvyYC8mUQ?t=1429
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Every beat will be a relationship with the Nama."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that a background consciousness grounded in Shravanam is essential for soulful Japa, and that practicing Japa develops a relationship ego with the Holy Name.

So, not that we keep our background consciousness empty. Of course, it will be empty, if you have not done Mananam, Dhyanam. Actually, if you go on repeatedly hearing automatically Mananam, Dhyanam will happen. Mananam is stage two of Shravanam. Dhyanam is stage one of them. Stage two is Dhyanam. And then you practice that stage three. So, everything comes from Shravanam. Your consciousness is grounded with Shravanam. And we hear from Guru, Sadhu and Shastra, all the narratives. The narratives from Guru, Sadhu and Shastra, is what creates our perceptions about the world, about the thing, about the people, about Nama, about Krishna, everything. So, somewhere we have to start. Then what happens? When you Anushilanam, when you practice it, then you develop a relationship ego. I am servant of a realistic relationship ego, that I am servant of Nama Prabhu. I am chanter, I am hearer, I am tasker, I am serving. So, once that relationship is there, that is what, as soon as you take your beat back, your relationship persona with the Holy Name, along with its Shraddha, Preeti, everything that you have developed, that will automatically come. So, then every beat will be a relationship with the Nama.

### Relationship, Mechanical Japa, Nama Prabhu
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9jDvyYC8mUQ?t=1558
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "50 years also you do mechanical japa, you won't be closer to the Nama than to any of your friends."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of a deepening marital relationship to illustrate the lack of progress in chanting due to mechanical japa, and emphasizes the need to develop the belief that the sound is Krishna.

See, just like I asked in one seminar, Japa seminar, how many of you are married? Many of them raised their head. How many years? They said five years, six years, three years, twenty years, everything. Then I asked them, is there a difference in your relationship between both of you from the beginning of first day of your marriage to second year, third year, fourth year, fifth year, sixth year? Isn't it becoming deeper and deeper relationship? Everybody said, yes, definitely. I never knew her, he never knew, I never knew him. But, after few months, relationship, relationship, it is deepening. Then I asked, how many years you are chanting Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare. They all said, two years, three years, five years, six years, seven years. Hare, with your wife, you are able to build relationship. But with Krishna himself, with everyday, two hours you are with. Maybe in your real life, you are not even, you are going to office, coming back, maybe you talk with him. Still you build a relationship. But here you are not building relationship. Why? So, first of all, you are not feeling he is a person. So, step, mananam, had to develop the belief that this sound is Krishna. then it will change because Krishna has said I have come down I am part of your life by the mercy of Prabhupada who has given us two hours association spend two hours every day so our realization of Nama Prabhu our realization of everything should only deepen day by day the reason why it is not happening is because we are doing mechanical japa 50 years also you do mechanical japa you won't be closer to the Nama than to any of your friends there is a realistic relationship personal relationship Lord has come for us to relate with us so therefore this practice is important.

This practice of soulful japa when we are seeing these cards on the screen during japa that is only last part anushilanam we can go on practicing increasing the quality of japa but shravanam mananam dhyanam is one time effort that we have not put be determined to understand the Nama Tattva develop the belief that who the sound is before you it's all one time investment it goes into your system then afterwards you don't have to go on doing that anushilanam practice you can go on because you are engaging with Nama Prabhu eternally you are engaging but to engage with Nama Prabhu you have to understand you have to have the background created by shravanam mananam and dhyanam of the Nama Tattva our relationship with Nama what all guru, sadhu and shastra has said about Nama that you have to become rich when our background is rich then our foreground activity becomes very rich in relationship. It increases the quality of our chanting and hearing. Just like if you are sitting in an air-conditioned room, it is a background. You are sitting and working. ### Relationship, Detachment, Mercy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9jDvyYC8mUQ?t=1922
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Everything will start coming into your heart, shining by the rain of mercy of Satchidananda."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that as one practices, a concrete relationship will be developed with Nama Avatara, who is there to guide and shine Divya Jnana into the heart, burning away false beliefs and revealing real Ananda.

Then you will see your quality of japa will change and as you practice no doubt a concrete relationship will be developed with Nama Avatara a concrete relationship he is there for you for everything he is there to guide you he is there to lead us back to home back to Godhead he is there to shine us shine into our heart powerful Divya Jnana which will burn all asat, chitta, nir-ananda beliefs that we have our heart is full of false beliefs of ananda of this material world all that has to be burnt and when you see that happening you will doubt hey, how is this happening how I am getting detached oh my god this is Nama Prabhu is doing it I am a different person now than I what I was before all my old ideas are all changing pure chitta is coming into my heart my beliefs about pleasure my beliefs about happiness my everything is changing from false beliefs to real belief what is real ananda real happiness everything will start coming into your heart shining by the rain of mercy of Satchidananda.

### Japa Cards, Anusarana, Shraddha, Vinamrita Japa, Humility
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9jDvyYC8mUQ?t=2012
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "For you to chant with humility, you have to have done lot of Shravanam, Mananam and Dhyanam, what is humility."
**Summary:** The speaker begins to explain the use of japa cards, starting with Anusarana (obedience), Shraddha (faith), and Vinamrita Japa (humility), emphasizing the need for prior Shravanam, Mananam, and Dhyanam to cultivate genuine humility.

So regarding these cards japa cards I will just explain four first put that common card sixteen put that common card sixteen where is Indrapati? one seven seven in two eight nine ten 12 three two three two one two three two three one two three two three two four two three one two three same sound. So, with that obedience, that in the background, Guru's direction, with a purpose and invoking Divya Chesta Shakti. When you hear, Prabhupada says, the Shakti for Japa comes from the Nama Himself. That is how we are all able to chant 108 rounds. Because the Shakti, the Chesta Shakti, Chesta Shakti means the doing energy is coming from Nama Himself. If you don't hear, you cannot chant 108 rounds. At least some, you are focusing that it should be heard. Because I am going on prompting now and then. And you know the whole Prabhupada himself, Prabhupada's recording itself comes and prompts you, chant and hear exactly the same sound. Therefore, you know, even every now and then some hearing is happening. That is why our, we are able to do 108 rounds. Shakti is coming from there. You don't realize that. Otherwise mind will say enough, enough, enough, enough, enough. Mind will say enough. It's because it's tiring. It becomes tiring. Shraddha Japa. Mala too is, your background environment is respectfully presencing. Shraddha Japa. Namaprabhu, before me with faith, feelings, Krishnam is before me. Krishna is before me. Krishna is before me. With that practice, second mala, you dedicate for Shraddha Japa. First mala, you dedicate for Anusarana. Means obedience to Prabhupada. On His command, I chant the way He says. So you start, chant. Second is Shraddha Japa. Third is Vinamrmudha Japa. For all human beings, Nama, Prabhu, before me with faith feelings. Krishna is before me. Krishna is before me. Krishna is before me, with that, practice. Second Mala, you dedicate for Shraddha Japa. This is Vinamrita Japa, chant with humility before Nama Prabhu. Now for you to chant with humility, you have to have done lot of Shravanam, Mananam and Dhyanam, what is humility. Otherwise it won't come out at that time. Humility is a feeling. A feeling if it has to generate, you have to have the thoughts, just like if you are angry with someone, there is a thought behind that anger. Similarly, if there is a feeling of humility, there has to be a thought behind it. And that thought behind it, philosophy behind it, you have to have hear how insignificant you are etc, etc, etc, all that thing, just see. You see the connection? So now whatever little you have got understanding of humility, you can chant today. But parallelly you can go on doing your Mananam. Shravanam, Dhyanam on all these things, your quality of humility will go on increasing. But today you can chant and hear only with the extent of humility that you have developed by Shravanam, Mananam and Dhyanam.

### Preeti Japa, Atma Jnana Japa, Transcendental Knowledge
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9jDvyYC8mUQ?t=2236
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "You have to give your soul. Because soul is what, you know, wants relationship with Krishna, not your Yantra."
**Summary:** The speaker continues explaining the japa cards, covering Preeti Japa (love and pleasure), and Atma Jnana Japa (self-knowledge), emphasizing the importance of transcendental knowledge and detachment in devotional service.

Preeti Japa, feelings of love and pleasure giving to Nama Prabhu. In one sentence it is simple. But why? Because you feel love. When you know that someone loves you only, your love comes. That means all this, your Shravanam, actually Krishna loves us all. When that becomes a realization, automatic. So when he says, I am chanting and hearing, he thinks his body is chanting and hearing. But gradually, by the touch of the holy name itself, real self-knowledge, liberation of body and the self, all these things also in the background, your quality of Japa changes. When you understand that you are different from this body and you are using this body and offering this Seva, you are the real chanter and hearer. The Yantra Japa, mechanical Japa is called Yantra Japa. You are not involved. You are involved somewhere else. And you are offering Yantra Japa. That's also so powerful, such a big Punya it is. So powerful. But you don't get Bhakti if you do Yantra Japa. You have to give your soul. Because soul is what, you know, wants relationship with Krishna, not your Yantra. So that is Atma Jnana Japa. So entire, see for Atma Jnana, background who you are, who God is, why are you here, all these Jnana, when you read Prabhupada's book Shravanam, they are all matter, matters a lot in creating the background. Devotional service has to be executed in the background of transcendental knowledge and detachment. Something is required in the background. Knowledge, transcendental knowledge and detachment from this illusion. And then execution of service.
